comment_text: Never in my life had I heard about lowering insulin way to lose weight until 2023 (Dr. Jason Fung, Dr. Ben Bikman) - not even from my dr or nurse.  It's always: calories in v calories out, must be in a calorie deficit and don't eat fat and salt.  Well long term that's not how I lost weight - it's through lowering insulin which was a game changer!!

I had RnY gastric surgery 20 years ago. I lost 200, and then gained the majority of it back. I then lost about 150 pounds doing Weight Watchers, and then gained it all back again. Then started resetting my pouch and conditioned my stomach to eat no more than the 4 ounces again, focusing on protein. And this helped me lose about 200 pounds, but I know that I was not eating adequate fat and was still not eating enough protein. And so I know that I lost considerable muscle mass as well. I still have about 25 pounds to lose, and am now doing carnivore for healing and love it. However, I am still struggling to find any information about how much I should really be eating and what I should really be focusing on with my macros in respect to my RnY surgery. If you can help me with any information on this, it would be so much appreciated. I enjoy your channel. Thank you so much.

i listen to many of your videos, and this video, watching second time, so i can retain more of what i hear. I am 68 and diabetic for 30 years. Very long time ago i lost 50 pounds, by cutting my portions. I was always addicted to sugars. Out of 50 losed, i gained 10 pounds and kept it off for 30 years. During pandemic gained another 10 and my weight was 179. i take Metformin, 2 insulins and 2 years ago got on Ozempic. No side effects and i lost 30 pounds. I exersice every day, walk 3 miles and weight exercises few times a week. I also got into intermittent fasting, i eat only 2 times a day with a very small snack and i lost another 4 pounds. Now i rearly inject any insulin, my blood pressure is normal. I am still on Ozempic, but the sideffect of not wanting to eat is gone and i can eat all i want, but i am getting used to eating small portions and only 2 times a day.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I do fast. It works for me i lost 15 lbs in a month

I have to agree. I used to feel hungry constantly. I stumbled on the low carb died, as a way to reduce my borderline type 2 diabetes and fatty liver. Ever since, even with eating only 2 meals per day, I haven't felt hungry and don't crave snacks.  I've also lost nearly 50lb, in four months 👍
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Type 2 Diabetes; Fatty Liver Disease

comment_text: I did low carb and was very successful but had my fasting insulin checked while I was still on it and it only went from 15 to 13 with a 70 lb weight loss. I was still battling insulin resistance even as my fat disappeared. Someone needs to tell me how to get my insulin in control.

@Dr Ben. What can be done for a woman suffering from type 4 stage 2 Lipodema ??? I have followed Dr Mindy's Hormone fasting regime and lost 20pounds and then it stopped, however the Lipodema does not shift. Please advice. TIA
aspect_names: Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Stroke

comment_text: I have been cutting out most carbs for 2.5 years, especially sugars. I also went to intermittent fasting 4-5 times a week. I slowly lost 20kg which is not much but then I did never weigh more than 89kg. If I eat carbs only as brown organic rice once in 2 month, dark rye sourdough or whole grain bread. I cut out potatoes and noodles. I only use butter olive oil and coconut fat. I cook all my own food from fresh organic produce eat meat mostly chicken some beef lamb and pork(organic free range). I make my own fermented vegetables. I eat enough and not too much as I feel full after a small meal. No urges as some days when busy I nearly forgot to eat. I do now and then snack a little but have no problems afterwards. I am 75 and needed to lose weight to take pressure off my hips and knees. I am trying to go down a few more kg. My waist went down from 86cm to 77cm. It is achievable.😊

Had the Gastricbypass in 2007 and it's a choice I regret some days. Yes lost over 150 lbs but the supplements I have to take just to feel normal is astronomical been Lowcarb/Keto now 7 years and has helped in controlling my insulin and issues I was having Didn't learn about digestive enzymes untill 6 years ago So I lost several teeth, iron deficiency problems,severe muscle wasting, fatigue,depression etcetcetc Not untill I started using patch supplements and vitamins with oral ones did I see a change.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

I cut out potatoes and noodles. I only use butter olive oil and coconut fat. I cook all my own food from fresh organic produce eat meat mostly chicken some beef lamb and pork(organic free range). I make my own fermented vegetables. I eat enough and not too much as I feel full after a small meal. No urges as some days when busy I nearly forgot to eat. I do now and then snack a little but have no problems afterwards. I am 75 and needed to lose weight to take pressure off my hips and knees. I am trying to go down a few more kg. My waist went down from 86cm to 77cm. It is achievable.😊

Had the Gastricbypass in 2007 and it's a choice I regret some days. Yes lost over 150 lbs but the supplements I have to take just to feel normal is astronomical been Lowcarb/Keto now 7 years and has helped in controlling my insulin and issues I was having Didn't learn about digestive enzymes untill 6 years ago So I lost several teeth, iron deficiency problems,severe muscle wasting, fatigue,depression etcetcetc Not untill I started using patch supplements and vitamins with oral ones did I see a change.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

comment_text: I did low carb and was very successful but had my fasting insulin checked while I was still on it and it only went from 15 to 13 with a 70 lb weight loss. I was still battling insulin resistance even as my fat disappeared. Someone needs to tell me how to get my insulin in control.

comment_text: Having followed Dr Bikman for sometime, his metabolic view to cure disease makes a lot of scientific sense. I’ve acted on his advice and my chronic inflammation is down and my IBS issue is under control. Amazing.

Not just more frustose by mouth, but fructose via the polyol pathway, having been converted from glucose.   I found i have a fasted blood glucose 100+ and polyol is activated 90+.  So even if you don't eat fructose, you can still have too much.  For me, I believe I need more glycine and arginine.  20 hours fasted mynblood glucose is still 100+, but my A1C is 5.1-5.3 at worst.

So interesting! I love the chemical details for metabolic pathways. I want to see the stick figures for the molecules now. I love that Allulose is being studied and the news is great. Thank you Thank you from Keto reversed Type 2 Diabetic.

I have gout symptoms at irregular intervals.  I am an 81 year old white male.  The pain can be in either my hand thumb joints or my big toe joint - crystals are effected by gravity and drop to the lowest part of the body, so I have been led to believe.  I get almost immediate relief of pain symptoms by taking 2 750 mg capsules of Cherry Concentrate.   I happen to use are NOW brand.  I know this works, as a female 75 year old friend had same symptoms - I told her about this - she called me 20 minutes after taking a single capsule because her pain was gone in that 20 minutes.  Not sure about taking on regular basis to reduce ongoing inflammation.

My uric acid levels have always been around 8. And I know it should be less than 6 & greater than around 3. My C reactive protein levels are non detectable yet my ALT & AST are in the 40s which is way too high. My platelet count is also lower than the lower range of what is called “normal”! I also read that there is a protein synthesized in the liver that allows for increased platelet production. Now my question is, are the ALT, the AST and that protein synthesized in the liver all connected in some biochemical pathways that makes sense? I use to drink a lot of fruit juice and now I only eat whole fruit because I know that the fiber is very important in inhibiting much of the fructose from getting into the bloodstream. And it feeds the “good” bacteria. And since I lowered my fructose consumption, my psoriasis patches which are asymmetrical are almost gone! My weight has dropped significantly to my correct weight and my blood pressure has normalized. I use to have systolic hypertension but my pulse has remained in the 50s per minute and my diastolic pressure has been always around 68 to 78. I will be 70 years young in a few months. Is a ketose like fructose? Or is it an aldose like glucose? I understand the stereochemistry is a bit different which is why I suspect there are no enzymes that can metabolize allulose.
aspect_names: Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Inflammatory Markers; Liver Function; Hypertension; Gout

I have noticed that I continue to have Gouty nodules in my feet and hands, but not significantly more than when I was having repeated gout attacks.  Would you recommend going off of Allopurinol.  I do minimize fructose and cruciferous vegetables.

So interesting! I love the chemical details for metabolic pathways. I want to see the stick figures for the molecules now. I love that Allulose is being studied and the news is great. Thank you Thank you from Keto reversed Type 2 Diabetic.

I have gout symptoms at irregular intervals.  I am an 81 year old white male.  The pain can be in either my hand thumb joints or my big toe joint - crystals are effected by gravity and drop to the lowest part of the body, so I have been led to believe.  I get almost immediate relief of pain symptoms by taking 2 750 mg capsules of Cherry Concentrate.   I happen to use are NOW brand.  I know this works, as a female 75 year old friend had same symptoms - I told her about this - she called me 20 minutes after taking a single capsule because her pain was gone in that 20 minutes.  Not sure about taking on regular basis to reduce ongoing inflammation.

My uric acid levels have always been around 8. And I know it should be less than 6 & greater than around 3. My C reactive protein levels are non detectable yet my ALT & AST are in the 40s which is way too high. My platelet count is also lower than the lower range of what is called “normal”! I also read that there is a protein synthesized in the liver that allows for increased platelet production. Now my question is, are the ALT, the AST and that protein synthesized in the liver all connected in some biochemical pathways that makes sense? I use to drink a lot of fruit juice and now I only eat whole fruit because I know that the fiber is very important in inhibiting much of the fructose from getting into the bloodstream. And it feeds the “good” bacteria. And since I lowered my fructose consumption, my psoriasis patches which are asymmetrical are almost gone! My weight has dropped significantly to my correct weight and my blood pressure has normalized. I use to have systolic hypertension but my pulse has remained in the 50s per minute and my diastolic pressure has been always around 68 to 78. I will be 70 years young in a few months. Is a ketose like fructose? Or is it an aldose like glucose? I understand the stereochemistry is a bit different which is why I suspect there are no enzymes that can metabolize allulose.
aspect_names: Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Inflammatory Markers; Liver Function; Hypertension; Gout

this is literally so true. i stopped eating sugar for 2 months last year cuz i figured it played huge role in my acne & inflamed skin. my food supply in a day was 80% sugary foods before trying this out. i know yikes i set myself up for teenage diabetes. sooo 1 month in the sugar-free diet & i forgot i ever liked sugar. for real. 2 months in & i noticed the biggest difference in myself. i was no longer hungry all the time. i was no longer craving food, especially junk & sweet foods. my mood improved. my skin calmed down, inflammation was completely gone & i was getting only one pimple versus a face full of pimples that i had before cutting sugar. my water weight dropped. i finally wasnt getting bloated after eating. i didnt lose any weight cuz i've been skinny but i had motivation to look after myself & my body, versus before when i didnt care ab my body i just craved that sugar 24/7 & nothing would stop me from getting it. fast forward a year later im still off sugar for the most part (i cheat once in a while when out w friends) & the benefits only increased. whenever i have even a lil bit of sugar the inflammation shows up on my skin immediately so i remember to back off. it's not hard. u get used to not having sugar, u dont crave it, u dont even want it after a while. really it's so easy to get urself out of that lifestyle it only takes a lil effort in the beginning & then u dont even think about it bc u no longer feel like u need it. ur body DOES NOT need sugar u CAN live without it.

Don’t wait, start slow, stay steady and change your life forever…

I can testify to this.  I do two meals over a 6 hour period and I have lost 57 lbs.  I'm almost never hungry.  I sometimes forget to eat my second meal.  I don't count calories, I eat pretty much what I want.  I did count my calories one day.  I had a 1020-calorie breakfast and a 700-calorie dinner and I burned about 400 calories on my daily bike ride.  That's a total of about 1300 calories for the day and I can't describe the feeling of bliss that I feel after 18 hours of fasting, like I am totally content and won't ever need to eat again.   I started fasting because I was having a lot of issues with heartburn and a sour stomach.  I didn't know about the weight loss part of it until I watched one of your videos.  By the way, the heartburn and stomach issues cleared up that first night that I didn't eat dinner and they've not come back.  I was insulin resistant before.  I'd eat 3 to 4 thousand calories a day, always hungry and craving junk.  I had a huge belly and felt terrible.  My cravings for carbs have gone.  I eat way more meat and vegetables now.  some Fruit and some bread but not like before.  My typical breakfast is huevos rancheros with bacon and greek yogurt.  my dinner is usually a small piece of grilled chicken, pork, beef, or fish and a large salad with lots of bell peppers mushrooms and other vegetables and a bowl of berries with cream.  I don't eat between meals and I drink water or unsweetened tea.    I been riding my bicycle 7 miles a day and I'm getting ready to ramp that up to 10 for a bit and then 15 to 20 as I get my fitness back after having not done much riding during the winter.  Just riding 7 miles a day has really got the weight coming off now.  I hope to be at my goal of 108 lbs. lost by June.   I started at 268 lbs. and I'll finish at 160.  I know I'll eat like this the rest of my life.  I wish I would have know this in my 20's.  Thank you for all the videos.  I've recommended this to many people but I think they can't get over the idea that they will be super hungry if they try it.    I like to ride my bike in the morning when I've been fasting since 2 pm. the day before and to my utter surprise I can do it just fine.  I don't even feel hungry at the end of my ride.  That's because my body switches to burning fat  easily now.  I never "hit the wall" anymore.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

I don't count calories, I eat pretty much what I want.  I did count my calories one day.  I had a 1020-calorie breakfast and a 700-calorie dinner and I burned about 400 calories on my daily bike ride.  That's a total of about 1300 calories for the day and I can't describe the feeling of bliss that I feel after 18 hours of fasting, like I am totally content and won't ever need to eat again.   I started fasting because I was having a lot of issues with heartburn and a sour stomach.  I didn't know about the weight loss part of it until I watched one of your videos.  By the way, the heartburn and stomach issues cleared up that first night that I didn't eat dinner and they've not come back.  I was insulin resistant before.  I'd eat 3 to 4 thousand calories a day, always hungry and craving junk.  I had a huge belly and felt terrible.  My cravings for carbs have gone.  I eat way more meat and vegetables now.  some Fruit and some bread but not like before.  My typical breakfast is huevos rancheros with bacon and greek yogurt.  my dinner is usually a small piece of grilled chicken, pork, beef, or fish and a large salad with lots of bell peppers mushrooms and other vegetables and a bowl of berries with cream.  I don't eat between meals and I drink water or unsweetened tea.    I been riding my bicycle 7 miles a day and I'm getting ready to ramp that up to 10 for a bit and then 15 to 20 as I get my fitness back after having not done much riding during the winter.  Just riding 7 miles a day has really got the weight coming off now.  I hope to be at my goal of 108 lbs. lost by June.   I started at 268 lbs. and I'll finish at 160.  I know I'll eat like this the rest of my life.  I wish I would have know this in my 20's.  Thank you for all the videos.  I've recommended this to many people but I think they can't get over the idea that they will be super hungry if they try it.    I like to ride my bike in the morning when I've been fasting since 2 pm. the day before and to my utter surprise I can do it just fine.  I don't even feel hungry at the end of my ride.  That's because my body switches to burning fat  easily now.  I never "hit the wall" anymore.

I can testify to this.  I do two meals over a 6 hour period and I have lost 57 lbs.  I'm almost never hungry.  I sometimes forget to eat my second meal.  I don't count calories, I eat pretty much what I want.  I did count my calories one day.  I had a 1020-calorie breakfast and a 700-calorie dinner and I burned about 400 calories on my daily bike ride.  That's a total of about 1300 calories for the day and I can't describe the feeling of bliss that I feel after 18 hours of fasting, like I am totally content and won't ever need to eat again.   I started fasting because I was having a lot of issues with heartburn and a sour stomach.  I didn't know about the weight loss part of it until I watched one of your videos.  By the way, the heartburn and stomach issues cleared up that first night that I didn't eat dinner and they've not come back.  I was insulin resistant before.  I'd eat 3 to 4 thousand calories a day, always hungry and craving junk.  I had a huge belly and felt terrible.  My cravings for carbs have gone.  I eat way more meat and vegetables now.  some Fruit and some bread but not like before.  My typical breakfast is huevos rancheros with bacon and greek yogurt.  my dinner is usually a small piece of grilled chicken, pork, beef, or fish and a large salad with lots of bell peppers mushrooms and other vegetables and a bowl of berries with cream.  I don't eat between meals and I drink water or unsweetened tea.    I been riding my bicycle 7 miles a day and I'm getting ready to ramp that up to 10 for a bit and then 15 to 20 as I get my fitness back after having not done much riding during the winter.  Just riding 7 miles a day has really got the weight coming off now.  I hope to be at my goal of 108 lbs. lost by June.   I started at 268 lbs. and I'll finish at 160.  I know I'll eat like this the rest of my life.  I wish I would have know this in my 20's.  Thank you for all the videos.  I've recommended this to many people but I think they can't get over the idea that they will be super hungry if they try it.    I like to ride my bike in the morning when I've been fasting since 2 pm. the day before and to my utter surprise I can do it just fine.  I don't even feel hungry at the end of my ride.  That's because my body switches to burning fat  easily now.  I never "hit the wall" anymore.

I had a 1020-calorie breakfast and a 700-calorie dinner and I burned about 400 calories on my daily bike ride.  That's a total of about 1300 calories for the day and I can't describe the feeling of bliss that I feel after 18 hours of fasting, like I am totally content and won't ever need to eat again.   I started fasting because I was having a lot of issues with heartburn and a sour stomach.  I didn't know about the weight loss part of it until I watched one of your videos.  By the way, the heartburn and stomach issues cleared up that first night that I didn't eat dinner and they've not come back.  I was insulin resistant before.  I'd eat 3 to 4 thousand calories a day, always hungry and craving junk.  I had a huge belly and felt terrible.  My cravings for carbs have gone.  I eat way more meat and vegetables now.  some Fruit and some bread but not like before.  My typical breakfast is huevos rancheros with bacon and greek yogurt.  my dinner is usually a small piece of grilled chicken, pork, beef, or fish and a large salad with lots of bell peppers mushrooms and other vegetables and a bowl of berries with cream.  I don't eat between meals and I drink water or unsweetened tea.    I been riding my bicycle 7 miles a day and I'm getting ready to ramp that up to 10 for a bit and then 15 to 20 as I get my fitness back after having not done much riding during the winter.  Just riding 7 miles a day has really got the weight coming off now.  I hope to be at my goal of 108 lbs. lost by June.   I started at 268 lbs. and I'll finish at 160.  I know I'll eat like this the rest of my life.  I wish I would have know this in my 20's.  Thank you for all the videos.  I've recommended this to many people but I think they can't get over the idea that they will be super hungry if they try it.    I like to ride my bike in the morning when I've been fasting since 2 pm. the day before and to my utter surprise I can do it just fine.  I don't even feel hungry at the end of my ride.  That's because my body switches to burning fat  easily now.  I never "hit the wall" anymore.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

Intermittent fasting is not only good for your belly fat and Insulin resistance, but it will help get rid of the malformed proteins that sticks to your brain cells and can cause debilitating diseases like dementia or Alzheimer. This is not me saying this, it from the many research that were done and have indicated that fasting can prevent soooo many different kind of diseases... Don’t wait, start slow, stay steady and change your life forever…

I cut the sugar out a couple of years ago. In my own personal experience, none of these things happened in the first two weeks. To be clear, all of these things absolutely happened, just not as quickly.   The first week was hell on earth. I couldn't focus, my memory was screwy, I got irritated much easier, I constantly felt hungry, and I was exhausted. The one thing not mentioned enough here is that our bodies are physically addicted to sugar, so when we cut it out, we go through withdraw. There's a lot of similarity with drug addicts. Our drug is sugar.   If any of you are thinking about doing this, just stick with it through the first week or two. Once your body gets through the withdraw, all of the items on this list kick in and you will feel better than you have ever felt. The weight falls off, your mind clears, your energy returns, foods you never thought would fill you up will make you full. It's 100% worth it. I dropped 50 pounds in less than a year, I started writing again, and I was just happier with my life.   Just know you have to get through that physical withdraw first. Nothing worth doing is ever easy.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I dropped 60 lbs a few years ago like it was nothing - went from 213 to 153.  Half my friends were bugging me to find out what fad diet I was on.  The other half thought I had some illness like cancer since I had dropped 60 lbs in a matter of months like it was nothing.  I mean the weight poured off me like water.  I kept telling them that all I did was cut almost all the sugar out of my diet and no one wanted to believe me.  I told them that there's so much sugar in daily stuff that they're not even aware of.  I mean, I would go shopping for diet meals back then like lean cuisine or weight watchers only to see on the ingredients that some of them had upwards of 15-20 grams of sugar!  That's not a diet meal!  It's insane how much added sugar there is in everything.  The first two weeks were the worst.  I would get these crazed hunger attacks...like I'd eat and a half hour later my stomach would growl and I'd feel like I was gonna pass out.  But I realized that the sugar was a drug and I was going through withdrawal.  What's amazing is that once I made it past the two weeks, all of a sudden, ANY sugar was too much for me.  Whereas in the past I could eat a whole candy bar, now I'd break off one square from a hershey bar and I'm fine.  Any more than that and I want to gag because it's too much sugar.  Sodas?  Same thing - I gag just putting them in my mouth.  Once you get past the sugar cravings and come out the other end the rest is easy because once you get over the sugar, you lose your taste for it because the amount they put in daily foods and drinks is so much that the smallest taste and you gag.

You will spend a few days on the crapper as your body makes the switch from using sugar to using fat as fuel, but it passes after a few days and you'll be right as rain.  Best of luck.

I did this going on 3 years ago. I'm 46. 215 pounds when I started and now I've been at 168 to 170 for a year now. No other diet changes, just cut out added sugar. I was a big sugar eater.  This video popped up in my feed a couple of months ago.  I stopped most sugar consumption and have lost 20 lbs... my joints feel better, less inflammation everywhere, and i have way less desire to actually eat or drink sweet foods... appreciate the videos.

aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Appetite & Satiety; Skin Health; Glycemic Control; Blood Pressure

comment_text: I lost 140 pounds over nine years ago and the first thing I cut was sugar.  Within weeks, I saw and felt a difference. Cutting sugar along with other changes and exercise have had a positive impact on my overall health.  I have maintained my weight loss (from 280 to 140) and feel better at 50 than I did at younger ages.  I encourage everyone to give this a go.  Choose a day and then take it one day at a time and you will be amazed by how you feel.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes

What a great, great video chockful of valuable information! Thank you so much! I cut sugar and (fast) carbs 98% out of my life 3 years ago and lost 25 pounds without any effort and felt great! I quit sugar 3 months ago and I’ve lost 20 pounds without exercising. My face is thinner, my clothes fit looser and I feel better all around.

Just by cutting fruit juice out of my diet , removing sugar from my lemon water , reducing fruits and avoiding sugar as much as possible I dropped 4 kilograms in a small amount of time . Thank you Dr Berg ! Your channel is enlightening , I am subscribed ! I've been off sugar for the most part for four months after watching Dr Berg and Dr Ekberg. I've lost 35 pounds on a low carb system (I don't want to say diet because I honestly believe this is a lifestyle for me) and have not missed anything except fries. I eat twice a day and rarely do I feel hungry and my friends say I look 10 years younger. I noticed that my mind is a lot clearer and I definitely have more energy. I had my favourite coconut bread pudding a few weeks ago on a rare cheat day (three days in four months)  and it tasted like sweeteners, leaving a horrible aftertaste. I realised there is no such thing as a "sweet tooth". It really is sugar addiction. Once I passed the two week mark, I never craved ice cream, cakes or cookies again. I have another 24 pounds to go but so far, so good.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I started eating about 25 gm of carbs per day on 4/3/22.  I have lost 50 lbs.  59 years old.  I am keto for life I feel so much better.  Thanks Dr. Berg.

I am 53 and cut out refined sugar, bread and rice 2 months ago because I was having issues with my vision and was having to urinate all of the time.  I'm in decent shape and rarely drank soda or consumed sweets in the first place, but some of the symptoms I was having seemed to match consuming too much sugar so I made a conscious effort to cut it out.  Ilive in a 27 story condo and in addition, I added walking bottom to top back to the bottom twice a day, attempting to decrease the time with each effort.  Within two weeks my blurry vision disappeared and with every passing day the time between having to urinate has gotten longer.  Now, two months into it, I've lost 22 pounds that I didnt realize was "extra", I'm 6'3" and down to 210 lbs, my vision is great, I'm urinating 2-3 times a day instead of 2-3 times per hour and to put it as politely as possible, there has been a significant improvement to the  lead in the pencil.  I have not cut out fruit, I eat oranges, pinapple and bananas as part of my regular daily diet, plenty of chicken as well as lentils, onions carrots and potatoes and drink lots of water.  A VERY simple lifestyle change that has reaped enormous rewards.  An issue that has develope dthat is not so good is that when I made the dietary changes, I started having severe pain in my feet, achilles tendons, knees, hip joints, lower back and hands.  I started with my toes and expanded from there upwards gradually.  That part sucks, but I have high hopes that with some time it will go away.

Facts!!! 💯 I wasn’t feeling well over a long period of time, went to the doctors as I was convinced I had diabetes 🤦🏽‍♀came back negative. Decided one day that I would ‘eat clean’ to see if that made a difference. It was the best thing I ever did! First I cut out sugar. After 2 weeks I felt like a new person! Then I decided to cut out all processed foods. Life changing! No more headaches and brain fog, less bodily aches and pains, more energy, clearer skin… The list goes on. You really are what you eat. I still have treat days once or twice a week but I’m telling you, cut out the crap for the most part and you’ll feel like a new woman/man ❤
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Type 2 Diabetes

I realized I had to read ingredients labels. American food practically have added sugar in everything. So it was tough to wisely choose my groceries and meal plan along with meal prep.   If I can do it, you definitely can do it.
I lost 26 lbs after watching this video. I stopped sugar for 3 weeks. I feel WAY better. Thanks Dr. Berg!

Dr. Berg, I switched from sugar to honey several years ago and I got every benefit you said. I think honey is attacked like fat and they both aren’t bad for you. I use about 2-3 lbs of raw honey every week and lost 20 lbs without trying and feel freaking amazing. I don’t wake up during the night for the bathroom unless I’m out and drink a drink with sugar in it.

God bless you Dr. Berg for sharing all the hours of work/studying you do in such understandable videos. I have learned so much from you. I few years back I got off the sugar (which took 3 days & I went through some “headaches” which I don’t normally have). When I broke the sugar free time frame (I ate sugar again). I had a “sugar headache” again. I weigh around 122 lbs (hike/run/walk). If I get off the sugar your saying I could lose 13 lbs (hubby won’t like that) but my mother was diabetic/obese, high blood pressure, pour health. I don’t wanna go there. I prayed and ask the Lord to help me lose weight. To which I’m now down 70 lbs (my weight loss journey started in 2006). My brother did keto and last 30 pounds as well. I am seeing the signs of beginning of cataracts and looking for wisdom and what to do. Thank you so much for putting up your videos. Praying 🙏🏻 for the LORDS help in dropping the sugar❣️
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I'm 23 years old and 28 Days sugar free -including all the grains and starches that turn into sugar in the body.  I've been told I'm nicer and happier. I feel better. My face is thinner. I'm not thirsty all the time - before I felt like I could never get enough water. My heart doesn't race after I eat. I'm down 14 pounds!  Thank you Dr. Berg for all of your Informative videos that really jumpstarted me into my healthy journey.
I started eating about 25 gm of carbs per day on 4/3/22.  I have lost 50 lbs.  59 years old.  I am keto for life I feel so much better.  Thanks Dr. Berg.

I am 53 and cut out refined sugar, bread and rice 2 months ago because I was having issues with my vision and was having to urinate all of the time.  I'm in decent shape and rarely drank soda or consumed sweets in the first place, but some of the symptoms I was having seemed to match consuming too much sugar so I made a conscious effort to cut it out.  Ilive in a 27 story condo and in addition, I added walking bottom to top back to the bottom twice a day, attempting to decrease the time with each effort.  Within two weeks my blurry vision disappeared and with every passing day the time between having to urinate has gotten longer.  Now, two months into it, I've lost 22 pounds that I didnt realize was "extra", I'm 6'3" and down to 210 lbs, my vision is great, I'm urinating 2-3 times a day instead of 2-3 times per hour and to put it as politely as possible, there has been a significant improvement to the  lead in the pencil.  I have not cut out fruit, I eat oranges, pinapple and bananas as part of my regular daily diet, plenty of chicken as well as lentils, onions carrots and potatoes and drink lots of water.  A VERY simple lifestyle change that has reaped enormous rewards.  An issue that has develope dthat is not so good is that when I made the dietary changes, I started having severe pain in my feet, achilles tendons, knees, hip joints, lower back and hands.  I started with my toes and expanded from there upwards gradually.  That part sucks, but I have high hopes that with some time it will go away.

Facts!!! 💯 I wasn’t feeling well over a long period of time, went to the doctors as I was convinced I had diabetes 🤦🏽‍♀came back negative. Decided one day that I would ‘eat clean’ to see if that made a difference. It was the best thing I ever did! First I cut out sugar. After 2 weeks I felt like a new person! Then I decided to cut out all processed foods. Life changing! No more headaches and brain fog, less bodily aches and pains, more energy, clearer skin… The list goes on. You really are what you eat. I still have treat days once or twice a week but I’m telling you, cut out the crap for the most part and you’ll feel like a new woman/man ❤
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I'm 23 years old and 28 Days sugar free -including all the grains and starches that turn into sugar in the body.  I've been told I'm nicer and happier. I feel better. I started eating about 25 gm of carbs per day on 4/3/22.  I have lost 50 lbs.  59 years old.  I am keto for life I feel so much better.  Thanks Dr. Berg.

I am 53 and cut out refined sugar, bread and rice 2 months ago because I was having issues with my vision and was having to urinate all of the time.  I'm in decent shape and rarely drank soda or consumed sweets in the first place, but some of the symptoms I was having seemed to match consuming too much sugar so I made a conscious effort to cut it out.  Ilive in a 27 story condo and in addition, I added walking bottom to top back to the bottom twice a day, attempting to decrease the time with each effort.  Within two weeks my blurry vision disappeared and with every passing day the time between having to urinate has gotten longer.  Now, two months into it, I've lost 22 pounds that I didnt realize was "extra", I'm 6'3" and down to 210 lbs, my vision is great, I'm urinating 2-3 times a day instead of 2-3 times per hour and to put it as politely as possible, there has been a significant improvement to the  lead in the pencil.  I have not cut out fruit, I eat oranges, pinapple and bananas as part of my regular daily diet, plenty of chicken as well as lentils, onions carrots and potatoes and drink lots of water.  A VERY simple lifestyle change that has reaped enormous rewards.  An issue that has develope dthat is not so good is that when I made the dietary changes, I started having severe pain in my feet, achilles tendons, knees, hip joints, lower back and hands.  I started with my toes and expanded from there upwards gradually.  That part sucks, but I have high hopes that with some time it will go away.

Facts!!! 💯 I wasn’t feeling well over a long period of time, went to the doctors as I was convinced I had diabetes 🤦🏽‍♀came back negative. Decided one day that I would ‘eat clean’ to see if that made a difference. It was the best thing I ever did! First I cut out sugar. After 2 weeks I felt like a new person! Then I decided to cut out all processed foods. Life changing! No more headaches and brain fog, less bodily aches and pains, more energy, clearer skin… The list goes on. You really are what you eat. I still have treat days once or twice a week but I’m telling you, cut out the crap for the most part and you’ll feel like a new woman/man ❤

I’m on my last day of 7 day fasting.. doing 162 hrs at this vary moment leaving me with 6 more hrs to go. I’m so excited that I was able to push threw I remember my 5th and 6th day were my worst I wanted to quit I was so lazy, dizzy and without strength and The so hungriest I ever been but stood my ground and I’m happy I did I lost around 15 lbs and feel great this last day.

I’ve started doing one meal a day at the end of the day after my workout and my brain fog at work during the day has completely gone. The pure focus improvement is astronomical. I drink 3+ litres of water during the day and I feel so good.

I just finished a 72hour water fast! I have been doing intermittent fasting and I’ve fasted for 36 hours before but pushing to 72 hours was a little more difficult than I thought. But it is definitely all in your mind and the mind is very powerful! Staying optimistic and researching about fasting and reading my Bible really helped me push through! They say it gets easier after 48 hours and it did! But the “feeling like crap” feeling still would come in waves. I just rode the waves as best as I could. I suppose it might be easier for someone who lives alone but I am a mother of 2 young children and a wife. So having to prepare meals for them etc made it a little bit more difficult. I’m also breastfeeding my youngest child which I tried to stop while I was fasting but I still nursed him in the morning and evenings. On the last day I wasn’t even hungry but my husband had bought cheesecake (shame on him) and it was literally talking to me from the fridge. I was thinking about pushing to 86 hours but I couldn’t resist. Of course I didn’t eat the cheesecake right away. I broke my fast with 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar diluted in water with a probiotic supplement. 30 min later I made a smoothie with half a banana, a small apple, kefir, coconut milk, coconut water, and a few blueberries, spinach, and monk fruit sweetener. Then an hour later I had chicken soup with vegetables. Then I had the cheesecake. Just a TINY piece! I felt great just very tired from digestion. Then a couple hours later I had a nice solid poop. Sorry if that’s TMI lol but it’s true. And I’ve read about people having diarrhea after breaking their fast. Now about 4 hours after all of that I’m feeling clean, detoxed, strong, and vibrant! I also did Pilates during the fast but nothing too intense. I lost 7 pounds and drank Lots of water with Celtic salt. Oh yeah, and I took a calcium, magnesium, and zinc supplement. Now I might go back to OMAD or 18:6 I’m not sure yet. That’s it. I hope this helps. Happy fasting😊
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I'm in my last day of seven-days water fast, I have never felt better my face slimmed so I look younger and my skin brightened and I feel content..

Started intermittent fasting “one meal a day” July-2019.  Lost 90 lbs over next 12 months and have kept it off as of today 3/19/21.  It Works people and best of all its free!  If I can do it as a 57 year old man anyone can!
I fasted for 72 hours lost 10 pounds felt better than I had in 10 years. Probably won’t do it again but definitely will do 24 hours no problem.

When you simply shift your focus from eating for pleasure, to eating for health and well-being, suddenly your entire attitude towards food changes.     I started with 16:8 intermittent fasting with bulletproof coffee as a crutch, now after a month I easily do a 20 or 24 hour fast every single day. With only 1 or 2 meals a day, it's easy to make every single ingredient healthy and keto; and keto is much more than a diet, I'm positive it's the way humans mostly ate in earlier evolution.    In a single month of 16:8 IF and 2 weeks of keto, I've lost 4kg of unneeded tummy fat (and water-weight), saved hours each day spent eating and cooking/cleaning, have WAYY more energy, wake up wide-awake in the morning, have amazing control over my behavior, never get day-time drowsiness (used to be all day long fatigue), I could go on and on and on.     But my favorite benefit has been the improved mental clarity, focus and elimination of depressive mood. I used to be deeply depressed (sleep 12+ hours, exhausted all day, could barely move), although i was doing much better, keto all but put the last nail in the coffin. I have been studying nutrition for hours each day with amazing focus because I've been so interested in it as my health improves by the day. It just feels amazing and it's like I have my own brain back again.   Don't let this be just another video you watch.   Give your a mission to eat within 8 hours each day, then slowly progress to healthy cuts of meat, healthy oils, and several handfuls of greens a day. Drink mostly water, green or herbal tea, and black/bulletproof coffee. There is not much else you need to eat to be healthy, it's more about getting rid of the crap in the start anyway.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I went on an intermittent fasting for 8 months only eating one meal a day. Mainly a huge salad with veggies, chicken,one hard boiled egg, cheese,avocado very little dressing and I drank one glass of pure orange juice. I lost 70 pounds, I feel like a new person, my skin was flawless, my eyes seemed to see so much clearer, I can go on with the improvements my body made that people I knew for yrs wondered why I looked so good. Fasting can be a miracle

In 2018, I was diagnosed with colon cancer. After surgery and treatment, my body felt drained and weak. A friend introduced me to fasting, and little by little I built up to longer fasts. My longest was 5 days, and it changed everything—my energy returned, my digestion improved, and even my lab results looked better. One thing that made a huge difference was breaking the fast with bone broth and lightly steamed veggies instead of heavy meals. It helped my gut heal faster. I also learned fasting boosts stem cell activity, which explains why I felt stronger month after month. Wishing anyone here facing health challenges the same renewal and hope. This video captures that journey beautifully.

this is so healing! i was mildly sick for 4 days in Feb, just had no appetite, low energy. just drank hot tea and water. after 93 hours I had bone broth and I noticed right away i had no more inflammation in my knees! up to that point I thought I would be a cripple or need surgery but it took it all away, what a blessing! Everything we need to survive and be healthy is in our body.

comment_text: Here are some real-life facts that i observed by caring for my 87 year old dad with high cholesterol(285), heart disease, and polycystic kidneys(egfr=12, stage5 kidney failure). After a long discussion with dads General Practitioner, he agreed to temporarily stop dads cholesterol meds for 3 months to try to help his kidneys. I started dad on healthy keto and mild daily exercise. Eggs for 1 meal almost every day. Either 2 eggs for breakfast, or 3 eggs for lunch, or 4 eggs for dinner and almost  always with a few slices of bacon. After 3 months, dads GP was shocked that his cholesterol was under 200. Egfr=26, stage 4 kidney failure. After 1 year, dads cholesterol was in the double digits, and his kidney function was in the upper 30's stage3 without spiking his protein, phosphorus or creatinine.  Dads GP was shocked that in 1 year, keto fixed what cholesterol meds couldn't in 30 years. Dads nephrologist was shocked that dad went from stage 5 to stage 3 kidney failure at his age. Kidney doc said," i dont know what you're doing, but keep doing it!"  During that year, dads cardiologist was able to take him off the 2 high blood pressure meds and the water pill for edema. Dads arrhythmia also improved. Also, dads cognitive function drastically improved, and he lost about 40 pounds.  In my opinion, the eggs are not the problem. The problem is the toast(carbs) and orange juice(sugar) that most people eat with eggs.  Thank you, Dr. Berg for saving and improving dads life and helping both of us to get more healthy and to break the stranglehold of big pharma. And, thank you, Dr. Berg for offering quality supplements.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Kidney Function; Cardiovascular Disease; Chronic Kidney Disease

comment_text: I recently went in for a fairly major operation and as part of the pre-op told the doctor that I used to suffer from heartburn and acid reflux at night. Six years ago, I got an A1c test above normal.  I knew I was overweight, tried various diets and NutriSystem; but could not keep the weight off.  Then I saw Dr Sarah Hallberg's TED talk and started keto.  I lost 55 lbs and brought my A1c back to normal.  I tell people, I only eat "the good stuff".

I quit drinking alcoholic beverages about 12 years ago - when my primary care physician was telling me I need to start taking a statin drug for life. Yes, it was a life changing moment - by no longer drinking alcoholic beverages, my weight reduced by 40 pounds, my blood pressure normalized, and I no longer wake up with the physical jitters, and a hang-over. I initially told my PC doctor, that I'd consider, and come back for a follow up appointment, and then went to an acupuncturist who target treated for high blood pressure for about 6 weeks. When I returned to my PC doctor, he and his staff were dumb founded in disbelief when I explained going to an acupuncturist for treatment - checking/double checking even triple checking blood pressure. comment_text: I just turned 83 and am thrilled to peices that I am so pain-free, disease-free and med-free! I'm a whole food addict and rarely ever eat packaged, bottled,  processed or canned foods. Im a bread freak so I limit myself to sourdough only... i make sourcrout every week and eat it every morning. I religiously  take vitamin D and k2 and magnesium capsules just to make sure I'm preventing future problems...

I lived in the South of France for a year and lost 80 pounds in 6 months. The food is so much better there. The regular food there is better than the organic food here in the USA. Even when I go to visit friends there, I lose an average of 6 lbs a week, without trying. When I moved back to the US, I suddenly had food allergies and felt so poorly. I learned then that our food lacks healthy germs and makes us so sick. I tend to eat as cleanly as possible.

In Sept 2019, I was diagnosed T2D with an A1C of 8.1. I was put on Jardiance because I also had severe IBS. My PCP didn’t think I could tolerate metformin. I was given dietary guidelines and instructed to cut my calorie intake to 1800. I began walking daily. I actually struggled to get enough calories while figuring out my new diet. By January, I had lost 70 pounds. My A1C was 5.8. I was taken off Jardiance. I was still struggling to get enough calories with food that wouldn’t spike my sugar and continued to lose weight, though much more slowly. At my follow up in May, my A1C was 5.4. It’s been 5.2-5.4 ever since. I’ve lost a total of 110 lbs and kept it off. I exercise every day, usually biking. We eventually backed off my checkups to 6 months and then once a year. At my checkup in June, my Doc said, “What you’re doing is working, keep it up.” I still track my calories and sugar intake and still cycle. It will be five years next month since I was diagnosed. I’m healthier now than I was in my 30s. (I’m 62.) This is my lifestyle for the rest of my life - and I’m OK with that! Oh, yeah - I’ve been off BP meds for a couple years and my IBS is infinitely better with no horrible attacks.  I would be remiss not to add that prayer was big part of this story. LOTS of people were praying for me. God has answered those prayers and kept me faithful!
aspect_names: Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes in June 2017.  Followed the standard advice which struck me as odd the first time I read it. The advice was for 25% of the food on your plate to be starchy foods and 75% non starchy foods. I saw that whenever I followed the advice my blood sugars rose sharply. I had heard of people solving their diabetes by diet and did some of my own research and found that lots of people had put their type 2 diabetes in remission by following a low carb diet. I went low carb, calory restricted, lost about 25kgs/55lbs in 3 months. My fasting blood glucose dropped from around 10 to under 5.5 and my HBA1C runs at a little ovr 5. Essentially my diabetes is well controlled with dieat and has been in remission for 6 years. My general health is excellent - resting heart rate 55bpm, cholesterol, liver, jidney fubtions all excellent. Not bad for a 67 year old.  I didn't go full keto because I find it a bit too restrictive - Carbs are still probably around 35% of my calory intake but nearly all "slow carbs". It's a rarity for me to eat bread, rice potatoes etc.  I just have one recurring question - why did the people pushing the stsndard diet of 25% starchy veg not notice the bad results it generated? They could have saved a fortune on metformin, insulin etc. just by looking at the effects of their advice with a critical eye.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I am 88 years old. I have had Diabetes for 40 years. I have lost 80 pounds. I been carnivore for three years, I feel great. It's not a fad.

Following Dr. Westman’s way of doing keto healed me enough to go from bedridden to farming. A side benefit was that I also lost 96 pounds. Most people told me, “Of course you got better, you lost weight.” But I got better BEFORE I lost the weight. And I was not able to exercise because of the inflammation in my back, hips and knees. I couldn’t even put on my own shoes and socks! I have been eating a keto diet for eight years now. I’m still a farmer, not bedridden and I can put on my own shoes and socks. I can even wash the BOTTOMS of my feet in the shower! My father died of a heart attack at 58. My mother had suffered multiple strikes and heart attacks by my age of 69 and died at 72 from complications of diabetes. Both parents were diabetic. I was pre-diabetic when I started keto. Today I have never had a heart attack or stroke and I am not diabetic nor ore-diabetic. I take no prescriptions.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: I can personally say the carnivore diet has been a game-changer for me. It reduced inflammation and eased arthritic pain, allowing me to get back to working out. It also made it easier to eliminate everything from my diet and reintroduce foods one at a time, so I could tell what worked for me and what didn’t. At 66, I’ve never felt better!
I'm 59, 3 months on keto...down 22 lbs, 213 to 192, a1c back to normal. Not going back to my carb addiction.

I am 66 year old nurse. I started keto 8 years ago after a health emergency. I couldn't believe how many health problems resolved. I lost weight and feel 100% better. 3 years ago I was at work and had crushing chest pain. I was taken to A&E where they found my troponin was raised and a Myocardial infarction was diagnosed (even thought there was thankfully no Myocardial damage) I was lying in hospital thinking that maybe I had it wrong, maybe it was saturated fat after all. However,  after an arteriogram the Consultant told me "your coronary arteries are remarkably free of plaque" such music to my ears. I was diagnosed with a MINOCA (Myocardial Infarction with no obstruction of coronary arteries) very rare! It was about this time that I was starting to question the injections 💉 that I had taken the first 2. I am convinced it was an injury. I have kept calm and carried on and my health has continued to improve. Incidentally,  I had an abdominal ultrasound as a follow up for a small abdominal problem . The Radiographer was amazed that there was so little fat in my liver whereas previously there was quite a lot!
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Liver Function; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: Been on the carnivore diet for about 1 1/2yrs.  Doctor is ALWAYS trying to push statins.  They tried 3 different types before I went carnivore and I had side effects from all of them BUT my cholesterol was down. Now I refuse to take any type of statin. I've learned my cholestoral isn't the "end all, be all" of heart attacks & strokes.  As I've said to the doctor; "I'd rather die feeling healthy & pain free that the carnivore diet gives me, rather than living & feeling miserable on drugs the doctors are pushing & eating the "standard American diet"

I'm 75. After going keto in 2020, I switched to a strict lion carnivore diet in 2022. Zero carb healed my body, mind, and my life, but I found I just needed some variety to break the monotony of eating a steak at every meal. A few months ago, I added back eggs, bacon, lamb, seafood, butter, some cheeses, and sour cream. I've also added back one or two avocados a week, plus I now make keto dishes like ribeye and lamb chili (2 medium organic tomatoes, 1 tablespoon of chili powder) and Swedish meatballs. I will occasionally have some blueberries. I have not noticed any changes in my body, nor have I gained any weight. I'm still 95% carnivore, with keto foods for variety. ** Edited ** as far as the other content creators go, most don't have enough brains to fill a thimble.

“Stupid is as stupid does”, as Forrest Gump would say.  See, at age 84, I was fed up with the inability to lose weight (175 cm/100 kg). The doctor told me that I was tested as pre-diabetic and prescribed metformin and statins. (Never took his advice.) I also had a few small skin wounds that never healed, and so on. After 12 months on keto, my pre-diabetes has gone (A1c 5.1), I lost 25 pounds, my wounds started to heal, I stopped using blood pressure pills, no more early morning sweats, no more strange pain in my stomach or knee, my skin on arms and hands started losing those old spots, no more foggy mind, after bathing no need for a daily deodorant... etc. The first day I started keto, I joined a gym. Exercising every second day with no excuses.  Now at 86, I am a new person able to lift heavy weights, walk miles, and feel great. My blood pressure is around 115/65, and my heartbeat is between 58-66. Since childhood, I have been told about a blockage in my heart. I have never been able to sprint or play soccer like the other kids. But those new gym exercises apparently opened new blood pathways, and thus, my heart now works fine. All of that was done in moderation while adjusting to the gym exercises and outdoor walking. My thanks to Dr. Berry, Dr. Cywes, Dr. Westman, and many more.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: My path to obesity started when an employer, a vegan, forbade animal products in the building. I tried vegetarian. Over the next three years I gained 50 lbs due to excess carbs. Since switching to keto/low carb/mod protein/healthy fats, and stopping all pre-diabetes meds 13 months ago, I've lost 40 lbs and my blood glucose is better than on the meds. My doc is tracking my results.

After I had lost 55 pounds on keto and maintained that loss for two years, my internist told me “enough with the keto“. I told her I would not stop eating a ketogenic diet or the weight would come back. I could see her grinding her teeth, but she said nothing. I also had weaned myself off of 100 mg per day of losartan during that period.  💪🥳🥩🥑

I’ve been eating meat, fish, eggs and raw multi-vegetable salads with berries, EVOO and balsamic vinegar dressing for 2 years 10 months. comment_text: Almost 72 years old, still doing carnivore. I have lost 65 pounds, and just did the thousand step stairs at Suncadia in Cle Elum Washington State. I am no longer pre-diabetes, (A1-C is now 5.5 vs 6.0), and fibromyalgia has quit flaring up.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: Not all strokes are nutrition related. I had a stroke because I had an undiagnosed hole in my heart and a blood clot from driving long distance that went through and got caught in the artery of percheron which they never knew I had. 30 days after having my annual check up and lab work from the VA my doctor said my results were amazing. 30 days later I was put into basically a coma. 60 days later I was totally fine no lingering results from the stroke. Had nothing to do with my diet, or lifestyle. The doctor told my wife I had better odds of winning the lottery twice the same time as this ever happening.

I would love to do an interview with you some time Dr. Westman.  I am a keto/carnivore for over 6 years.  I was completely carnivore for about 8 months and today I am mostly carnivore, but I have 5-10 percent veggies.  I lost over 200 lbs, I am no longer diabetic, many problems healed.  Last year I had a stroke.  A pretty severe experience.  My systolic shot up to 310.  They got my BP down with meds and gave me the clot buster, but I was blind and I couldn't walk afterwards.   They did an MRI and I had 4-5 previous strokes I didn't know about.   They kept wanting to put me on a Statin, but I wouldn't take it, because I know cholesterol is an Important and necessary.  And my brain needs it to form new neurogenic pathways, and statins cross the blood brain barrier.    My LDL was 121 two days after my stroke when I was still mostly blind and could get 4-5 steps in a walker, but normally it is around 105-110.  Well I went a little more keto for a while and had some salads, but still ate a LOT of meat and 3 months later, after much physical therapy, I could see, walk, and drive, and I went back to work.  I have since had all the heart tests, ultra-sounds of my heart and neck, cat-scans of nearly everything, and a CTA scan.  They showed I have NO blockages of any kind in any of my arteries.  My High BP was pulmonary (something about the right side and left side of the heart).  It is different than normal high BP, and they have started me on a medication for that.  I am 50 years old and was a severe diabetic for years.  I was on over 300 units of insulin per day and still was having A1C's in the 14's.   Today the only thing I fight with is BP.  I am going to try to loose some more weight and see if that helps it further, but really life is good.  I feel better today after 5-6 strokes than I did when 200 lbs ago when I was diabetic as heck.  I get up early and do things all day long then I eat in the evenings watch a little YouTube and go to sleep about 6-7 hours and do it all over again when the sun comes back up.  I have fun with my family.  I go on walks for miles and miles.  I have discovered I love roller coasters and we are always going on trips to places to ride them.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes; Stroke

comment_text: Been on keto for 3 months and lost 50lbs.  My blood sugar is perfect, no more arthritis.  I’m 60 and feel awesome!  Never going back!

Most of the Drs. I used to go to get really hostile when I tell them I lost weight on keto and carnivore diets. I ask them how many people they have cleaned up the patience weight and blood glucose problems, no more insulin.

61 yrs old here and diagnosed type 2 diabetic with some heart issues going on almost a year ago to this date. I was obese and decided on my own without really knowing what it was called, to only eat Whole Foods and meats with no labels as much as possible. Also no rice, potato’s, bread, fried foods. I have lost 90 pounds and almost at perfect BMI, my A1C is now 5.3 and I aced my stress test after barely passing it the last time. I think we need to think about all the nutrients we need so I try to eat vegetables and fruits as they are in season somewhat, but not always of course depending on other things. I also eat eggs, nuts, cottage cheese, which is one of the few foods that does have a label but I try to only get the healthier one. I even make some of my own condiments. But it took me a lot of trial and error to really understand what works for me that I like and others may be able to eat more things if they aren’t diabetic. But definitely stay away from all the packaged stuff except maybe coffee and tea. I just don’t do it anymore mainly because I don’t even recognize half of the ingredients on the labels and even if they state keto friendly, they still have too many carbs for my diabetes. And I eat a lot of fruit but I do it with lower sugar fruits or with protein like cottage cheese. There are a lot of good nutrients in whole fruits, not smoothies. They are now my desert.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I did the 1 month carnivore challenge.  I lost 10 pounds of weight.  Weight I was trying to get to for the longest time.  I also reversed my triglycerides which was way out of control prior.  My LDL and HDL balance was way better than before.  Pretty much everything was going in the right direction that I wanted. comment_text: I've changed to carnivore just over two years now after much research and it changed my life in extraordinary ways. Lost 30 kg so naturally and able to sustain my weight without even trying. Lost any medical issues I had and feel better than any time in my life. I am 68 years old and can't thank enough people like Dr Westman for the advice he has given to give me back the quality of life I deserve and will never go back to eating food that I see is slowly killing people.  For me now I see food as a way of healing yourself instead of taking endless medications prescribed by my MD.

My doctor keeps pushing me to get on a statin because my cholesterol is higher on carnivore.  Wants me to limit the red meat even though I've reversed diabetes and improved so much.  It is a struggle.

2 years ago, I saw a doctor for the last time after 20 years of T2D, high blood pressure obesity and finally also neuropathy. My doctor told me to take my meds (8 of them) and he prescribed me Xanax... so I feel better about my situation. I never filled the prescription and started educating myself. I started on a Keto diet and within a week my blood pressure was normal (120/80) and within 6 month I lost 50lbs and got my A1C under 6.0. But after that I somewhat stalled... I am 70 so I do never will look like Prince Charming again. Still my morning BG is 120-150 and does not go under 100 unless I fast for more than 36 hours. So a few month ago I went full carnivore, no coffee, no dairy, absolutely no plant based food, other the pepper on my steaks. As I write this, I am on hour 48 of my 7 day autophagy (water fasting). I feel great, just came back from my daily 5 mile speed walk. Keto and Carnivore is the ONE AND ONLY way to reverse metabolic illness (aka type 2 diabetes). Sure vegetarians can be healthy, providing they do not consume Trans-Fat, seed-oils, sugar and refined carbohydrates and avoid any food which contains "preservatives", because when it is toxic to bacteria... it is also toxic to you.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

Sure vegetarians can be healthy, providing they do not consume Trans-Fat, seed-oils, sugar and refined carbohydrates and avoid any food which contains "preservatives", because when it is toxic to bacteria... it is also toxic to you.

comment_text: As a 73 y.o. type-2 diabetic and 240 lbs with an A1C of 10.2 and high blood pressure. My Doctor wanted to start me on a shot and double my metformin to 1000 mg.  I agreed on the Metformin increase but refused the shot.  Two weeks later my best friend of over 50 years died of a sudden heart attack. I was stunned and shocked although we were both in our early 70s and relatively healthy, it was still a wake-up call for me to take my health more seriously.  When I committed to lose weight, I started the carnivore diet first. I remained on it for about 2 weeks but started to feel ill with diarrhea. It's referred to as the Carnivore flu and I didn't know about it at the time or that it would pass.   So, I started on the Keto diet which allowed me some low-carb veggies. I have stayed on it for 6 months now. For 2 months I ate -/+ 20 carbs. No problem. In July I met my first weight loss goal of 125 so I upped my carb limit to 50. I continued to lose weight and I never felt hungry.  Fast forward to now, and I have lost over 40 pounds and I have dipped below 195 now for the first time in over 15 years!  My latest A1C was 5.2 and some days I don't take Metformin at all and my blood pressure is well within normal readings.  Good luck to all if you're trying to take control of your eating habits. It's worth it if you want to live!
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: Started low carb keto under the care of Dr. Westman in March. Just had my blood tested today. Just 4 months, my A1C down from 6.3 to 5.6 and my Triglycerides is normal, 1st time in 14 years! Thank you, Dr. Westman!!!

I was struggling with depression and low energy on the carnivore diet, then I went hard core all in - eating only beef, butter, Redmond's salt and water and making sure I ate 3x per day and I IMMEDIATELY felt much better (within 24 hours). The depression lifted, and my energy surged. At age 59 I feel better than I have in at least 20 years.

After a type 2 diagnosis and a heart attack resulting in a heart bypass 7+ years ago I spent a year eating plant based and not a single number had been improved.  I will say that for the first 4 months after the surgery I had cleaned up my diet considerably and then I did  the plant based. Stayed on the same meds, A1C remained at 6.3 with 2 meds, still had fatty liver and ejection fraction remained at 37% which it was at that 4 month mark.  I switched to keto and after 9 months, all meds were gone, fatty liver was gone, A1C was now 5.2 with no meds and ejection fraction was 48%.  Now I'm almost 100% carnivore and have been for 5 years.  Fiber is not needed.  Still no scurvy.  this 76 year old will never go back to carbs beyond what celery or romaine have.

In 2017, I was diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes. A1c = 11.6 at the time. Dr. Westman and Dr. Jason Fung showed me the way via you tube. I'm down 65 lbs in 8 months on Carnivore. All my arthritis is gone now. Let me repeat that... ALL MY ARTHRITIS IS GONE. This way of eating has literally saved my life.

My doctor was very against keto when i told him i was going to try it, when i went in for a medical because im a truck driver with sleep apnea i have to have a medical every two years i was down 50 pounds and the heartburn was gone and sleep apnea non existant and he was shocked. His mind wasnt completely changed but was happy that it was working for me.  Im now down about 90 pounds and sitting pretty steady at 220 pounds give or take a couple pounds day to day. The only issues I've had since going keto is my blood pressure went up slightly and I've got rosacea now, my father also had rosacea at my age so it may just be hereditary.  Who really knows.  Im contemplating trying carnivore in hopes it helps with the rosacea. I figure its worth a shot.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Digestive Health; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: I had a copy of Fuhrman’s book “Eat to Live” and ate a high vegetable, low fat meat diet for many years. I maintained my weight around 165 and thought I was doing pretty well. (Edit 1/13/24: I gained in 2018 and started Keto @ 173) Then I tried Keto in January 2019. I lost 10 pounds in 2 weeks and felt satiated!  Then I continued to lose and by August had lost 40 pounds! PAINLESSLY, btw.  In January 2022, having maintained my loss, I went more fatty meat and much less veggies — I lost another 15 pounds in two months and now maintain THAT ever since.  Oh yeah, and I feel amazing and virtually never crave Carbs! I threw out my Fuhrman book…😉

Carnivore saved my life literally.  I suffered from congestive heart failure,  diabetes,  arthritis, declining cognitive functions,  acid reflux,  and several other symptoms.  Within months, I was off all medications and feeling great again.  To think 7 years ago, if I had continued to follow what the American Heart Association,  American Diabetes Association, and others that are similar, I would have been dead now for over 6 years.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Digestive Health; Type 2 Diabetes; Cardiovascular Disease; Arthritis

comment_text: I've been on the Carnivore Lifestyle since March 30th, 2023...and I feel f@cken amazing now! Diagnosed with MS for 16.5 yrs, and for the first time I finally have hope. Got off all my MS meds now, lost 30 lbs, energy better, concentration is better, skin cleared up etc. Everything is better now!
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I'm starting my eighth week of Full Carnivore. I did my research with Anthony Chaffee MD, yourself, and other doctors and made the decision to do it cold turkey. My first visit to my local GP came with warnings about the need for carbs and that there was a risk to my health.   I gave the Doc a few of the links for him to follow up on his own research. After seven weeks I have lost 17 Kilos in weight, I have a lot more energy, and when my blood work came back it had my GP scratching his head. I was Obese with Pre Diabetes, High Blood Pressure, and my cholesterol, prior to my starting this new lifestyle, was high for the first time in my 57 years. \  Now, everything is in the normal range, and I mean everything. My GP wants to wait another three months to run another range of blood tests, but, according to the current data, if it continues, as it is today, he wants to remove me from my blood pressure medication. Such an incredible change in only seven weeks, with sooo many more years ahead of me.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: That doctor must work for weight watchers that destroyed 50 years of my life— Keto-Carnivore saved my life, helped me remove all my meds. Something is wrong with that Doc

Not too sure how this is BAD ADVICE!? I have been diabetic for over 12 years and I have been on carnivore since January 8th 2024. I am no longer on insulin and I have lost over 55 pounds!! How does that seem to bad? The ADA suggestion for food would ensure I am always diabetic! Moderation, right? If we could do that we wouldn’t be talking now! When the food pyramid came out ant people went low fat the heart disease went up!! I have been so sick for so long! I will never go back!
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: 1 month on carnivore.. lost 10kg of weight... now I actually do have to balance a bit, because I do not want the loose skin, I just want to shrink over time and eventually be what I end up being. I am "bodypositive" that way.  Weightlifting and running will be involved.

I started with Adtkin's Low carb high 'Good Fat' back in the early 90's and lost 50 lbs in about 4 months and increased my muscle mass after trying every 'LOWFAT' diet around.   After getting RID of hydrogenated 'Plant Based Fats' and eliminating short chained  Carbs like sugar, breads, grains and pastas, my joint pains and sleep problems dissappeared.   Go figure...  These days, I enjoy a salad or veggies a couple a times a week and enjoy my 'Phytochemicals' from a nice glass of Red Wine and fermented krauts and kimchee.    I have found what works for me through trial and error over the years.    We are ALL unique creatures with unique microbiomes.  I personally believe we should never COMPLETLY abdicate the responsibility of our HEALTH to someone else who doesn't LIVE in our body.  Every BODY is different.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: We need more studies about keto and carnivore to battle accusation like that Since carnivore I reversed prediabetes, feel so much better, and look younger then 2 years before. Skin improved and suspect that internal tissues got younger as well. Do not think that I need salads

Since I'm on a carnivore diet, I eat less. My joint pain is gone.  I'm losing my weight slowly.  My only problem is oxalate.  I'm working at dumping them at a lower pace.  That doctor does not know what he's talking about.  I've seen people who've been carnivore more than 10 years and they are very healthy.
My husband and I started Carnivore 1-1-24.  1st week he lost 12 pounds and I lost 10.  My husband has so much energy and his skin is getting better.  Me the same.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I went animal-based at the beginning of '23. I'm at the lowest weight I've been in 30 years, my joints feel much better, and I am no longer diabetic. There's no going back!

A typical diet day for me would be a cup of coffee with butter cream, coconut oil, fasting till the night where I will have a 55 ounce ribeye steak and shrimp cocktail. If I want other food I might indulge I start the same process all over again and I am ripped my blood pressure went down and I am off my blood pressure medication.

Several years ago, Dr. Esselstyne of the  Cleveland Clinic was promoting a Vegan lifestyle:Don't eat anything with a mother or a face. It's a catchy line. I followed the lifestyle and lost 40 lbs along with better lab numbers and just generally feeling better-fewer aches and pains. I do recommend it. I don't remember his position on added sugar, grains or oils.

My son has been on a carnivore diet for almost 2 years. He lost 285 lbs. He did well , but now he is not, he used to be a strong person, he has no energy now, can’t work at a job,  not much muscle , skin sags. His BP was 157/92 , why. Last time he had blood panel Done his doctor told him to  go see a kidney specialist. He refuses to comply with anything and takes a bunch of supplements and eats one stacked soup bowl a day of meat . Also eats 4 lbs of butter a week. He developed a rash from eggs, was eating 6 a day. Looks to me like he is destroying himself. He won’t eat any holiday dinners - this is crazy. As far as I’m concerned he has an eating disorder and needs help.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure
