comment_text: This carnivore stuff is life changing. Two months exactly into it and I have reversed pre diabetes. My husband lost 12 lbs. and this was only both three weeks into the diet. Thank you both for what you do❤

Carbs gave me such serious heartburn and GERD, that Tums were at the top of my food pyramid.  Within a couple days of going low carb, all heartburn disappeared, and has not returned for the past eight months.  No more nexium.

Month into my new diet. I'd call it a ketovore diet. Watched your videos and others. Really did not do it to lose weight although I have lost 12 pounds. I am not overweight (I am 6'2 183 lbs).I did it more for the health benefits. My wife has just started a keto diet. She definitely does not need to lose weight but I suspect she will initially. Keep up the good work!
I found Bella a few months ago.  I started doing carnivore and I've lost over 20 pounds and cured my hypoglycemia.  My skin looks good and my mood has improved a lot.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I think the key, and where carnivore and "keto" people should agree, is that low carb / high fat is the healthiest diet for humans.  I prefer saturated fat from animals, but others may prefer poly or mono unsaturated fat from plants.  Either way though, we don't need a lot of carbs to survive and thrive and we do need lots of protein and fat.  It's that simple really, for me carnivore fits my needs perfect.  My story is that I am 46 years old and a former competitive powerlifter.  My old doctor, I fired her, saw that my blood work was very high for total cholesterol (only 204 lol) and immediately wanted me on statins.  Everything else was in range (I don't agree with the ranges).  I told her I wanted to try diet first, she said I needed to do Mediterranean diet.  I was reluctant as I have ditched grains a long time ago and went keto nearly a decade ago but have been slacking since I quit competing.  Anyway, after a lot of research I decided to go carnivore because it was advantageous for me to eat meat to get my protein needs (over 200g a day) and not have to eat for hours at a time.  Plus we raise chickens so I have more eggs than a family can eat every day.  Long story short, I feel awesome and have really never been stronger.  All my chronic joint pain from years of powerlifting is completely gone, I had bad skin allergies and those are all gone too.  In the past, if I so much as touched something new I would break out in hives.  I don't even seem to get sunburns anymore which is so odd to me.  I am so strong now I may actually compete again and I thought those days were long past me.  My wife even ditched Wegovy and is kind of doing ketovore now.  Every now and again I have some pineapple or fruit, but only for a treat because I want too not for health reasons.    Blood work is great if you go by the new paradigm of HDL/Triglycerides and not worry so much about total cholesterol and LDL.  I would say, anyone curious give it a try for 3 months and see what happens.  It's not restrictive like people say, I eat everything I want to eat.  I just don't want to eat plants and sugar, everything else is on the table.  Forcing me to eat plants that taste bad is more restrictive imo.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Lipid Profile

comment_text: I started carnivore 9 months ago. The improvements to health are real. No more arthritis pain, no more diabetes, periods normal, heartburn gone, hair is thicker, skin cleared up, and im off all meds.
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Skin Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Type 2 Diabetes; Arthritis

comment_text: I'm 56 and Carnivore healed my irritable bowel syndrome and I lost 30 lbs in just a few months. My psoriasis hasn't flared up in over a year. My joints and muscles feel better with no more aches and pains. I will never eat carbs again. I eat sticks of butter too. 😁
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I had an IUD before losing weight on keto & never had a period which I was told was normal. When I lost 120lbs I started having a regular period even with the IUD albeit light. I got pregnant 2 months after removing IUD & had a healthier pregnancy at 30 with my 2nd child than my first child at 15yrs old.
I am new here, and new to the Carnivore diet as of October 1. I have lost 7 pounds in the first 3 days! So far, so good. I am pleased to have found you!

I was 445. I had surgery and ate mostly protein because I couldn't eat anything else. I lost 225 pounds. I was diagnosed with PCOS and had a normal period, but I had a lot of complications from the surgery.   I gained 50 pounds over 3 years from eating so-called healthy carbs.   My allergies and asthma returned with a vengence, and I've realized that I'm very sensitive to any type of carbs and have recently returned to a stricter low-carb way of life. It is what it is. We aren't all the same, and I know this is what works for me. I appreciate your channel.

I went on carnivore and lost about 50lbs in 6 months. Brought my waist size down to what it was when I was 27 years old, though I am 59 now. I eat about 70% animal fat and 30% protein. Eggs, steak, burger, bacon, pork rinds, butter, bone broth, chicken, and smallish amounts of cheddar cheese, occasional salmon. Never feel famished; can go about 14-16 hours before my first meal in the day. Generally 1 -2 meals a day, one larger and one smaller, in a 6-8 hour time frame. Energy levels constantly level; no drop-off. I did also recently give up coffee. I drink copious amounts of water, and take magnesium supplements and some potassium to keep my electrolytes up, and omega-3 fish oil. Warning to those starting out - you will likely have diarrhea until your gut biome adjusts to the volume of fat you are eating for a 1-2 weeks, but it is not like being sick, and eventually tapers off to like she said, going #2 once or twice a week, because you digest everything. Lots of positive side effects from carnivore: never feeling hungry, weight loss, constant energy levels, skin tags and acne all disappeared, body odor greatly reduces (1/10 of what it is if you eat the standard North American diet), to the point you may not need deodorant after a month or two, no longer have flatulence, spend less money overall on food because you don't have the need to snack all the time, insulin resistance goes away, feel much better, sleep better, feel more calm and happy.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

comment_text: Almost 70 and have had digestive problems most of my life. I’ve tried vegan/vegetarian diets without relief. I switched to a carnivore diet in desperation and within a week, belly fat shrunk, bloating disappeared, and I’ve noticed more muscle tone. I’m now 6 weeks in and feeling amazing. I’m still in shock at the changes.

I did a keto diet for 7 weeks and then carnivore for 30 days. It has been 25 years since I felt as good. I do go to gym regularly but, during that period I lost 6kg could do 10 pull-ups again and lost no strength in any of my resistance exercise. I had both hips replaced in 2013 and 2014 and hadn’t run since c2000. On February this year, after c25 days into carnivore, I did 70 minutes of weights including 3 sets of 20reps with 140kg. An hour later I ran six miles through woodland and felt good. I concede it could be placebo but who cares if you feel this good. From a 62 year old professional physiologist who had a heart attack in 2007.

just had a blood lipid panel - Dr office says I have high cholesterol : Says lose weight(lost 40 lbs.) gives a list of foods (none of which i eat) Carnivore for 4 months.  39 HDL,155 LDL  112 Triglycerides

The FDA can’t be trusted. It’s an agency that is controlled by lobbyists of food manufacturers and pharmaceutical companies. I’ve been carnivore for months and I’ve lost 25 lbs, been cured of diabetes high blood pressure and cholesterol. All my labs are good now. Used to have gout. That’s gone too. Everything they put in the food is created to keep you addicted and eat more so that you end up needing the pharmaceutical companies to save you. Both get rich and we get sick
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes; Gout

comment_text: I’ve been carnivore for 2 years now, I’ve put my diabetes into remission. I started with a fasting bsl of 21.2 and now regularly I. The 4/5’s  with zero medication. I personally found if I didn’t have enough fat (butter) my glucose wouldn’t be as stable and my hair would drop
I have been doing the keto diet since mid-September. I reversed diabetes, less inflammation in my gut, lost 32lbs, and no symptoms of PCOS and fibroids.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Type 2 Diabetes; PCOS; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: I honestly never felt better when I was in keto several years ago. I lost 33lbs, had endless energy, was mentally sharp, my GI was always calm and I pooped every other day.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being

comment_text: I’m 2 weeks in lost 6 lbs I apparently have Crohn’s disease I’m going at least 6 times a day and it’s very runny. But I’m hopeful that this will work itself out.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I’m ketovoire  for 2 years now. I have lost about 135 pounds. I do struggle with constipation, but it is getting better 😅 lately. I think my body is adjusting.

I'm 3 weeks back on keto, I have no gas. Happens every time. I might get some gas after broccoli, but it's rare. It helped me figure out that the "cashew" low carb snacks were a little higher in carbs than advertised. I got rid of them, no more gas. Also, my blood pressure was ridiculously high, around 215/110 when stressed, 130/70 at rest. It's now around 150/90 when stressed and still coming down(I'm 3 weeks in) with no medication.  I'm getting foot cramps (mostly toes), but  not severe or often. Around 2008, I had dropped significant weight on keto and had severe legs cramps that resulted in a trip to the hospital. It was not the keto, it was the diet energy drinks according to the  ER doc. Heart was fine, markers were fine, but potassium levels were non existent. There was nowhere near the amount of information on ketogenics then, at least not easily accessible and most doctors would tell you it was horrible. That's changing, but I went off keto and within a year, I returned to my status of "large mammal". I have been going on and off since 1999 and this time, it's for life. I would also say this is the first time I'm pretty much "carnivore" as the diet is high fat, protein, very little greens(small serving every couple of days), butter, steaks, lamb, organ meat, and water. Coffee is hard to quit..... I feel good. My weight is coming down, I have little to no joint pain. I'm more active (to be clear, I'm not an exercise person and have no plans to start) as far as just walking around for work. I'm a technician and spend a lot of time bending, squatting, kneeling and it gets easier everyday.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: Very interesting content, as ever. I've watched a few of 'Steak and Butter Gal's' videos, and found them interesting and informative. No, I would not be chomping down slabs of butter, fat bombs, or tall glasses of olive oil with a dozen egg yolks any time soon. That said, I do eat a lot more meat than I used to.  Seven year's later, and I'm still 30kg down, on LCHF, IF along with weight training, walking and work. Honestly, it is not difficult. The best advice is to find a healthy diet and lifestyle that is both sustainable and effective at keeping you healthy.
Keto - Carnivore gave me back youth ! No more gas, snoring , pre diabetes, pollen - wasp allergy or any other issues ! Triglyeride from 175 to 72 and 103kg to 80kg (182cm ) !
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Digestive Health; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: Over 2 years carnivore.  Lost 100 pounds. No meds and lots of problems are now gone. Feel 20 years younger. ❤ I use tons less tpaper these days. Hard stool? Up your fat and water intake. Problem solved.

I eat real meat, much more real beef fat, plenty of homegrown eggs, a little fish and chicken and some real raw veges and nuts. I drink heaps of rainwater with electrolytes in it. I take a few supplements and also some iodine. All my food is liberally sprinkled with Celtic salt. I also indulge myself with an espresso in full cream milk every mid morning. I have a nice slice of cheese with plenty of butter on it for ‘sweets’ probably 4 or 5 evenings a week before bed. I’ve been doing this for 153 days and am feeling pretty good actually. I have on average 3 bowel movements a week. No smelly farts unless I eat raw cabbage. No sugar…at all. Very minimal carbs, if any. No alcohol. No cake or biscuits or bread. No cereals or grains or seed oils. Moderate daily exercise. I turn 70 in September. Have lost 8kg since I initially started Carnivore late February this year for 120 days. Definitely less inflammatory issues in my body, more movement and less pain in my fingers. My cholesterol has gone through the roof but instead of taking statins I’ve taken myself off them after 15 years on them. Taken myself off strong painkillers for my bone on bone hips. Don’t seem to need them now. Still have tinnitus. Am working on improving my gait after observing my shadow and noticing it had changed. Walking straighter now but have to practice it. Swinging my arms more helps. Life is good. I don’t really get hungry anymore but I have a good appetite and eat above foods until I’ve had enough. My energy levels are very constant throughout the day and I no longer get sleepy or feel like I’m crashing after eating. I’m fully on the fat adapted, slow burn metabolism now and it works. I’m pretty much completely over cravings except for wifey. My recommendation? Give it a go. It’s hard mentally at first because of the way we have been conditioned to eat and what we have been told about what an ‘acceptable diet’ looks like. Try for yourself low carb, moderate protein, high fat, certain vegetables/nuts omnivore ketogenic eating. For me it’s been a beneficial lifestyle change.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Lipid Profile

comment_text: I’m ketovoire  for 2 years now. I have lost about 135 pounds. I do struggle with constipation, but it is getting better 😅 lately. I think my body is adjusting.

I'm 3 weeks back on keto, I have no gas. Happens every time. I might get some gas after broccoli, but it's rare. It helped me figure out that the "cashew" low carb snacks were a little higher in carbs than advertised. I got rid of them, no more gas. Also, my blood pressure was ridiculously high, around 215/110 when stressed, 130/70 at rest. It's now around 150/90 when stressed and still coming down(I'm 3 weeks in) with no medication.  I'm getting foot cramps (mostly toes), but  not severe or often. Around 2008, I had dropped significant weight on keto and had severe legs cramps that resulted in a trip to the hospital. It was not the keto, it was the diet energy drinks according to the  ER doc. Heart was fine, markers were fine, but potassium levels were non existent. There was nowhere near the amount of information on ketogenics then, at least not easily accessible and most doctors would tell you it was horrible. That's changing, but I went off keto and within a year, I returned to my status of "large mammal". I have been going on and off since 1999 and this time, it's for life. I would also say this is the first time I'm pretty much "carnivore" as the diet is high fat, protein, very little greens(small serving every couple of days), butter, steaks, lamb, organ meat, and water. Coffee is hard to quit..... I feel good. My weight is coming down, I have little to no joint pain. I'm more active (to be clear, I'm not an exercise person and have no plans to start) as far as just walking around for work. I'm a technician and spend a lot of time bending, squatting, kneeling and it gets easier everyday.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: I have been strict carnivore for 5 years, I completely reversed my hypertension and lost 50 Kg. My blood pressure returned to normal within first 8 weeks and I flushed all my chronic medication for it. Within 8 months I lost 50 kg of fat. Zero carbs, zero fiber,zero cheats and I am in the best health of my life.

I went from keto to mostly carnivore. My digestive issues are gone. I had acid reflux everyday of my life. My energy is back. Wearing tight clothes used to make my stomach bloat to the point of excruciating pain. Now I can wear whatever clothes I want. I also lost 80 pounds.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: How great of Kent to share his story! I've been 95% carnivore for more than a year and I lost 25kg's to reach my goal weight for the first time in my adult life! I'm 61yrs old and feel great and I take no supplements or medication. I wish Kent all the best.

A zero fibre diet (carnivore) has put me into clinical remission from severe acute IBD UC after a "healthy" diet and then a careful/restricted diet including plants put me in hospital TWICE!  Carnivore saved my life while doctors literally LAUGHED IN MY FACE at the idea of it.

About 3 1/2 years ago my husband and I changed to a ketovore  diet because my husband had been diagnosed with type two diabetes. We both lost weight. I am 63 years old and totally eliminated acid reflux, lost weight, eliminated afternoon, tiredness, and have almost no gas.  My husband put his type two diabetes in remission, and got off Metformin under the guidance of his doctor. His doctor was hesitant to say he was in remission, but appointment after appointment, his test results kept coming back good finally his doctor had to declare him in remission. What started out as a diet has now and will forever be a lifestyle change for us  because we both know that we cannot go back to our previous way of eating or the diabetes will come back for him. I feel like I may have avoided type two that could have been in my future.
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I had a severe bout of pancolitis that put me in the hospital for several days two years ago. Before that, I had diabetes and psoriatic arthritis and had started a keto diet to help treat those conditions. At first, it was working great. I had already gotten entirely off of my diabetes meds, had my AC1 back to below 5.7, lost 75 pounds, and was feeling the best I had in years. Then, a year later, I added more plant-based foods, including brussels sprouts, cabbage, broccoli, and cauliflower. That's when the problems started that led to the hospital stay and the Pancolitis diagnosis. After my hospital stay, I backed off of my keto until I settled down. I'm almost entirely carnivore now, with an occasional plant-based food that's very digestible or fermented, like sauerkraut. I'm slowly returning to normal, and most of my pancolitis symptoms have gone away.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease; Autoimmune Disease; Arthritis

comment_text: That poor guy, if he had only known before he got the bag! I see so many people who accept what the doctor says, and rush them to medical interventions when there should first be diet intervention (but the doctors don’t want to know!). I went on keto in may 2023. In august 2023 my oldest son, 16 at the time went on keto voluntarily and lost 45lbs (he was overweight), then my younger skinny son went on keto, my husband cycles in and out of keto, and last week I was working a lot so we ended up eating a lot of refined carbs, after a few days the kids were BEGGING to put them back on keto! They all said they felt like rubbish, low mood, cranky etc., wow I thought that would never happen! (It was pressure but I delivered on the eggs, bacon and arugula!) and today everyone is happy again!  Never ever did I think anyone in this house would be requesting that I don’t give them refined carbs!!!! 😂 thank you for your great videos Dr. Westman! ❤🎉

I can tell you my story in reverse ! I had  always eaten 'healthily' on the Mediterranean diet'  I struggled with  my weight ,  in terrible pain everywhere. . and the itching in my skin drove me mad.  I lived on Paracetamol. So, ( I won't bore you with my journey) I went   Keto which gave me some relief : then   I went Ketovore then carnivore and OMAD (one meal a day).that was years ago, and I lost 40 kilos in few months and my weight has never varied since than.  Pain went away, skin irritations disappeared and  at 77 I felt like a 50 year old.  THEN; A couple of months ago, I  had a problem with my pension, and I have existed solely on the food bank... bless them !    They have given me   tinned food, pasta, rice, vegetables,  fruit, bread, and for a new years treat, a packet of biscuits and a bar of chocolate.  For the record, I have never been able to afford much  on carnivore..just minced beef and fish and eggs and liver which are relatively cheap and also perfectly fine. but i never skimped on butter ! .   . That  awful pain has come back. I could hardly dress myself this morning and ....I am itching again..
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: i used to eat salad and small amount of meat, and kept fat at a minimum ON DAILY BASIS, i was known as the healthy eating freak in my office. Yet, i was having constipation which led to me going through a course of hydro colon therapy. It was a great feeling having 30+ year old fecal matter removed. Then i was back to work and continued the same diet. I continued to have constipation until i found blood in my stool. In 2021, i decided to book an appointment with a colorectal cancer surgeon and underwent colonoscopy. I had many polyps removed including one that was unusually large. A biopsy was done and this large polyp was evolving and expected to turn cancerous within months and i was lucky to have discovered it at the right time. The whole colonoscopy was filmed and saved in a CD-ROM, and the doctor said, "bring your friends together and watch the video "😂. I stopped eating salad, eating meat only, no more constipation and never felt better!

I have sarcoidosis effecting all my major organs. I have suspected RA as well. I spent 2 years going from dr to dr, referral after referral, racking up 10s of thousands of dollars in dr bills and the medications they gave me only made me sicker. I hit a breaking point and just decided to stop going to doctor appointments. I figured if i die, it's meant to be. I couldn't afford it anymore, and i didn't want my wife and kids to be left with my debts. I eventuality tried keto, it helped some. Tried to stick to AIP a couple of times. It helped me be able to function but i was still in massive amounts of pain. Then i began carnivore last December after seeing some Ken Berry videos. I felt awful for about a month but my joints began feeling better. 2 to 3 months in i felt the inflammation in my lungs and around heart area breaking down. It was like a miracle. I can go to work everyday, i can breathe, my joints and muscles dont hurt 24/7. Its amazing... I've fallen off a couple of times during holidays or when traveling and it flairs me up for a couple of days. It really reminds me how amazing carnivore is and that god blessed me a way to heal myself. The only dr i see is my pcp and she is amazed with my progress. Ive also lost 50 lbs. I thank god every morning that i can go to work and provide for my family. Perhaps ill live long enough to be an old man and have grand kids. For years i accepted i would die young. Now i have some hope for the future.

I actually was being diagnosed with ulcerative colitis 2023 May, got the same advice from my doc. There’s no known cause for it, no known cure for it. You just need to start taking steroid medication and hopefully you get better/ stable.   Took the matter into my own hands, didn’t take ANY steroid medication , while doing research online.   Long story short I did a ton of supplements, OH can’t forget the organic raw cabbage juice every morning, that taste is still stuck on my head lol. Not so pleasant.   Anyhow! Finally came across  Carnivore diet , truly a game changer, you can see the difference in weeks . Weight lost without a doubt , normal blood pressure and I’m not going to say I fully recover but things are stabilize without steroids! Stools are solid and no need to run to a restroom every 10mins after meal.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: My acid reflux stopped on day 1 of Carnivore diet and cutting pop and candy. I still eat very little green veggies and spinach, with minimal dairy and raw honey. I've lost 11% of my body weight since November and i can see my six pack and have gained muscle definition without even restarting my exercising. Most importantly, reflux is gone!

Bio-individuality: there is no one-size-fits-all approach to health and nutrition. What works for one may not work for another, and verse visa. I've been carnivore for over 2-1/2 yrs & have been thriving. My autoimmune, metabolic and mental health concerns were reversed. I lost 85 lbs. My visceral fat is <0.4%. All my blood markers are optimal. I run up to 100 mi a week on only meat, water & salt. I recently qualified for the Boston Marathon. My VO2 max is superior for a 40 yo. BTW I'm 61 yo. So, carnivore works for me, but may not work for someone else. Sorry Kurt.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Autoimmune Disease

comment_text: I completely reversed my IBD with keto and was able to come off Remicade after 5 years. I’m now moving more towards carnivore. At 52 I’ve never felt better.

similar story here, i was recently diagnosed with ulcerative colitis after having a severe flare with lots of blood loss, lost 50lbs in a month i was skin and bones, every doctor/nutritionist i talked to told me to avoid red meat and saturated fats, and also told me i should be eating lots of seed oils and lots of plant foods, had i listened to them i would be without a colon at the ripe old age of 20, as plant foods and seed oils both cause me to flare. they put me on biologics which helped the symptoms enough for them to discharge me from the hospital but i was never really 100% and after a month or so of suffering in pretty bad pain, struggling to eat enough to gain any weight and still losing blood on the toilet every day i said screw the doctors im trying carnivore, witihin 2 or 3 days i noticed major improvements such as less pain, improved appetite, and less urgent bowel movements, and after about a week the symptoms were completely gone, no more blood, no more mucus, and no more pain, i stayed on the biologics for a few months to ensure my gut could heal as much as possible before going off, and i now maintain remission with diet alone, i can eat more fibres now that my gut has healed but should i ever flare again im doing strict carnivore for a few weeks before i even consider calling a doctor, eat carnivore to heal your guts and get into remission, and cut out all processed food to maintain remission, i also recommend getting food sensitivity testing as some whole foods may be trigger foods for you aswell, dont assume its safe just because its natural, i cant tolerate corn or gluten. I cannot stress enough however, the thing that fixed me was the exact opposite of what my doctors told me, had i been a bit more trusting of them my life woukd look much different, and not for the better. seeing people like this who trusted their doctors and now have a bag as a result makes me realise how lucky i am to have figured it out on my own before it was too late. Best of luck in figuring it out to anyone going thru a similar thing right now, its not fun but do everything you can to take care of yourself and you will make it!
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: My mom had ulcerative colitis 20 years ago.  She was gaslit by multiple doctors and hospitals.  They even had a psychiatrist telling her she was making up the problems in her head.    She couldn't hold down any food for months.  She lost 80 lbs and nearly all of her muscle mass.  By the time we encountered a doctor who took her seriously, she was quite literally on de'ath's door.    She had emergency surgery to remove the entire large intestine, part of the small intestine, appendix and other tissues that were destroyed by the waste leaking from her colon for months.   My mom also had untreated diabetes since i was born.  After the colitis debacle, her health only continued to deteriorate.    She had a quadruple heart bypass 2 years later.  And 2 years after that, she started dialysis for ESRD.    A low carb diet could have saved my mom.  She passed away the week i graduated college to become a mortician.  It was cruelly ironic, planning my mom's funeral while simultaneously cramming for my second board exam.   My mom was complicit in her own demise, by ignoring her diabetes and not taking her kidney health seriously once she was on dialysis.  But this complicity, in my opinion, was fabricated and encouraged by the Western medicine goons who first ignored her, and subsequently encouraged diet / lifestyle / medications that only masked numbers on paper... all the while, making her sicker and weaker, and diminishing her will to fight.   i was sick with my own issues, with morbid obesity, high blood pressure, prediabetes, hypothyroidism and NAFLD at only 28.  My son was a year old when i realized i needed to REALLY whole-a$$ this low carb thing.    I didn't do carnivore.  i started out rather lazy low carb, and tightened things up over the years with each hurdle and plateau.   I've lost 130 lbs and gotten off all my metabolic meds.  My labs are great.  I will be here for MY children, if i have anything to say about it.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Fatty Liver Disease; Chronic Kidney Disease; Thyroid Disease; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I was on the KETO diet, couldn't eat more than 5 carbs. Had alot of inflammation and several other ailments, diabetes, carpo-tunnel, headaches, knee pain, hair loss, IBS, candida, gingivitis, bloat and memory loss. Now after one year on carnivore diet , inflammation reduced the first day and now the only things left to heal are head aches wich are not as severe as they were and hair loss and once in a while I get alittle pain in my wrist from carpo-tunnel. I feel great and while I am healing my body I have also lost 45 lbs.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: Interesting. I had never heard of Crohn’s disease when I was finally diagnosed with it 30 years ago. What’s interesting is my gastroenterologist actually told me not to eat high fiber foods at that time. When I was having pain and symptoms, all the doctors who didn’t diagnose me correctly were telling me to eat more fiber because my problem was I wasn’t eating enough, which of course just caused me more issues. Crazy my gastrointestinal told me 30 years ago to stop eating fiber but now they’re telling patient to eat it!  Lucky for me I had a bowel resection about three years after I was diagnosed becuse medication wasn’t working for me and I’ve been in remission ever since. I have had no serious issue in 27 years. 🤞

One year on a carnivore Keto diet I have lost 40LBS, gained immense muscle strength (5dyas/week at the gym) & reduced my cholesterol from 260 to 215. I am 63 years old 5' 10" & my starting weight was 225LBS. Plus my morning wood has returned & from seeing how great I was looking my wife also went Keto & lost weight & revived her sex drive.

I started a carnivore diet with the support and information from my best friend. She has psoriasis and when she first moved in with me her skin was purple, she had tons of skin hanging off every part of her body and she was suffering. She had intense itching and she’d bleed from scratching. She had previously lost a lot of weight and told me she needed to get on carnivore again. I was interested and she explained everything to to me. She was in a bad state of her life that made her leave the carnivore diet for a spell and it really affected her. She moved in with me in June of 2023 and we started carnivore together a few months later. Her skin now is a normal color now and is totally clear. She looks and feels amazing! I work my job relentlessly but she cooks meals for me and it has helped tremendously! I’ve lost 40 lbs and every now and then I’ll eat some cabbage, green beans, asparagus or Brussels sprouts. I can’t eat salads anymore as I will get diarrhea from it. We eat good and we feel good. I would recommend this lifestyle to anyone wanting to become a healthy human again!
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

When I was having pain and symptoms, all the doctors who didn’t diagnose me correctly were telling me to eat more fiber because my problem was I wasn’t eating enough, which of course just caused me more issues. Crazy my gastrointestinal told me 30 years ago to stop eating fiber but now they’re telling patient to eat it!  Lucky for me I had a bowel resection about three years after I was diagnosed becuse medication wasn’t working for me and I’ve been in remission ever since. I have had no serious issue in 27 years. 🤞

One year on a carnivore Keto diet I have lost 40LBS, gained immense muscle strength (5dyas/week at the gym) & reduced my cholesterol from 260 to 215. I am 63 years old 5' 10" & my starting weight was 225LBS. Plus my morning wood has returned & from seeing how great I was looking my wife also went Keto & lost weight & revived her sex drive.

I started a carnivore diet with the support and information from my best friend. She has psoriasis and when she first moved in with me her skin was purple, she had tons of skin hanging off every part of her body and she was suffering. She had intense itching and she’d bleed from scratching. She had previously lost a lot of weight and told me she needed to get on carnivore again. I was interested and she explained everything to to me. She was in a bad state of her life that made her leave the carnivore diet for a spell and it really affected her. She moved in with me in June of 2023 and we started carnivore together a few months later. Her skin now is a normal color now and is totally clear. She looks and feels amazing! I work my job relentlessly but she cooks meals for me and it has helped tremendously! I’ve lost 40 lbs and every now and then I’ll eat some cabbage, green beans, asparagus or Brussels sprouts. I can’t eat salads anymore as I will get diarrhea from it. We eat good and we feel good. I would recommend this lifestyle to anyone wanting to become a healthy human again!
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Crazy carnivore diet healed my gut!
Hamburger literally saved me and put my crohns disease in remission. I have had an illeoanal pull through because i literally lived in grains 30 years ago. . Never been healthier since I quit grains.

I used to have UC. I started to heal myself by eating a Paleo diet, using an infrared sauna, eating lots of bone broth and a few cooked vegetables. I was off all medications within 9 months, but my poo was not fully corrected in that time. Over the next 7 years, things continued to improve, I ultimately got off of 14 prescription medications was doing well until I turned 56… at which time I just started gaining weight. So I cut out sweet potatoes, bananas, squashes and cassava. Last year I increased my protein and not only did I lose weight, my brain is even more powerful and my poo is AMAZING!!!   I still eat blueberries, a small amount of kale (in a smoothie), onions, an occasional apple and a small green leaf salad maybe 4 times/week… so it’s more of a keto diet.

I was on the KETO diet, couldn't eat more than 5 carbs. Had alot of inflammation and several other ailments, diabetes, carpo-tunnel, headaches, knee pain, hair loss, IBS, candida, gingivitis, bloat and memory loss. Now after one year on carnivore diet , inflammation reduced the first day and now the only things left to heal are head aches wich are not as severe as they were and hair loss and once in a while I get alittle pain in my wrist from carpo-tunnel. I feel great and while I am healing my body I have also lost 45 lbs.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I’m 55 and my total cholesterol has always been over 220 and my other numbers have always been fine HDL 96, Triglycerides 59, LDL 120, I asked for a calcium score since my husband was getting one and it was 0. I still occasionally eat “low carb” bread or tortillas, I’ve lost 14 lbs. and don’t need to lose any more weight. I love Dr. Westman and have learned so much from following him. Thank you 🙏🏼

My mother just aggressively told me I would die of a heart attack doing this (as she believes it will make my bad cholesterol go up and kill me), even though I lost 80 lbs doing it and feel physically better (as well as my IBS being cured). She's morbidly obese (like, very morbidly). I hate when unhealthy people try to scare you into stopping something that is clearly making you healthy. It's such an asshole move.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Cardiovascular Disease; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: Now 71 and on the keto diet for 8  years I did it to normalize high blood-pressure. I was a plasma donor and saw my blood-pressure measured every time before donating about 24 times a year and in my country it is voluntary for all blood donations. but I was rejected twice with a lower blood pressure over 93 although it usually was not that high. Then for sport diving I had to have a general checkup and  my values were all over the place. I knew that a keto diet would possibly solve this and did not get on meds as my GP suggested but  but said that I would do this Atkins diet. During an all-in vacation at the Red Sea I tried out the diet as there was plenty meats to choose from and when I got home 2 weeks later my blood-pressure was normalized. Lately I restart measuring my blood-pressure in the morning after sitting and shaving and it always is between 65 an 78. In the 2 years I'm now measuring it has never exceeded 85.   Now if something is bad for heart decease it high blood-pressure. I hope one day I can convince my GP that I am OK. I lost 13 kg but I also slowly lost all arthritis like joint pains in my shoulders, hips and knees and also no longer have to deal with nerve twitching in my feet. As A kid I had a climbing accident and also had a compacting fracture in my right femur and so on that side the leg is almost 2" shorter.  I was expecting to be the first requiring hip replacement prosthesis surgery but I now still liver in my house with stairs and have no problem climbing up and down these stairs nor at my old workplace where I regularly climb/jog 4 stairs of about 4 m or 13 " although  I do need to ketch my breath after the last one I feel no problem with my feet, knees of hips.   I do take supplements D3 10.000 IU and B12 1000 mcg My mother also needed High B12 to avoid anemic problems and I can get pretty dizzy rising after kneeling or crouching , but I just recently discovered that within days the dizziness is over.

Was on keto/carnivore eating for a year and lost 25 pounds and am taking hardly any BP meds and my pending type two diabetes is history. Im now eating almost 100 percent carnivore for a few months and lost another 20 lbs and pain level is down my allergies are almost non existent my BP is 120/78 most days and im better in so many ways than a couple years ago. Im 66 and still working and leading a very active life. Unless it's a have to case I'm never going back to carbs
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: All I can say is he should try it. Might calm him down and put some colour in his pasty cheeks. I was vegetarian for 7 years and had many health issues I put down to age. I'm a 67 year old woman, been doing animal based for 137 days. Osteoarthritis has receded, along with other health issues. I have more energy I feel amazing. I've lost 18 pounds, my clothes fit better. Eyesight has improved. A twitch under my eye has gone, I can move better, I am stronger and fitter than I've been for years. My cardio vascular health is excellent and oxygen levels are 100%. What can't speak can't lie.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Arthritis

comment_text: People often go onto any diet because they are in a state of unhealth and want to be healthy. So if their poor health decisions catch up to them it doesn't mean the new diet caught up to it. It means the past has.   Such as I have deep vein thrombosis. I got it eating a standard American diet. Since I have gone keto, lost a lot of weight and most of the symptoms that it exists have largely gone away. However, I can still tell there is blockage. It isn't completely gone. So if it gets dislodged and kills me with a heart attack or something its not because of diet. It can be any number of things. Such as when the docs did an ultrasound to confirm it, because they presumed I might have DVT, they did dislodge it some and cause the blockage to migrate in my leg. It caused massive pain and swelling for a few weeks. I felt horrible for that period of time. It would be disingenuous that my diet caused something that was literally caused by the medical technician who the doc scheduled to do the imaging.   Just as when the guy mentioned in the video having blockage that went from 10% to 90%. it is assumed that it was because of diet but it could have been anything else and the blockage was likely at the heart because it traveled from somewhere else. Its the nature of these things. Such as if my DVT dislodges it could end up at my heart and have 90% blockage. My DVT wouldn't go away either if i swapped to a Vegan diet. At least not over night. But I'm sure as hell if my DVT caused my death if I were on a Vegan diet then none of them would say it contributed to my death like they would say a keto diet would have.   Such dishonest people.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: VERY interesting program.   Two things :  I did not find your link to the Norowitz & Feldman - paper in your description?  Technical: Your mike drops out 1-2 sec. every time you switch to your own picture. Just asying.  Still very informational video.  PS. 1 year strict keto, dropped 13 kg, normalised blood pressure. 2 years less so, OMAD, feeling fine.

I quit sugar & only eat natural foods. I shop the outside edges of the grocery stores. I do eat more meat than veggies. Lost 34 pounds . The almost Keto diet just works for me. My numbers are still good. I don't understand why this guy is so angry about what others do.
I saw my doctor today after being on the carnivore diet for 3 months and he was very pleased with my bloodwork, triglycerides went from 217 to 116...

I was diagnosed with NAFLD with NASH and F3-F4 fibrosis in 2019 and went on a low carb diet, transitioning to keto and then in February of 2022 I went to almost exclusively animal sourced foods.  A CAC scan a year ago showed a score of 119, which is average for my age of 75.  A Fibroscan, also about a year ago, showed my liver to be completely healed.  I have dropped 55 pounds while adding considerable muscle.  I feel better and am stronger than I have been in decades.  My triglycerides are low and my HDL is high.  I am healthy and don't care that my LDL is considered high.  In fact, the PURE Study shows that for seniors, those with high cholesterol have lower all cause mortality than those with 'normal' cholesterol.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Lipid Profile; Fatty Liver Disease; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: Myself and my wife went keto for 3 years and felt much healthier although my wife was suffering still with IBS and Asthma so decided to try carnivore. 2 months later her IBS is almost non-existent and her asthma symptoms have pretty much completely gone and can now take full deep breaths for the first time in years with no tightness or restriction etc, hasn’t taken her pumps in weeks. Carnivore heals we’ve seen it ourselves whilst on keto eating plants were inflammatory especially for her. I would recommend keto and carnivore but carnivore has been a game changer for us. Personally I’ve always had higher than average blood pressure, tested this week at 128/82, I’m 47.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Blood Pressure; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I was a vegan. With high blood pressure. And my highest weight. A blood test showed I had high cholesterol. My doctor put me on a statin, claiming that it cuts risk of heart attacks and strokes by 50%. It made me sick! Several symptoms. What I found out was when reporting the benefits of a statin they use relative differences but when reporting side effects they use absolute. My doctor was unaware of any side effects.   I discovered carnivore and lost 20 pounds and my blood pressure dropped so much I don’t take any medications. At 67 I am healthier than in my entire life.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Blood Pressure; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: Everyone should eat the way they want. Just get educated and make your own decisions. For me, strict carnivore has been a game changer. Lost over 100 lbs & healthy now than I've been in 30+ years. I will continue with what works for me.

Funny I lost 167 pounds keto and fasting and carnivore. 3 months into carnivore after I was near my lowest weight of the last 20 years my LDL shot through the roof. My doctor did the advanced particle size test and was satisfied with the results. My next physical was 11 months into my carnivore diet and suddenly all my numbers became amazing. LDL dropped to a normal level triglycerides dropped to amazing b4 I went carnivore. My HDL was super low for years and it jumped up to within 2 points of what is thought of as optimal. I am 14 months into my carnivore now and not looking back. I have never had more perfect labs in my life

Carb creep is a danger here. I was diabetic diagnosed 21 years ago. Cutting out carbs and now carnivore. Diabetes is in remission. I had to cut out fruit especially here in tropical Malaysia. We binge on fruit! That has to stop

For me personally, as a former type 2 diabetic, I find I do best when I flatline my blood glucose levels. I can eat one pound of a fatty ribeye steak and my glucose level would rise less than 10 points one hour later and would be down to premeal level after 2 hours. I once ate 1 tablespoon of raw honey once while testing my blood sugar. My blood sugar went from 101 to 167 in 30 minutes. At 1 hour my glucose was 144. At 2 hours my blood glucose was 171. It went down some and then jumped even higher. I respect Dr Saladino, but I know I can not eat more than a few carbs per meal or my blood sugar levels go up. Tonight I ate 2 double hamburgers for dinner, both on 25 grams of carbs buns, for a total of 50 grams of carbs. I used 73/27 ground beef and I slathered mayo and organic catchup on the buns. My beginning BG was 107 and 1 hour after eating my BG was 116. I was scared my blood sugar would jump eating 50 carbs at one meal, but adding lots of fats help slow the speed of the sugar spike. I know this information is boring and uninteresting to most who read it, but for me, someone obsessed with flatlining my glucose levels, I'm impressed. But I'm not going to press my luck, I'm not going to break my body again. I plan to eat as close to zero carb as possible just like always. But I am thrilled to know that once every other week of so I can splurge and enjoy 2 big fat juicy hamburgers with real buns and still not spike my blood sugar. Everybody has different definitions of sugar spike, I consider a jump of more than 30 points a spike. Keep this in mind people, the average human body had 10 pints of blood. The amount of sugar, or glucose in the blood is about one teaspoon, 4 grams. Please understand that sugar is a toxin, too much will kill you. When you eat a high carb diet you overload the blood with sugar. Say you ate a bowl of heart healthy Quaker Instant Maple and Brown Sugar Oatmeal, before you add any butter or sugar, a 43 gram single serving packet contains 33 grams of sugar spiking carbs. Now add 13 more carbs for the 0% fat milk and that is 46 grams of pure sugar. This is 11&1/2 teaspoons of sugar.  When you eat that bowl of heart healthy oatmeal you dump 11.5 spoons of sugar in your blood. Your body, fearing it is about to die from the poison, dumps loads of insulin into the bloodstream to grab the sugar, convert it to fat as quickly as possible and store it as fat. It does this so quickly the sugar rush you felt quickly drops to low numbers because the body is crappy at calculating how much insulin you need. So to err on the side of caution the body produces more than it needs and this causes your glucose levels to drop. This causes you to become "hangry" and interferes with ghrelin regulation in your body. This forces you to continue with the repeating cycles of carb load, fat storage, and repeat. I firmly think keeping glucose levels as flat as possible works best for me because I damaged my bodys metabolism. People who have never been obese may can eat 300 carbs a day, but I know I cant.

I am a man who has a job that requires full body, intense exertion for, often, hours at a time. I have done this job for 37 years. I started the carnivore diet 7 months ago because of the pain/inflammation reduction results claimed by those on said diet. The desired results I was looking for were realized 4 days after beginning carnivore. Im 55 years old and my rigorous exertions have taken their toll over these many years. Now I am virtually pain free. Another "side affect" of my new diet is after about 3 weeks I was doing my very difficult job and suddenly noticed that I was not becoming fatigued at the rate I had before when I was a carb gobbler. I noticed my heartbeat was slower and less intense during heavy exertion and found I could continue working far beyond what I could before; about twice as long before requiring a rest period. So it does, obviously, take some time for your muscles to adapt to burning ketones instead of glucose. And that time will vary. So if you start carnivore or keto be patient, the benefits WILL come. By the way I am an Arborist. I specialize in large tree pruning and removal. Coming up on 38 years in the business. Carnivore has shaved at least 10 years off my current age. I have never experienced a method, fad, supplement or process that claims extraordinary results and actually delivers, and Ive been motivated to try many, many of them. The ONLY one that has delivered, in spades +, is the simplest, cheapest, most intelligent and authentic...Carnivore!
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Glycemic Control

comment_text: I have nothing to loose, following 90% carnivore. All my life I was a low fat/high carb diet, including 4 years of being a vegetarian. Had an ill health since I was 20. I am 65 yo F, my digestive screwed big time. On carnivore diet for nearly 5 months, lost weight and my forever heartburn is gone. I am planning to stay put on this diet. Still keeping my black teas w/ sugar... yep, as an emotional support, it's cultural and hard to quit. But sure planning on quitting eventually all sugar.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I saw Dr. Saladeno’s interview with Dr Berry where they disagreed about intake of addition carbs. Dr Berry was against it stating it would take most people out of ketosis, and get them carb addicted again. Dr Saladeno stated he surfs for 3 hours. Ok as a person who has surfed you burn a lot of calories surfing and 3 hours a day, plus other physical gym workouts, is why he can consume additional carbs. Dr Berry stressed that for most people on carnivore keto or low carb, introductions carbs is a BAD idea. Thank you Dr Westman for looking at some of these youtube videos and where some of the information is not as scientific as we wish. I do carnivore 7 months now, with added yogurt and whey protein because when i started carnivore i lost too much weight, and needed to gain weight back. I eat about 5-6 eggs daily with a 26oz 750gram ribeye steak. this diet is working for me but i am still monitoring my blood work and i didn’t start carnivore to fix anything, i did it just to lose weight, but the benefit is most all my joint pain is gone, i lost about 40lbs in 3 months, and i have better energy levels than i remember when i was very physically active in the military at 35 and now i am 52. thanks again for you deep dive on this video. I am learning a lot form your channel.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I was carnivore for over 4 months, lost over 25 lbs., felt fantastic, and was thin & fit without any exercise. 5'10" - 132 lbs. You do the math. Was this real? I unfortunately suffered a hernia from a work related accident one week after my journey began. Fast forward to the surgery and they tell me to stop eating steaks everyday during recovery. I thought it sounded like bad advice. How was Gelatin & Pudding supposed to speed up recovery? But, alas, I'm not a doctor and the fear of any complications scared me into compliance. I immediately felt bloated, old aches & pains returned, started snoring again, my heart rate skyrocketed, and my blood pressure went through the roof. I told myself it's just 2 to 3 weeks of recovery. 15 lbs. later, in the wrong direction, I am almost done with recovery, but definitely done with eating that way. Enjoy your fruit & honey Paul. I'm gonna eat a ribeye covered in salt.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: Six years ago, had heart surgery. Over 315 pounds at 6ft. Went keto lost 40 pounds, still had arthritis. Reduced a bit. Type 2, blood 8.5 or so. It went down to around 6.  Went full lion. lost 60 pounds and everything went away. Blood sugar now 4.5 The only thing that i do not like, was i only needed about 4 to 5 hours of sleep. I would wake up like a spring. Fell of the wagon this year. Gained 50 pounds back. Plus all my issues are returning.  Scary. Back on the Human diet, losing a pound to 2 pounds a day. Mostly water i believe, and some belly fat. Body pain reducing, and my blood sugar is dropping, no longer Shakey. From my past experience i know that i will stop losing weight at some point. Sometimes for a few weeks, or more.  Fasting is a blessing for me, I can go 16 hours or so, and not be hungry.    Look this diet is boring, end of story. For me at 62, it gives me hope.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes; Arthritis

comment_text: I was keto eating fruit and vegetables. I still had high blood pressure and inflammation. Once I went carnivore with no fruits and vegetables, my hereditary high blood pressure dropped to 111/64 and my inflammation is gone.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: 1yr half on carnivore. Started to get tired, low libido, leg cramps (had been constant for a while), memory was failing but still brain function clear. Seen a video of someone talking about iodine. So I got salt with iodine. Literally the same day I started all symptoms went away. Never heard anymore mention iodine deficiency.

I know someone that has done it for a year now and has lost 100 lbs, feels GREAT! His numbers are very good, I think he would refuse to go off it the way he feels. I still have 20 additional pounds to go and feel confident that this is a lifestyle change and because I have no real cravings or hunger, it’s very doable.

Tremendous videos. I have lost 18 lbs largely because of intermittent fasting (when I have done this). If I follow IF, it is not a struggle to lose weight. Combined with focusing on organic foods, mostly, I definitely feel better.

Dr Fung- Thanks very much for your excellent information and the way you teach. This session is GREAT. I wonder if you plan to do any intermittent fasting studies in the proper manner as you mentioned today?    I have  followed you for almost 12 months and embraced various versions of IF in addition to keto whereby I limit my carbs to 30 total carbs per day. I’ve lost 50 pounds and I feel great- and healthy at 70 years old. Only 20 Pounds to go to reach my goal weight. Thanks again for the EXCELLENT sessions.

I’m loving IF/TRF. I did OMAD on my first month. No exercise, high carb and I lost 10lbs. Now on my 2nd month, now incorporating low carb sometimes 99%carnivore meal; still no exercise. This time I am doing OMAD and 2MAD depending in my hormones aka monthly period. I’m loving this lifestyle; can’t wait for my result come October 2023😊 I love this lifestyle. Thanks Megan Ramos, Dr. Jason Fung and everyone who advocates fasting and low carb meals. ❤You guys saved my life❤

Omg i literally did this i needed this video like a week ago when i started OMAD! I started with a 36 hour fast lost 4 lbs first day ate healthy was doing great next 3 days i was starving ate sooo much in my eating window (mostly carbs) and ended with an ice cream every day thinking as long as i didnt go over 1200 calories id be good i gained all 4 lbs back over the 3 days finally realized i was eating too much sugar and now ive got it back under control as well as eating normally. But man this woulda been great to know before 😆

I do 20:4 and I have a calorie budget I follow. Within the 4 hours sometimes 3 hours sometimes 2 I just follow a calorie budget sometimes I eat less but never over and I am always satiated! I walk do and do yoga almost everyday fasted this really works for me. I went from 220 to 183 in 2 months the first month I did not exercise at all. I plan to keep up this way of eating and I found that as long as I focus on a balanced meal during my feast window there is very little room or temptation to binge or eat junk food. Sometimes I will have a small dessert but if I eat a good balanced meal I barely crave or have room for it.
This guy. Amazing accurate data. I've lost 17 pounds in 10 days. I'm empowered through these posts. :) I now finally have the answers that eluded me.

I was 5'7" and 220 pounds (59 years old).   I just started 3 weeks ago doing 20:4 IF with Keto and have lost 11 pounds.  So far so good, but I have lost weight before and have a hard floor at 200 pounds that I have not been able to break through.   Hoping this works.  Plan on keep going even if I get stuck there a while as I am pre-diabetic so I want to break that and reduce my fasting blood sugar to normal.  (my blood pressure is already back to normal with the diet change and small weight loss.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I reversed Diabetes and stopped medication with keto diet and intermittent fasting 👌 my blood pressure is perfect thanks to exercising and I am about to stop B.P medication as well . At 59 y.o I feel blessed by a miracle because I did not feel so good and so strong since 30 years 🙏 thanks to Doctors like Dr Fung, Dr Berg and Dr Ekberg God Bless them all
I started fasting 18:6 for the last two months and I’ve lost 40 pounds.  I also do two 72 hour fasts twice a month.  I’m loving my new lifestyle.

Yes! Thank you! I couldn't understand why I wasn't losing weight and only consuming 1000 calories per day? I was snacking continually on apples or rice crackers and hummus or grapes. Always eating healthy whole organic foods. @ 2:00 time line in this video,  it confirmed what I learned about IR fasting over the last 2 weeks watching your videos. and other Dr. too So......I stopped snacking I began fasting for 14 - 16 hours and cut out the rice/quinoa bread and crackers (only 1 per day) and began 2 meals per day instead of 3. Anyway  GOOD NEWS! I finally broke my plateau of 9 months and lost 3 pounds in 5 days on this new routine. Feel much better already. My goal is to decrease my insulin resistance and clean my digestive system for optimal health and weight loss. I had a rather severe and unusual reaction from quitting smoking 🚬 and my body went to sleep for about 4 or 5 months . I would like to get back to my previous weight before quitting smoking and know now that losing another 15 pounds is very doable .Thank you  for 😮your videos and wisdom on these important health issues. I will keep in touch. I am so happy!
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

comment_text: I just completed a water fast for 4 days. I lost the weight I needed to.  I never felt good. I never lost hunger cravings. I added salt, magnesium, and potassium to some of the water. I didn't have headaches until the last day. Since breaking the fast, I feel 100% better. The only positives for me were losing weight fast and now my appetite isn't as big.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Doing my first fast.  I am on my 3rd day, full fast, no food - had a cup of chicken broth the first day and last eve. I weighed 165 which is heavy for me and now down 4 pounds and feel awesome.  I am 68 and suffer from leg cramps... they are gone!  Knock wood.  Going to do it today making it a 3 day fast.  Also doing it because I have 2 fat friends that are giving excuses as why they can't... want to give them inspiration to just do it!

I've now lost 259 pounds since 4/12/21 doing IF 16:8 and taking in around 1300 calories a day. I've been eating breakfast shortly after 10am and finishing my eating day with dinner before 6pm. After watching this I think I will try to move (or eliminate) my 100 to 150 calories that I usually have around 2:30 or 3:00 in the afternoon. I'm thankful to you, Dr. Fung, for showing me how this lifestyle can work for someone like me. I expect this will be something I'll be happy to continue with for the rest of my life!

This is my 63rd day of fasting and eating mostly carnivore, and I've lost 26 pounds and a dress size. I plan to fast 23 hours now and have just one meal tomorrow to hopefully get things moving a little quicker. Bonus: I no longer take blood pressure medication.

comment_text: I am working on completing a weekday fasting routine where I do a 48 hr fast from Sunday evening to Tuesday evening. Have one reasonable size meal. Then run a 72+ hr fast to end the week. The Tuesday is depending on social lifestyle to allow myself to enjoy a singular meal over a 5+ day stretch. I just completed my first run at this and have lost 13 lbs. went from 286 to 273. I broke fast with OMAD approach on Saturday and Sunday with moderate to extended workouts through softball tournaments and league play, mix of swings, sprinting, standing, various baseball like motions. Felt great.   I’m going to keep doing this because I want to go from 273-220 at the least, not sure my healthy weight at this point, and lean out. I had run into continual IF blockades and it was because I’d been doing IF with eating at 11;30 and 5:30 M-F for 2 years, with cheating on the weekends allowed (3 meals, some desserts or pizza allowed, some alcohol here and there). I made it from 386 to 286 but the plateau because aggressive. 3 months without a single pound lost. Went from 286 to 273 in just a total of 10 days. Sure, a little is probably glucose stores in my muscle, but my understanding is 95% of that SHOULD we fat weight lost, and that’s fine by me. But it’s clear I can do days without eating.

Dear Dr. Fung,   Thank you for this clarification and for all your wise advice over the years.  Yes, I have been making the mistake of eating too much on OMAD — I did think I needed to make up the calories.    My digestive process is also very slow.  Food stays in my stomach a long time.  Eating too much is not helping my digestive process either.  I’m learning by trial and error.  It seems that I do better if I have a 24-36 hour fast once a week.  After the longer fast period, my fasting glucose is 4.2 in AM before starting OMAD.  After that, I eat once a day in the late morning.   I eat healthy low carb food, drink water, water with apple cider vinegar and herbal tea each day.  I tend to want to eat too much protein though.  I already kind of figured that I had been eating too much during OMAD because my fasting glucose can be 5.0 —5.5 the next morning.  That’s seems prediabetic to me or too close for comfort.  I have however, lost 10 pounds in the past seven weeks.  I feel good about knowing I’m learning how to reverse bad habits.  I really am striving for Autophagy for the health benefits, not just weight loss.  The addiction to sugar is still active though.  I jumped out of Ketosis when I ate chocolate on Valentine’s Day, which was a real eye opener.  I concluded that my days of binging on chocolate are over.    I’m over 60 and my glucose was 10, 2 1/2 hours after eating the surgery chocolate.  I had to take a long walk and drink extra water to bring it down before bed that night. I realized that I am too old to do eat chocolate like that anymore.  I’m sure that can be damaging to my body.  It was still 5.8 the next morning.  That also scared me.  My goal is to get back to morning readings in the 4.0 - 5 range.  I feel much better then.  Thank you for all your advice on you tube.  I’m just going to keep trying.  It is helpful to read all the success stories.  Thank you to everyone for sharing your success stories.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: My favorite fast length is nine or ten days. I like to start a fast on Friday after early dinner and resume eating Sunday evening the following weekend or noonish on Monday. This allows me to avoid work stresses when starting my fast and get walks in the sun, and then get another weekend of light exercise in toward the end of my fast. I can usually best tolerate fasting during work if I'm already fasted when the work week begins. Additionally, by the time we reach 7 days, the benefits of fasting are thought to have reached the maximum, so all things considered, this seems optimal. I have done 22 days before, but I find I begin "missing food" if I go much longer than a week.   Another useful fast for me is Friday night to Sunday night or Monday morning, for similar reasons. Sometimes I fast just a day or two, often 36 hours. The easiest daily pattern for me is 16/8 or 18/4, but some think it's not a good idea to do that every day, and I don't. I do that two or three times a week, usually on resistance training days, but always try to keep the eating period under 12 hours.  I don't seem to meaningfully lose weight unless I fast over 24 hours, and doing a fast of a week or so helps me break through a weight plateau. I hadn't fasted much longer than 72 hours in a couple years, so I decided to do a longer fast last weekend, and I'm coming up on seven days. It's been a good fast, and I plan to end it at around nine or ten days because it's been so long since I did one. I plan to do another extended fast at the end of summer. It's easier to do an extended fast when the weather is warm, so I'll probably wait until at least spring.  PS: I lost over 100 pounds with quarterly extended fasting and eating low-carb between fasts.

In March 2021, I lost 13 pounds after two weeks of being sick. I thought what a great start to losing even more weight to get healthy. While searching for fasting ideas I came across your channel and I watched a few videos and became inspired to try it out. So my start date was November 7, 2021 and I started to see the pounds melt away literally. I was losing about 3lbs a week on average. I must admit it was super hard to get through first 2-3 days. I was eating fresh food and no sugar or carbs (Ok maybe a couple carbs got devoured ). But I focused my attention on it and now see the results of this type of daily diet. It’s now February 17, 2022 (4 months approximately ) and I now weigh 193lbs. That’s a 35lbs loss ( before 228lbs-after 193lbs and I’m 5’10) Wow!! I had to buy new clothes and now wear smaller sizes. Now I still need to lose about 15lbs until I reach my long term goal. It’s been amazing and now my wife is interested in this based on my results. Hopefully I can give an update in another 3 months. [ Side note: Yes sometimes I still eat some minimal junk while doing this. Like chips and salas and potatoes and I don’t see it as failure but these food are good and more a treat. I also had to redefine what a treat is. It’s not every night after dinner, every day or week for myself. My sugar cravings have lowered so much that I don’t have a high sugar desire and makes these food overly sweet and they don’t taste as good anymore. So keep up the great work on this style of health. comment_text: SO appreciate this video! I do have the dawn effect as I have a 4 - 6 hour eating window before fasting and then I routinely water fast one day a week (about 36-42 hours).  But I was very concerned as the glucose went up after a recent 3 day fast. I'm not fat but I do have visceral fat around the belly. Compounding the rise in sugar is the fact that I just recently started eating meat after about 15 years the last 4 of which were Keto Vegetarian (only dairy and fish allowed). By consuming meat I may be virtually zero to little carb, but the protein (glucogenesis?) has caused my levels to go up even more. I blame the rise also on the fact that my metabolism is very slow in a 65 year old and I've read it takes 3 days to digest meat. Seems to be an uphill battle. So I will wean myself off meat except for salmon and eggs. My recent blood work shows my triglycerides within range even though my A1C is 8.3 and my blood glucose is 227. FYI, I don't take medications for my type-2, but I do take a lot of supplements. I did an experiment where I stopped all supplements for about 3 weeks. My blood sugar went down slightly. Hmmm.... Big thanks to Dr. Fung for his books!

I've fasted all lengths, up to 22 days. I've long felt the "magic" period was between 5 and 10 days, as someone who was morbidly obese at the time. At this point, I felt like I never needed to eat again, and full of energy, as long as I didn't severely tax myself. Eventually, I experienced the effect of "missing" food, kinda like a "treat" I remembered having had. The feeling of being fasted around a week is a memorable and powerful experience.  It's been about two years since I lost over 100 pounds, and I could stand to lose another 25 pounds (of fat) to reach my "ideal weight," but my body responds differently now to fasting than it did. It doesn't seem as easy to fast beyond 72 hours or so. Perhaps it's a natural mechanism we still don't understand, and my body "knows" it's shed the extremely unhealthy weight. Regardless, I would still like to lose that extra fat. Doing so would put me within the statistical mean of healthy body fat percentage.  A tip to would-be fasters: I strongly suggest full-body and weight-bearing exercise while fasting. I believe autophagy led me to lose some lean body mass I did not wish to lose. I have resumed resistance training and high-intensity interval training, which I did before becoming obese, as I wish to gain more lean mass and retain what I have. I do resistance training and cardio while fasted. I don't do HIIT while fasted for more than 24 hours.

My only problem is I am an alcoholic. My reason for drinking was because I was a loner and never felt comfortable in social situations without being drunk. I did this for a very long time...I got married when I was 38 (for the first, and hopefuly the only time),  I met a fantastic lady who is a very social (as if it's a bad thing) person and continued to drink to keep up with being social. I finally got sick of feeling like crap every morning from the booze and decided to quit. It was 2 weeks before I started to quit drinking that I ran into a friend of mine that I used to work with that told me he was on a carnivore diet. I asked, what is that and he told me he read a book by Shawn Baker, he tried it and he was feeling great. With the respect I had for my friend, I decided to try it and it changed my life!!! I did the carnivore thing, ate eggs, ribeye steaks, bacon, butter and sometimes some suit and before I new it I was feeling better. I decided to quit drinking beer (my flavor of choice) and I found it much easier to quit than I thought it would be. I don't know if the carnivore diet work for everyone but it's worth giving a try.

comment_text: My A1C got high. The Dr. said I was Diabetic and wanted to start me on Metformin.  I said give me a few months and test again.  I changed my diet to mainly meat and cheese.  I limited bread, and other carbs I lost 70 lbs. in six months and A1C came out normal.

I have been on this carnivore journey now for 5 month now. I started out at 360lbs and as of right now today I'm at 330lbs and have lost 30lbs so far and I have to say that for me, you cannot just eat all the meat you want! If you do that, you will gain weight! I do not have any carbs and that includes eggs because eggs do have a small amount of carbs in them. No sugars or fake sugars that may give zero carbs but do damage to you in other ways. I drink Water only and if I feel like having a soda I drink La Crox water. It is the best no sugar or sweetener drink out there, just essenced sparkling water.   The amount of calories per day depend on how active you are and if you are going to work out while doing this diet. I am a Truck Driver. I eat once a day while I drive my truck because that is when I reach for food. When I go home I do not eat, I drink more water, do what ever I need to do and if I start to get hungry I go to bed. I eat about 1.2lbs to 1.8lbs of a cheap $8 chuck steak each day. That is about 1500 to 1700 Calories. No more, no less. I am usually done eating by 3pm, home by 6pm and in bed by 8pm. I lose about 1lb per day. There are days where I mix it up with hamburger and bacon, but it is very hard to count the calories with them and very easy to over eat. That has set me back a few times. So when ever I here people say eat all the meat you want, it makes me angry because I can't eat all the meat I want! Are you kidding me! I tried that and gained a lot of weight in the process. The only way this works is by controlling your calories. I feel amazing just eating the meat and for the most part I'm not that hungry anymore because I have shrunk my stomach after 5months of doing this.  This diet is awesome, but no one ever explains anything, so I am telling all, if you are doing this and hitting road blocks like I did, it's because when you tell a fat person to eat all the meat you want, that is the problem! This is an elimination diet start with meat and water, it will suck at first but it gets better. Do not use any spices of any kind because they have carbs period. Salt and Salt only on your steak. Cook it on medium heat no more than 3-4min on each side chop it up in small pieces, put it in a container and salt it shack it up salt it again and that is you breakfast, lunch and dinner eat it slowly, snacking on it here and there. Drink more water than you care to and that help fill the void in your stomach to turn off the hunger receptors for short periods of time.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes
