If you do that, you will gain weight! I do not have any carbs and that includes eggs because eggs do have a small amount of carbs in them. No sugars or fake sugars that may give zero carbs but do damage to you in other ways. I drink Water only and if I feel like having a soda I drink La Crox water. It is the best no sugar or sweetener drink out there, just essenced sparkling water.   The amount of calories per day depend on how active you are and if you are going to work out while doing this diet. I am a Truck Driver. I eat once a day while I drive my truck because that is when I reach for food. When I go home I do not eat, I drink more water, do what ever I need to do and if I start to get hungry I go to bed. I eat about 1.2lbs to 1.8lbs of a cheap $8 chuck steak each day. That is about 1500 to 1700 Calories. No more, no less. I am usually done eating by 3pm, home by 6pm and in bed by 8pm. I lose about 1lb per day. There are days where I mix it up with hamburger and bacon, but it is very hard to count the calories with them and very easy to over eat. That has set me back a few times. So when ever I here people say eat all the meat you want, it makes me angry because I can't eat all the meat I want! Are you kidding me! I tried that and gained a lot of weight in the process. The only way this works is by controlling your calories. I feel amazing just eating the meat and for the most part I'm not that hungry anymore because I have shrunk my stomach after 5months of doing this.  This diet is awesome, but no one ever explains anything, so I am telling all, if you are doing this and hitting road blocks like I did, it's because when you tell a fat person to eat all the meat you want, that is the problem! This is an elimination diet start with meat and water, it will suck at first but it gets better. Do not use any spices of any kind because they have carbs period. Salt and Salt only on your steak. Cook it on medium heat no more than 3-4min on each side chop it up in small pieces, put it in a container and salt it shack it up salt it again and that is you breakfast, lunch and dinner eat it slowly, snacking on it here and there. Drink more water than you care to and that help fill the void in your stomach to turn off the hunger receptors for short periods of time.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I lost 60 lbs on keto in about 5 months...I kept it under 20g. Just sharing my anecdote. also want to add, I no longer drink sugar drinks, just sugar free and I only eat once a day and I really only eat because I feel like I should more so than that I am hungry. I am literally never hungry. I eat carbs now and cake and whatever but I do not gain weight. My issue is that I love food so much that it is when I would start eating, I want to keep eating but the one meal a day has made my stomach shrink and I have pulled way back on the over eating. I just stop when the portion I have made is gone and since Im so used to not eating these days it doesnt bother me any more. full disclosure I usually do have some sort of dessert about 30 minutes to an hour after eating dinner. Again, I don't gain weight now.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

I have been doing keto and (now) Carnivore and I have to say this was the best thing, health wise, I have ever done. I lost over 50 lbs in about 1.5 to 2 years and I am still not done. I think doctors have been lying to us for too long. The food pyramid is all wrong. Carbs should be on the top, not at the bottom.
Amazing!!! This hit home so hard! I’m doing Carnivore now! However thanks to you Dr. Berry showing me exactly how to do keto, I have lost 150 pounds! Thank you for all you share with us❤️

What a great video!! So much great info!! I love listening to Kelly Hogan!!  I want to go carnivore, but with fibromyalgia and chronic fatigue syndrome and spine issues and surgeries, I struggle to cook often enough!! Saw my internist today and asked about my metformin, which is 500mg twice a day! Diagnosed with pre -diabetes years ago, but have lost 45 pounds, even though it took me two years!! Was told my last AIC was 5.7 one year ago, and the one before that was about the same. So they told me to stop my metformin now, and did lab work today, and told me to come back in 4 months to check it again!  Told her I lost the weight going keto with lots of protein! She definitely approved of that!! Now I have extra motivation not to cheat, so my A1C will be good in 4 months without metformin!! Thanks to Dr. Berry and Neisha for your encouragement!! Love this video and the inspiration from Kelly!!   Now I feel like I can do this, even with my pain!  The benefits will be worth the pain!! Thanks to you both for your encouragement, and changing my life!! God bless you!! 🤗🙏
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: My A1C got high. The Dr. said I was Diabetic and wanted to start me on Metformin.  I said give me a few months and test again.  I changed my diet to mainly meat and cheese.  I limited bread, and other carbs I lost 70 lbs. in six months and A1C came out normal.

I have been on this carnivore journey now for 5 month now. I started out at 360lbs and as of right now today I'm at 330lbs and have lost 30lbs so far and I have to say that for me, you cannot just eat all the meat you want! If you do that, you will gain weight! I do not have any carbs and that includes eggs because eggs do have a small amount of carbs in them. No sugars or fake sugars that may give zero carbs but do damage to you in other ways. I drink Water only and if I feel like having a soda I drink La Crox water. It is the best no sugar or sweetener drink out there, just essenced sparkling water.   The amount of calories per day depend on how active you are and if you are going to work out while doing this diet. I am a Truck Driver. I eat once a day while I drive my truck because that is when I reach for food. When I go home I do not eat, I drink more water, do what ever I need to do and if I start to get hungry I go to bed. I eat about 1.2lbs to 1.8lbs of a cheap $8 chuck steak each day. That is about 1500 to 1700 Calories. No more, no less. I am usually done eating by 3pm, home by 6pm and in bed by 8pm. I lose about 1lb per day. There are days where I mix it up with hamburger and bacon, but it is very hard to count the calories with them and very easy to over eat. That has set me back a few times. So when ever I here people say eat all the meat you want, it makes me angry because I can't eat all the meat I want! Are you kidding me! I tried that and gained a lot of weight in the process. The only way this works is by controlling your calories. I feel amazing just eating the meat and for the most part I'm not that hungry anymore because I have shrunk my stomach after 5months of doing this.  This diet is awesome, but no one ever explains anything, so I am telling all, if you are doing this and hitting road blocks like I did, it's because when you tell a fat person to eat all the meat you want, that is the problem! This is an elimination diet start with meat and water, it will suck at first but it gets better. Do not use any spices of any kind because they have carbs period. Salt and Salt only on your steak. Cook it on medium heat no more than 3-4min on each side chop it up in small pieces, put it in a container and salt it shack it up salt it again and that is you breakfast, lunch and dinner eat it slowly, snacking on it here and there. Drink more water than you care to and that help fill the void in your stomach to turn off the hunger receptors for short periods of time.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I lost 60 lbs on keto in about 5 months...I kept it under 20g. I am literally never hungry. I eat carbs now and cake and whatever but I do not gain weight. My issue is that I love food so much that it is when I would start eating, I want to keep eating but the one meal a day has made my stomach shrink and I have pulled way back on the over eating. I just stop when the portion I have made is gone and since Im so used to not eating these days it doesnt bother me any more. full disclosure I usually do have some sort of dessert about 30 minutes to an hour after eating dinner. comment_text: My A1C got high. The Dr. said I was Diabetic and wanted to start me on Metformin.  I said give me a few months and test again.  I changed my diet to mainly meat and cheese.  I limited bread, and other carbs I lost 70 lbs. in six months and A1C came out normal.

I have been on this carnivore journey now for 5 month now. I started out at 360lbs and as of right now today I'm at 330lbs and have lost 30lbs so far and I have to say that for me, you cannot just eat all the meat you want! If you do that, you will gain weight! I do not have any carbs and that includes eggs because eggs do have a small amount of carbs in them. No sugars or fake sugars that may give zero carbs but do damage to you in other ways. I drink Water only and if I feel like having a soda I drink La Crox water. It is the best no sugar or sweetener drink out there, just essenced sparkling water.   The amount of calories per day depend on how active you are and if you are going to work out while doing this diet. I am a Truck Driver. I eat once a day while I drive my truck because that is when I reach for food. When I go home I do not eat, I drink more water, do what ever I need to do and if I start to get hungry I go to bed. I eat about 1.2lbs to 1.8lbs of a cheap $8 chuck steak each day. That is about 1500 to 1700 Calories. No more, no less. I am usually done eating by 3pm, home by 6pm and in bed by 8pm. I lose about 1lb per day. There are days where I mix it up with hamburger and bacon, but it is very hard to count the calories with them and very easy to over eat. That has set me back a few times. So when ever I here people say eat all the meat you want, it makes me angry because I can't eat all the meat I want! Are you kidding me! I tried that and gained a lot of weight in the process. The only way this works is by controlling your calories. I feel amazing just eating the meat and for the most part I'm not that hungry anymore because I have shrunk my stomach after 5months of doing this.  This diet is awesome, but no one ever explains anything, so I am telling all, if you are doing this and hitting road blocks like I did, it's because when you tell a fat person to eat all the meat you want, that is the problem! This is an elimination diet start with meat and water, it will suck at first but it gets better. Do not use any spices of any kind because they have carbs period. Salt and Salt only on your steak. Cook it on medium heat no more than 3-4min on each side chop it up in small pieces, put it in a container and salt it shack it up salt it again and that is you breakfast, lunch and dinner eat it slowly, snacking on it here and there. Drink more water than you care to and that help fill the void in your stomach to turn off the hunger receptors for short periods of time.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes

I have been on this carnivore journey now for 5 month now. I started out at 360lbs and as of right now today I'm at 330lbs and have lost 30lbs so far and I have to say that for me, you cannot just eat all the meat you want! If you do that, you will gain weight! I do not have any carbs and that includes eggs because eggs do have a small amount of carbs in them. No sugars or fake sugars that may give zero carbs but do damage to you in other ways. I drink Water only and if I feel like having a soda I drink La Crox water. It is the best no sugar or sweetener drink out there, just essenced sparkling water.   The amount of calories per day depend on how active you are and if you are going to work out while doing this diet. I am a Truck Driver. I eat once a day while I drive my truck because that is when I reach for food. When I go home I do not eat, I drink more water, do what ever I need to do and if I start to get hungry I go to bed. I eat about 1.2lbs to 1.8lbs of a cheap $8 chuck steak each day. That is about 1500 to 1700 Calories. No more, no less. I am usually done eating by 3pm, home by 6pm and in bed by 8pm. I lose about 1lb per day. There are days where I mix it up with hamburger and bacon, but it is very hard to count the calories with them and very easy to over eat. That has set me back a few times. So when ever I here people say eat all the meat you want, it makes me angry because I can't eat all the meat I want! Are you kidding me! I tried that and gained a lot of weight in the process. The only way this works is by controlling your calories. I feel amazing just eating the meat and for the most part I'm not that hungry anymore because I have shrunk my stomach after 5months of doing this.  This diet is awesome, but no one ever explains anything, so I am telling all, if you are doing this and hitting road blocks like I did, it's because when you tell a fat person to eat all the meat you want, that is the problem! This is an elimination diet start with meat and water, it will suck at first but it gets better. Do not use any spices of any kind because they have carbs period. Salt and Salt only on your steak. Cook it on medium heat no more than 3-4min on each side chop it up in small pieces, put it in a container and salt it shack it up salt it again and that is you breakfast, lunch and dinner eat it slowly, snacking on it here and there. Drink more water than you care to and that help fill the void in your stomach to turn off the hunger receptors for short periods of time.

comment_text: Maybe no one sees this but, for the first time, I’ve consciously chosen to do a 5 day fast. Just beginning day 4 of it. Day 3 has a stomach acid taste building up in my throat. I think this is my stomach acid rebuilding.  I’m down 13 pounds in 3 days from 276 to 263 so far.

I'm 66 years old and lost 30 lbs. I'm down to 170. I lost 17 lbs in the first two weeks. Almost strictly carnivore and I eat at 2 PM every day. I really liked them! Who knew! Thank you Dr Berry!!

My wife and I decided to give this a try figuring we can do this for a month. We stopped tracking it as it just plain fits for us so we expect to just keep it up. She’s seen so many hormone related benefits, no hot flashes a big one, allergy inflammation gone (except when she has her nemesis red wine), weight loss, clearer thinking, better sleep no mid afternoon fatigue. Me, weight loss and leaning out generally. Already down to my “ideal” weight. Less inflammation than I might otherwise have.   Only downer was leg muscle cramps. Those sucked but adding a magnesium supplement pretty much looked after than one.   No looking back now.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes

aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; PCOS; Thyroid Disease

comment_text: I started 20 days ago. Within 48hrs I could feel results in many areas. In weight alone, I've lost about 15lbs in those 20 days. I transitioned over from keto. It has now been just less than 6 weeks and I'm down a total of 25lbs as of 11 May.

I'm soooooo hoping this will get rid of my autoimmune because the meds can cause cancer and I've already had breast cancer 3 years ago.  The liver is a hard one for me.  I start going to the butcher up in Tennessee. aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Cancer; Thyroid Disease; Autoimmune Disease

comment_text: Exactly! Since I started eating the Carnivore/KETO diet I have lost over 14 pounds and 5" around my body. My arms and legs are getting really skinny first, the belly fat is going, but I had more of it than on my extremities.  I have been avoiding sugars and flour products. When I make the mistake of eating something that contains those ingredients, I pay a price in pain.  No cola or canned & bottled drinks!

“I was on KETO for 2 years. I lost weight and my blood sugars and A1C went down but then…. My blood sugar numbers started going up along with my weight and my A1C went from a 5.1 to 5.7. I decided to go plant based because that's what I was before but I wasn’t doing it the correct way. This time I did it with no fat/low fat. My A1C went down to 5.1. My fasting blood sugar is 77 and I’m eating more carbs than ever before. I trust Cyrus and Robby!”  fredsj
Started two days ago. Already dropped 6 lbs in water weight (bathroom runs are wild). Starting out as a 45 day plan to lose weight before vacation. We’ll see how it goes!

I’m coming up on 1 month of carnivore+small portions of fruit. I was skeptical and worried at first after reading the “risks” and high cholesterol bs. I’m happy to say I feel better than I have in a long time. Bloating gone, constipation gone, blood pressure down, 10lbs down. All this accomplished with out feeling hungry or worrying about counting macros. I decided to go for 60 days now due to my success, thanks Doc!  UPDATE: Approaching 3 months and I’m down from a chunky 200lbs to 178lbs. I think I’m a lifer at this point. No negative side effects
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile

comment_text: I have been carnivore for 30 days. Instead of dreading my yearly physical in months I’m excited to see my results on labs.  Most of all I love that Dr. Berry teaches to use “nose to tail” of the animal. (Carbs is another word for sugar.). Saved my life, I had neuropathy down leg and foot and nearly blind. It all reversed itself and in a short time too - a couple of months. And my blood sugar was reaching highs of 27.0!! (home blood glucose machines do not even go that high). I was diagnosed with diabetes four years ago, after only a few months on low carb, was in full remission and that was 3.5 years ago. I haven't looked back since. Saved my life. (My cat's diabetes is also in remission following similar principals).
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: Been on keto since March - reversed T2 Diabetes, gangrene, hypertension & lost weight. Now, after watching this video I note that I have no more skin tags!
aspect_names: Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Hypertension

comment_text: Some of my skin tags disappeared too
My skin tags are gone. I had over a 100 removed every year. Was not cheap. Thank you Dr Berry
My skin tags disappeared after fasting on and off they just disappeared
Since I started Keto, I lost weight  , feel better and the skin tags are disappearing indeed!

I went on carnivore in December 2022 after experiencing chronic diarrhea for months (I was diagnosed with microscopic colitis). The carnivore diet not only healed my gut issue, but apparently made a skin tag on my leg disappear. It had been there for years, and drove me crazy because it itched sometimes, I would scratch it without thinking about it and make it bleed. After a few weeks on carnivore, I  was in the shower and noticed it was completely gone. I couldn't believe it!

Yep, I switched to a keto diet about 2 months ago and my skin tags disappeared. It was the strangest thing. I didn’t realize that switching my diet was likely the reason they went away. Most changes and improvements happen at a cellular level and are measured by doing labs but this was something you could physically monitor and evaluate.
Been doing ketovore  for almost 4 months. I’ve lost 65 pounds my a1c has gone from 9.1 to 6.2. And my skin tags are almost gone! 👍🏻
aspect_names: Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

comment_text: He is spot on.  I was diagnosed with Type 2 with CKD last year.  Was fighting weight lost with diets that wouldn’t work.  Finally went to a weight lost Dr. And was put on Ozempic and told to follow a low carb diet.  Happy to say after 4 1/2 months I’ve lost 45 lbs and skin tags are diminishing!  Hoping that I can stay on this as my eFGR will determine that, but for now I’m pleased!
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Chronic Kidney Disease

comment_text: When I was pregnant with my son I started developing skin tags all over my neck. I also had gestational diabetes. When I seen the metabolic specialist oh, they had a student with them. They pointed out my skin tags to the student. And the student nodded and agreed. I asked what the significance was and they looked me in the eye and said nothing. I've not developed any skin tags since my gestational diabetes is gone.
My skin tags disappeared when I did intermittent fasting on low carb.

wow. I'm puzzled to find out that skintags are not what I thought they were. My dad has diabetes and I'm always afraid that one day I'll suffer from that as well. I am obese and started my weight loss journey one month ago. So far I lost 26lbs and I'm really proud. I will try the keto diet and see what changes.
Yes my skin tags disappeared with low carb diet.

Wow, my mind is blown! I had one on the outside corner of each eye and one on my neck. I've had them for thirty years, my dad had tons of them so I just figured it was my genetics. I started keto and intermittent fasting last year and lost 40 lbs. I didn't even notice that they were gone until I watched this video. I felt my face and just laughed, that's awesome!
No more skin tags (only had a few anyway). LCHF since Jan 2019.

Yep...they “dropped” off. I also had a lump on my shin bone that a dermatologist was going to remove (warning it would scar and possibly return)...scheduled for three months later because her practice was so busy, and I started keto\carnivore, by the time the surgery date came it had disappeared...along with several skin tags. Yepper!. The bonus was I dropped 40 pounds and my mood improved greatly. I was having symptoms of MS along with seizures and managed to decrease the episodes by about 80%...I’m still working on it. I see this as a permanent way of managing my health.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I'm so glad someone said this. I've been on keto for a month and a half. My skin tags are going away. Also the swelling in my feet is mostly gone. I've only lost 22 pounds ,but everything is getting better. My episodes of stress inconvenience are even better. Keto is amazing
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: It’s true. Skin tags disappeared around five months after keto

yup. most all skin tags gone (except 2 lol )  I had at least a dozen of them
I just realized my skin tags are gone!!! I' doing keto  for more than an year now, lost 35 kilograms (around 20 pounds).

My nephrologist commented on my skin tags and suggested I have "my dermatologist" remove them. This was three years before I was diagnosed diabetic. I was seeing her because of kidney health issues due to hypertension.  Insulin resistance, metabolic syndrome, hyperinsulinemia and prediabetes were not mentioned. My fasting insulin was never tested.  I believe all skin tags are gone today, after keto and fasting. Blemishes on my back and shoulders are gone too. I don't seem to get pimples, either.
aspect_names: Skin Health; Kidney Function; Type 2 Diabetes; Hypertension

I recall a time I thought I was eating healthy by having oatmeal every morning with a glass of orange juice. Then a fruit smoothie for lunch and a penutbutter and banana sandwich for dinner.  I erupted in eczema, had swollen lymph nodes, and severe candida overgrowth. My blood platelets were low, my triglycerides were sky high and my gallbladder was ready to exit my body. Stiff joints gone. Night blindness gone. Lifelong hypertension gone. There was only one thing left that was killing me. So I got divorced. Now I'm healthy AND happy.

aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Glycemic Control

comment_text: My A1C was 7.8 and my doctor was going to put me on insulin and I asked him to give me 90 days to change this. I watched the netflix documentary "The Game Changers" and it really changed my life. I started eating a bowl of oatmeal every day filled with dried cranberries, walnuts, chia seeds, dried blueberries/strawberries. And 90 days later, my A1C was 5.7 and I have proof. I changed to a plant based diet eating mostly carbs and protein from lentils, soy, tofu, beans, peanut butter, etc. My cholesterol went down significantly and my doctor had to cut down my medications by 50%. Saving me money and adding years to my life. With all due respect, I have to disagree with you. Plant based is the way to go. Respectfully, Frank

I wish I wish I had known this years ago.  I ate oatmeal for the better part of my life (I'm 50 now).  Although I never suffered from obesity, I experienced brain fog for much of my life.  Only recently (9 months ago), was I strongly suggested by a physician that I undertake a keto diet because I suffer from osteoarthritis in my right shoulder.  I remember from a previous video that Dr. Berry talked about PRP therapy for joint pain and keto diet.  Well, it's been 8 months now and I can say my future looks much brighter even though it's not as sweet.  Gone are the morning stiffness and the brain fog.  Lost 7 pounds even though I didn't need to lose weight.  I promised myself that one year after my PRP therapy, I would go back for an ultrasound to see if new cartilage has formed.  It's a long road, but I can see hope.  I was lost but now I'm found.

I can't speak to the medical aspect of what you are saying, but everything else, has been the total opposite for me.  I am a type 2 diabetic and been trying different diets; when I went on oats and fruit (every day), my blood pressure dropped drastically....  I was on meds, but they weren't working good enough, and I was sitting at 145/90 most days (which was much lower than before the meds)...  After a few weeks of only oats with fruit, my blood pressure dropped to 105/59 as the average.... my blood sugar dropped, also, and my doctor was please for the first time in a long time.  When I went all meat, I got sick... my blood pressure shot way up, again, my skin got all red with white blotches, and my face felt flushed all the time... my colitis came back... But, when I went back to oatmeal, my colitis cleared up and everything else went back to normal.  I find all you doctors, on the internet, say different things; some say oats are bad, others say it is good.... some say to only eat plants.... others, say plants will kill you.... others say only eat meat.... others say that  eating only meat will give you cancer.... etc....  Not being a doctor nor being educated in nutrition, I cannot make an educated guess as to which doctors to believe. The way I look at it, whatever we eat, will kill us... so, I might just as well eat what makes me feel good. Oats makes me feel great... and I am satiated after eating oats and I don't get hungry again, like you said.  [NOTE: anyone else reading this; I am NOT giving any advice, by sharing, openly, with the doctor, what happened with me on my different diets... I am just letting him know, my experience.]

comment_text: When it comes to eating, life has never been this simple. I went to the carnivore diet after a neck fusion surgery that left me with a leaky gut due to the antibiotics and opiates. I was unable to eat carbs of any sort without debilitating nausea. It took some experimentation to figure out that meat (animal products) was the only thing I could eat. At the time of the surgery, I weighed in at the hospital at 240lbs. I'm 53 yrs old and 6'1" tall. That put me at about 55-60lbs overweight. I started full carnivore in February, 2019. I didn't go carnivore to lose weight. However, after being on the carnivore diet (on and off) for about 4 months now, I am down to 209lbs. I say on and off as I have been experimenting with various non-animal products to see if my system can handle them. It's amazing what I have found:       1. If I am eating strictly carnivore, I will lose 1-2 pounds a week no matter how much I eat. When I initially went on the diet, I lost 5lbs the first few days. I found later this was water weight lose due to my insulin levels coming down. I do generally eat one large meal only once a day, but because of all of the fat I'm eating, that is as much as I can stand to eat.   2. Speaking of fat, I eat a lot of it. I pick the fattiest cuts of meat, generally ribeye, cooked in tallow and/or ghee. I then melt about 2 tbs of butter on top of the steak. When I'm done eating, I drink any remaining grease on the plate. No joke. I eat all the butter, cheese, bacon, heavy whipping cream, etc. that I want.     3. Prior to the surgery, I was on blood pressure medication and Levothyroxin (for hypothyroid). After some research, I found I don't need to take either of these poisons. I have not taken a single pill, not even aspirin, since late February. My blood pressure is as low as I've ever seen it at 117/76 (last measure) and my thyroid panel blood test shows my TSH levels are well within range.   4. After being on this diet for 3-4 weeks, I noticed a HUGE uptick in energy. I also noted that inflammation in my body disappeared. I used to ride motocross and I have broken 18 bones, including 2 compressed vertebrae in my lower back, so there was a lot of inflammation. I also had brain fog that is gone now. I went from a bitter, angry person, to a happy, energetic person in just a few weeks. I seriously don't think I have felt this good my whole life... or at least as long as I can remember.   It's really interesting, I I eat something off the carnivore menu, the weight lose stops immediately. For example, about 2 weeks ago, I was down to 210 lbs and decided to treat myself by eating a Hershey's chocolate bar. I weighed myself the next morning and I had gained 2 pounds and it didn't come back off for another 3 days. I believe it was only water retention, not actual fat that I gained. I also notice my inflammation and brain fog will come back. I have done several "experiments" similar to the above, a single teaspoon of sugar in my coffee, a spoonful of peanut butter, etc. and had similar results. Carnivore is now a lifestyle instead of a diet to me. I don't think I will go back to the standard American diet (SAD), even if I can. As time goes on, I find I'm not craving the other foods as much, so the "experimentation" with non-carnivore is getting less frequent as I care less and less about wanting to incorporate other foods into my diet. Besides, preparing and cooking a steak takes no more than 20 minutes and I only have to do it once a day. My freezer is full and my pantry is empty. Grocery shopping takes 15 minutes and I usually only go about twice a month. I think everyone should try this for at least 1 month. It could change your life for the better, like it did for me. Life is much simpler.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Blood Pressure; Thyroid Disease

This is a long post and I apologize. I first saw this video on 3/20/22. It motivated the hell out of me. I have had type II diabetes for 22 years. I ignored all medical advice until I was put on medication. Still, not taking it seriously until my lab numbers were off the hook. Years ago I developed neuropathy in my feet. More medication. My addiction to sugar & carbs were just going to kill me. I was resigned to that fact. Missing doctor appts. and still eating loads of carbs and sugar. Two weeks ago my A1C was 11.1. Down from a year ago at 12.1. My blood pressure numbers always scared the nurses. They thought I would stroke out in the chair. On 3/20/22, after viewing this video, I went to the kitchen and gathered all the carbs I had, bundled them up and placed them into a compost bin. Carbs, I thought were good for me. They've been killing me for years.  Today, 3/21/2022, I got up early, raced to Trader Joe's and bought the foods listed to do this Keto Diet Hack. I was motivated by this video. I felt a surge of positivity. I checked my glucose levels 90 minutes after my first meal, it was 20 points higher but not like the outlandish numbers I've seen. Usually after grain laden meals, the numbers fluctuated by 100 to 150 points. My first day on this diet and I got a lot done today. My usual routine was just to procrastinate and give in to the type II hell that I've been living in for years. Food day: 10:00AM: Pint of black coffee, 5 eggs scrambled,  butter, slice of bacon & steak strips. Lots of water. 4:02PM: 4 boiled eggs, 5 strips of bacon & beef. Lots of water. I am stuffed. Obviously done for the day.  I will add avocado & organ meat as I continue 30 days on this diet. Thanks Doc for your folksy approach and for keeping it simple for people like me. I have no doubt I will have very good success on this diet. I look forward, one day, to going to sleep without foot pain keeping me wide awake.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

My addiction to sugar & carbs were just going to kill me. I was resigned to that fact. Missing doctor appts. and still eating loads of carbs and sugar. Two weeks ago my A1C was 11.1. Down from a year ago at 12.1. My blood pressure numbers always scared the nurses. They thought I would stroke out in the chair. On 3/20/22, after viewing this video, I went to the kitchen and gathered all the carbs I had, bundled them up and placed them into a compost bin. Carbs, I thought were good for me. They've been killing me for years.  Today, 3/21/2022, I got up early, raced to Trader Joe's and bought the foods listed to do this Keto Diet Hack. I was motivated by this video. I felt a surge of positivity. I checked my glucose levels 90 minutes after my first meal, it was 20 points higher but not like the outlandish numbers I've seen. Usually after grain laden meals, the numbers fluctuated by 100 to 150 points. My first day on this diet and I got a lot done today. My usual routine was just to procrastinate and give in to the type II hell that I've been living in for years. Food day: 10:00AM: Pint of black coffee, 5 eggs scrambled,  butter, slice of bacon & steak strips. Lots of water. 4:02PM: 4 boiled eggs, 5 strips of bacon & beef. Lots of water. I am stuffed. Obviously done for the day.  I will add avocado & organ meat as I continue 30 days on this diet. Thanks Doc for your folksy approach and for keeping it simple for people like me. I have no doubt I will have very good success on this diet. I look forward, one day, to going to sleep without foot pain keeping me wide awake.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

I've been on a keto diet since February 2022. I have lost 45lbs. I'm doing this because I have many things wrong from a ruptured  appendix and Lyme disease.  I have arthritis and they think fibromyalgia from the lymes. I haven't felt this good in 20 years. I think  the primal diet is the way to go. We will see long term.

My European trained Oncologist/Hematologist put me on Keto a year ago to improve my overall health and help me drop the 20 extra pounds I found impossible to lose - following low fat, low calorie diet with excessive exercise. It would NOT come off… he schooled me that the cholesterol goals we’ve been told to follow are GARBAGE.  In 5 weeks I lost 22 pounds and have ENERGY.   American Doctors such as Dr Berry and Dr Ovadia need to put the Patient first.  Very informative video. Thank you 🙏🙌
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Anthropometric Changes; Lipid Profile

comment_text: Watching from New Zealand - lost 65 lbs switching to keto lifestyle just over a year ago and never going back!  Wasted so much money over roughly 3 decades on weight watchers, jenny craig and so many others…not to mention the expensive wholegrain products and starchy vegetables thar were supposed to be “healthy”.  My family doctor even prescribed some very expensive diet pills that were meant to block fat absorption!  Of course they made me sick and the one month supply at $265 was largely wasted and all but one week worth was thrown out.  Feeling much better these days and even confident enough in my current lifestyle to donate all of my old ‘fat clothes’.  Gone from size 18-20 down to a size 10-12 now and just aiming for normal BMI and just being comfortable in my own skin for the first time in my adult life.

62 yr old female who has been carnivore for just over a year. Keto for 8 yrs before that.  I do daily intermittent fasting for 17 hrs. Went from 220 to 145 on carnivore.  Had a complete physical 3 months ago which included a comprehensive metabolic panel  that I asked for. He knew about a personal loss I had suffered in January 2024 as well. My T2D is gone. My A1C was 5.2. He took me off of Metformin. All of the 10 metabolic tests came back in normal ranges. Blood pressure and cholesterol both so much better he took me off of all meds but 1. Down from 5 to 1 and that one meds mg cut by 2/3's. If other diets aren't working for you, step outside your comfort zone and try carnivore.  Side note: while my dr was happy  with my new metabolic health and T2D being gone,  he had NO INTEREST AT ALL in knowing how I did it.  Told me to keep doing what I was doing. 🤔
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: This rather then begs the question as to why, whenever I’m hospitalised, and so on the enforced “scientific” diet, I become insulin-dependent. At home on my regular low carb diet my fasting glucose is 4 mmol/l and no diabetic drugs. Edit: Located in Tasmania, Australia.

The keto diet is indeed great for body fat loss. I lost 22 kg in 8 months about 5 years ago, coming from 100 kg at 182 cm. I kept my ideal weight ever since, I still avoid sugars and junk food, I quit smoking 15 years ago, my insulin levels are healthy, I still do intermittent fasting and I exercise regularly.  YET, I suffered a heart attack last month. The cardiologists are still investigating my case but the determining factor for my CVA was most likely stress.  I took it as a warning and I'd like to pass it on to the readers. A healthy diet is important and relevant for good heart health plus exercise but don't completely rest on it assuming you're 100% safe. I have the hypothesis that stress is the leading trigger of cardiovascular problems. The fear of underperforming in your job, the fear of competing peers who might leave you in the dust, the fear of losing your job, the fear of social decline, the burden of having to live from paycheck to paycheck etc. All this creates a massive amount of stress and eventually CV disease.  People get sick because there is a systemic disorder.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Cardiovascular Disease; Stroke

comment_text: Been doing Keto and IF for almost 2 years now and I've never felt better! Always had fairly high blood pressure, even as a youngster I would have hypertension and have random nose bleeds but now my BP is completely normal without a single tablet from the doctors (although they did try!). The last 2 years I have taken full responsibility for my health instead of the 'professionals' and it's been an eye-opener for me and completely changed my view on healthcare. They push medication for everything because that's all they know and I could be wrong but maybe they get some sort of remuneration for prescribing pharma products!

Ken, your channel is inspirational. Been subscribed for a while now and you just keep adding more pieces to the puzzle.  In my journey I came across Paul Mason’s videos on cholesterol it completely blew me away. His explanation of LDL was so logical.   Identifying TG/HDL through  a standard blood test and knowing if you’re pattern A or Pattern B is incredible reassuring.  Mine was 0.6mml/l with less than 0.8 being the point at which the split occurs.. My dad having had an aortic aneurysm in his late 60’s this is so reassuring to me.  Lost 10kg in a matter of 2 or 3 months. I have to thank Jason Fung for starting my interest in this whole subject.last summer.  Returned from that holiday and tried a 4 day fast (100 hours in fact) to see what it was like.  Cut out nearly all carbs, all seed oils, only cook in butter, ghee, olive oil or coconut oil. Found out I lose about 3 kg on a four day fast. Went straight to OMAD after that. My wife too benefited from the same dietary changes I’m 5’7 was just under 80kg. Now stable at 70kg. Know exactly how to adjust if I drop too much weight. Have never enjoyed the food I eat so much. If i lose too much i add in 2MAD  never starting before midday and usually just sardines in olive oil and eggs as an omelette.  At 55 years old I have a six pack for the first time in my entire life through no exercise whatsoever save for long walks with our dogs. Thank you for your unwavering dedication to this.will always now be part of your family :-)
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

comment_text: Been doing Keto and IF for almost 2 years now and I've never felt better! Always had fairly high blood pressure, even as a youngster I would have hypertension and have random nose bleeds but now my BP is completely normal without a single tablet from the doctors (although they did try!). The last 2 years I have taken full responsibility for my health instead of the 'professionals' and it's been an eye-opener for me and completely changed my view on healthcare. They push medication for everything because that's all they know and I could be wrong but maybe they get some sort of remuneration for prescribing pharma products!

Ken, your channel is inspirational. Been subscribed for a while now and you just keep adding more pieces to the puzzle.  In my journey I came across Paul Mason’s videos on cholesterol it completely blew me away. His explanation of LDL was so logical.   Identifying TG/HDL through  a standard blood test and knowing if you’re pattern A or Pattern B is incredible reassuring.  Mine was 0.6mml/l with less than 0.8 being the point at which the split occurs.. My dad having had an aortic aneurysm in his late 60’s this is so reassuring to me.  Lost 10kg in a matter of 2 or 3 months. I have to thank Jason Fung for starting my interest in this whole subject.last summer.  Returned from that holiday and tried a 4 day fast (100 hours in fact) to see what it was like.  Cut out nearly all carbs, all seed oils, only cook in butter, ghee, olive oil or coconut oil. Found out I lose about 3 kg on a four day fast. Went straight to OMAD after that. My wife too benefited from the same dietary changes I’m 5’7 was just under 80kg. Now stable at 70kg. Know exactly how to adjust if I drop too much weight. Have never enjoyed the food I eat so much. If i lose too much i add in 2MAD  never starting before midday and usually just sardines in olive oil and eggs as an omelette.  At 55 years old I have a six pack for the first time in my entire life through no exercise whatsoever save for long walks with our dogs. Thank you for your unwavering dedication to this.will always now be part of your family :-)
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

Always had fairly high blood pressure, even as a youngster I would have hypertension and have random nose bleeds but now my BP is completely normal without a single tablet from the doctors (although they did try!). The last 2 years I have taken full responsibility for my health instead of the 'professionals' and it's been an eye-opener for me and completely changed my view on healthcare. They push medication for everything because that's all they know and I could be wrong but maybe they get some sort of remuneration for prescribing pharma products!

Ken, your channel is inspirational. Been subscribed for a while now and you just keep adding more pieces to the puzzle.  In my journey I came across Paul Mason’s videos on cholesterol it completely blew me away. His explanation of LDL was so logical.   Identifying TG/HDL through  a standard blood test and knowing if you’re pattern A or Pattern B is incredible reassuring.  Mine was 0.6mml/l with less than 0.8 being the point at which the split occurs.. My dad having had an aortic aneurysm in his late 60’s this is so reassuring to me.  Lost 10kg in a matter of 2 or 3 months. I have to thank Jason Fung for starting my interest in this whole subject.last summer.  Returned from that holiday and tried a 4 day fast (100 hours in fact) to see what it was like.  Cut out nearly all carbs, all seed oils, only cook in butter, ghee, olive oil or coconut oil. Found out I lose about 3 kg on a four day fast. Went straight to OMAD after that. My wife too benefited from the same dietary changes I’m 5’7 was just under 80kg. Now stable at 70kg. Know exactly how to adjust if I drop too much weight. Have never enjoyed the food I eat so much. If i lose too much i add in 2MAD  never starting before midday and usually just sardines in olive oil and eggs as an omelette.  At 55 years old I have a six pack for the first time in my entire life through no exercise whatsoever save for long walks with our dogs. Thank you for your unwavering dedication to this.will always now be part of your family :-)
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

comment_text: I wish we had more doctors like this in the world!  God Bless you. Ive been keto for 5 years. I lost 65 pounds quickly. I healed EVERYTHING I suffered from for 25 years, all those years I had so many docs, specialist and so many meds just to cover my symptoms.  I was SICK of no one helping me so I started digging on my own. 24/7..  I healed myself with the right foods.  People tell me all the time keto is dangerous, keto can only be done short term.. bla bla bla.  NO, keto is the best thing I have done for my  health. Thank you Dr... for telling the truth.

Dr. Berry I'm in Cleveland Ohio I went to my doctor my A1-C was 8.7 I went back 3 months later 6.5 keto diet and down 37 lbs.  doctor told me to not do keto anymore. Course I'm still doing it.  feel 100 times better
I'm from Calgary Alberta Canada 79 years old lost 100 lbs !!! In the midst of a 72 hour Fast right now !! I eat like you eat

God bless you guys, and thank you sooooo much for this video. I've been doing a deep dive into this topic because, at 66 I am faced with taking a statin. I've been taken off my BP med thank god, but I also want off my statin as well due to side effects such as ED. My cholesterol and BP has run marginally high for my entire adult life. I question the vaule of the pharmological intervention in my case.

62 yr old female who has been carnivore for just over a year. Keto for 8 yrs before that.  I do daily intermittent fasting for 17 hrs. Went from 220 to 145 on carnivore.  Had a complete physical 3 months ago which included a comprehensive metabolic panel  that I asked for. He knew about a personal loss I had suffered in January 2024 as well. My T2D is gone. My A1C was 5.2. He took me off of Metformin. All of the 10 metabolic tests came back in normal ranges. Blood pressure and cholesterol both so much better he took me off of all meds but 1. Down from 5 to 1 and that one meds mg cut by 2/3's. If other diets aren't working for you, step outside your comfort zone and try carnivore.  Side note: while my dr was happy  with my new metabolic health and T2D being gone,  he had NO INTEREST AT ALL in knowing how I did it.  Told me to keep doing what I was doing. 🤔
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: This rather then begs the question as to why, whenever I’m hospitalised, and so on the enforced “scientific” diet, I become insulin-dependent. At home on my regular low carb diet my fasting glucose is 4 mmol/l and no diabetic drugs. Edit: Located in Tasmania, Australia.

The keto diet is indeed great for body fat loss. I lost 22 kg in 8 months about 5 years ago, coming from 100 kg at 182 cm. I kept my ideal weight ever since, I still avoid sugars and junk food, I quit smoking 15 years ago, my insulin levels are healthy, I still do intermittent fasting and I exercise regularly.  YET, I suffered a heart attack last month. The cardiologists are still investigating my case but the determining factor for my CVA was most likely stress.  I took it as a warning and I'd like to pass it on to the readers. A healthy diet is important and relevant for good heart health plus exercise but don't completely rest on it assuming you're 100% safe. I have the hypothesis that stress is the leading trigger of cardiovascular problems. The fear of underperforming in your job, the fear of competing peers who might leave you in the dust, the fear of losing your job, the fear of social decline, the burden of having to live from paycheck to paycheck etc. All this creates a massive amount of stress and eventually CV disease.  People get sick because there is a systemic disorder.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Cardiovascular Disease; Stroke

comment_text: This rather then begs the question as to why, whenever I’m hospitalised, and so on the enforced “scientific” diet, I become insulin-dependent. At home on my regular low carb diet my fasting glucose is 4 mmol/l and no diabetic drugs. Edit: Located in Tasmania, Australia.

The keto diet is indeed great for body fat loss. I lost 22 kg in 8 months about 5 years ago, coming from 100 kg at 182 cm. I kept my ideal weight ever since, I still avoid sugars and junk food, I quit smoking 15 years ago, my insulin levels are healthy, I still do intermittent fasting and I exercise regularly.  YET, I suffered a heart attack last month. The cardiologists are still investigating my case but the determining factor for my CVA was most likely stress.  I took it as a warning and I'd like to pass it on to the readers. A healthy diet is important and relevant for good heart health plus exercise but don't completely rest on it assuming you're 100% safe. I have the hypothesis that stress is the leading trigger of cardiovascular problems. The fear of underperforming in your job, the fear of competing peers who might leave you in the dust, the fear of losing your job, the fear of social decline, the burden of having to live from paycheck to paycheck etc. All this creates a massive amount of stress and eventually CV disease.  People get sick because there is a systemic disorder.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Cardiovascular Disease; Stroke

comment_text: Been doing Keto and IF for almost 2 years now and I've never felt better! Always had fairly high blood pressure, even as a youngster I would have hypertension and have random nose bleeds but now my BP is completely normal without a single tablet from the doctors (although they did try!). The last 2 years I have taken full responsibility for my health instead of the 'professionals' and it's been an eye-opener for me and completely changed my view on healthcare. They push medication for everything because that's all they know and I could be wrong but maybe they get some sort of remuneration for prescribing pharma products!

Ken, your channel is inspirational. Been subscribed for a while now and you just keep adding more pieces to the puzzle.  In my journey I came across Paul Mason’s videos on cholesterol it completely blew me away. His explanation of LDL was so logical.   Identifying TG/HDL through  a standard blood test and knowing if you’re pattern A or Pattern B is incredible reassuring.  Mine was 0.6mml/l with less than 0.8 being the point at which the split occurs.. My dad having had an aortic aneurysm in his late 60’s this is so reassuring to me.  Lost 10kg in a matter of 2 or 3 months. I have to thank Jason Fung for starting my interest in this whole subject.last summer.  Returned from that holiday and tried a 4 day fast (100 hours in fact) to see what it was like.  Cut out nearly all carbs, all seed oils, only cook in butter, ghee, olive oil or coconut oil. Found out I lose about 3 kg on a four day fast. Went straight to OMAD after that. My wife too benefited from the same dietary changes I’m 5’7 was just under 80kg. Now stable at 70kg. Know exactly how to adjust if I drop too much weight. Have never enjoyed the food I eat so much. If i lose too much i add in 2MAD  never starting before midday and usually just sardines in olive oil and eggs as an omelette.  At 55 years old I have a six pack for the first time in my entire life through no exercise whatsoever save for long walks with our dogs. Thank you for your unwavering dedication to this.will always now be part of your family :-)

comment_text: I went through every single stage of illness that Dr. Li went through. I'm year 6 into my healing journey but KNOW that my autoimmune journey started YEARS before I hit rock bottom. I decided to take control of my health and saw major improvements and can now say that I absolutely put my autoimmune disease into remission. It propelled me to spread the message as far and wide as I could. I switched careers and made my life mission to help people get over their thyroid/hashimoto's disease!

I wanted to briefly share how I got my Hashimoto's thyroiditis in remission.  3 years ago I got a infection in pancreas which cause me to be nauseous so I didn't eat for 9 days (basically fasting, however definitely not planned) but tried to keep up my fluid intake.  It just happened that when I recovered I needed to have my labs drawn and my doctor was so surprised because my labs reflected 90% remission of Hashimoto's thyroiditis.  Asked all kinds of questions and once found out I hadn't eaten for 9 days, only had fluids asked if would wait 6 months and do another long fast (5-9 days) and see if we would get it in complete remission.  That is exactly what happened after the second fast and now 3 years later, I am still in remission from Hashimoto's thyroiditis.  :)

I was diagnosed with Ankylosing Spondylitis. They told me it was genetic. I prayed for guidance and the Lord Jesus Christ changed my life. I was compelled to change my diet and do a 40-day detox that I believe healed my gut. I haven't had a flare-up in more or less 10 yrs.

Great interview! ❤ Dealing with MS for about 16.5 years now. But, this year (2023) I found Keto, and now Carnivore. Changed my life significantly! Got all my MS meds, lost 25 lbs so far, tons of energy. And, so much more.

I was given psychiatric meds for losing my ability to sleep due to gastritis and the side effects of ppi’s. Those pills never put me off to sleep. I got no medical recognition and I was about to die with no sleep for almost a month. After a month some clonazepam worked but everything under my skull was inflamed. And possibly bleeding. After two years of searching I found a doctor who injected me with rescuvolin and glutathione plus Mg and vitb6. I started healing. Then stopped gluten and sugar and too GI sustain which healed the inflammation in my stomach. I paid for all the appropriate treatment which I found over the internet. Because medical aid doesn’t pay for functional medicine. I’m focusing on gut healing and learning more about leaky gut. It saved my life and I learned that allopathic doctors are just service people who really don’t want to heal people because there’s no business in it for them.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health

comment_text: I went on a all raw diet ,when I was 43yrs old, within 5 months I was totally healed of bad fibromyalgia that I had for 10yrs,also felt like I was 15yrs old again.

My father had ALS for over 20 years.  He was able to work, walk, dance and have a life for over 20 years until a few weeks after my mother died and all of a sudden he was sick.  My sister and I never knew that he had been diagnosed with ALS and every few years his doctors would have him come back because they thought they had misdiagnosed him - but no matter what he had it but could walk, work and drive. and was happy.  Over the years his speech slowed down gradually which we noticed but assumed it was just slowing some from aging.  In 2015 I had emergency lung surgery because of a large abscess in my lungs.  One of my lungs had deflated because it was flattened by the growth.  A doctor told me I had a mild case of pneumonia and when antibiotics didn’t work he told me to give it more time - they were actually rude about it so I went to an urgent care where an xray showed the lung issues.  SO - I had a complete thoracotomy and afterward was diagnosed with severe sepsis so they put me in a drug induced coma for weeks and put on a cocktail of antibiotics.  After weeks in the ICU and dealing with kidney issues and a brain bleed I was taken to an inpatient rehab facility.  A few days into it C dif hit.  It was like a car hit me.  I was already really ill but the Cdif was awful and they had a difficult time stopping the problem.  Now 5ish years later I have neurological and other issues.  Neurology at NYU Langone did a million expensive tests but offered me nothing as a remedy.  Why do the tests if you have nothing else to offer?  I’ve started to deal with some of the issues using specific Amino Acids which seem to be helping some.  I’m sick of going to a million different doctors who have no answers and just pass me from one doctor to another.  The neurologists have written off the idea that I might have something else going on.  Very frustrated and disgusted with the medical community.  It saved my life but once the crisis was over it had no answers for what I was left with. I currently take Arginine/Ornithine, Taurine, a multi Magnesium and Lemon Balm before i go to sleep and I sleep like a rock and my bp went down 40 pts/20.  The Arginine did what Lisinopril and Norvasc  didn’t do and Brain Awake in the am.  It has LTheanine and Acetyl L Carnitine during the day.  I had recurring bladder and kidney infections and the antibiotics were a short term fix.  I started drinking whole organic milk which us more hydrating than water and the infections stopped.  Skim and lactose free milks aren’t as hydrating and doesn’t cause the problems blamed on Whole milk.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Blood Pressure

I quit dairy from then on and have remained acne free ever since.
I gave up diary , and feel great no more acne, weight gain, no more hormonal fluctuations.

comment_text: I gave up carbs, sugar, and seed oils. Consume mostly dairy, eggs and meat. Dropped a ton of fat, got lean and my joints never felt better.

I've been totally dairy free for over a year. I started because I was breastfeeding my baby who had an allergy to cow's milk protein. I've literally never felt better. I never feel bloated anymore, my skin has never been clearer, I not only lost the baby weight but am now slimmer than I've ever been.  I rarely feel fatigued (even with a baby!), or irritable/crampy. I'm just one case, but I think it's really worth cutting it out for 2 months and just observing the way your body responds!

comment_text: Iv lost weight and no more aches and pains

I stopped eating most dairy (very rarely use parmesan) and most meat (on occasion eat fish). I eat absolutely all I want of the veggies, fruit, nuts, seeds. And even some olive oil on stuff. Went from 190 to 140 in one year.  And I can tell you I totally stuff myself daily with whole foods, so I am not on any kind of reduced food diet. Blood pressure dropped like a rock! Feel great so far after one year. Feel really quick, clear thinking and light, at 59 years old. Read The China Study.

I had the muscular body of my dreams at 54 and I thought to myself, now watch, I'll probably die.  And I almost did.  I spent two months in the ICU with covid, my lung collapsed three times and I was having hallucinations so they put me on a drug for schizophrenia.  My body completely evaporated and looked like I never had any muscles at all.  My veins that once popped out all over my body were impossible to find for the nurses to give me IVs.  I'm 'recovered' now two years later but I have skipped heart beats and it's been hard to sprint like I used to...I get panic attacks because I feel like I can't breathe and I can't seem to get my heart rate up as high as before.  All my blood tests are different like I'm a different person.  I have high cholesterol now and even high blood glucose.  I used to be 12% body fat and now I'm probably 25-30%.  I went from 155 and all muscle to 185.  My veins on my arms started to come back but they look strange and sickly where they were once strong and vibrant and healthy looking.  I get stabbing pains in my arms and legs sometimes that, if they lasted for 10 seconds I woudl certainly pass out from the pain but fortunately they last less than a second and suddenly the pain is gone like it was never there.  I gasp and people have sometimes asked, 'what's wrong' and I just say nothing because it's gone so fast.  No jab, by the way, and happy about it.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

comment_text: I had Covid last Oct. I began to have spells of low b/p and broke my ankle collapsing during a spell. I plan on using my time with broken leg to exercise religiously (turning it into a habit) and make improvements to my diet.

I lost 10kgs in weight  with COVID-19, from 60kgs to 50kgs within one month. My muscles just disappeared. I got pneumonia twice after. comment_text: And 55 and perimenopausal. I had my first blood test in 14 years in September 2023 and discovered that I was extreme type two diabetic. That would have never happened without it.  I have 70 more lbs to lose. I was 5’9” and 125 for years until I took birth control pills. I gained 80 pounds with in  few years. I now realize it was likely that I had undiagnosed PCOS for years. This made weight gain likely. GLP-1s have given me me hope I’ll one day be back in the body I haven’t had for 30 years.  With all the missed opportunities and fat bias I faced, if I have to take these for the rest of my life, I’m all for it.   Also, no, I do not worry about medullary thyroid cancer because I’m not a 🐀  BTW, my %muscle mass is higher in almost a 45 degree trajectory.

Tyna Moore is a chiropractor, not a trained/board certified endocrinologist, neurologist, physiologist, or pharmacist.  Calley Means is on the right track.  I was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes in 2013.  I was 64 years old and weighed 200 lbs.  My primary care physician prescribed Metformin and I refused, making an appointment with an endocrinologist, specializing in Diabetes.  I was put on a medically supervised diet, started walking on a treadmill and lost 75 lbs. in 5 months.  I bought a glucose monitor and tested my blood sugar 5 times a day.   I found that blood pressure lowering medication, caused my blood sugar to rise as high as 10 points.   Carbohydrates raised it even more..  At 5 months post-diagnosis, I had lowered my carb intake to less than 25 mg daily.   All meat & fish were lean and I ate my fill of fresh dark green vegetables.  At 125 lbs., I was able to get off all drugs, except 2.5 mg of Crestor and Synthroid.  My A1C went from over 7 down to 5.2.   I was abled to be disciplined because I wanted my life back.  In 2022, I began having severe degenerative back pain and have gradually regained all the weight when I became sedentary.  I was prescribed Ozempic and continued, even with bothersome side effects, until the supply became scarce and difficult to get.  At age 75, I have made the decision to not resume the Ozempic.  I retire in 4 months and will be free from my 10 hr/day computer job.  I bought a high tech carbon fiber trail “walker” and I will get my life back, for the second time!  🎒🏕️
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Thyroid Disease

I tried a WFPB diet, fasting, dieting, etc. for years still struggled. Started Mounjaro and now Zepbound and I’ve lost 72 lbs in a year and a half. That would have never happened without it.  I have 70 more lbs to lose. I was 5’9” and 125 for years until I took birth control pills. I gained 80 pounds with in  few years. I now realize it was likely that I had undiagnosed PCOS for years. This made weight gain likely. GLP-1s have given me me hope I’ll one day be back in the body I haven’t had for 30 years.  With all the missed opportunities and fat bias I faced, if I have to take these for the rest of my life, I’m all for it.   Also, no, I do not worry about medullary thyroid cancer because I’m not a 🐀  BTW, my %muscle mass is higher in almost a 45 degree trajectory.

Tyna Moore is a chiropractor, not a trained/board certified endocrinologist, neurologist, physiologist, or pharmacist.  Calley Means is on the right track.  I was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes in 2013.  I was 64 years old and weighed 200 lbs.  My primary care physician prescribed Metformin and I refused, making an appointment with an endocrinologist, specializing in Diabetes.  I was put on a medically supervised diet, started walking on a treadmill and lost 75 lbs. in 5 months.  I bought a glucose monitor and tested my blood sugar 5 times a day.   I found that blood pressure lowering medication, caused my blood sugar to rise as high as 10 points.   Carbohydrates raised it even more..  At 5 months post-diagnosis, I had lowered my carb intake to less than 25 mg daily.   All meat & fish were lean and I ate my fill of fresh dark green vegetables.  At 125 lbs., I was able to get off all drugs, except 2.5 mg of Crestor and Synthroid.  My A1C went from over 7 down to 5.2.   I was abled to be disciplined because I wanted my life back.  In 2022, I began having severe degenerative back pain and have gradually regained all the weight when I became sedentary.  I was prescribed Ozempic and continued, even with bothersome side effects, until the supply became scarce and difficult to get.  At age 75, I have made the decision to not resume the Ozempic.  I retire in 4 months and will be free from my 10 hr/day computer job.  I bought a high tech carbon fiber trail “walker” and I will get my life back, for the second time!  🎒🏕️
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Thyroid Disease

I am deadly allergic to gluten. I was so sick not knowing, lost 70 pounds down to 99 pounds. I thought I was dying. I started researching and learned it was gluten. The doctors couldn’t help and acted as if they didn’t know about gluten for a year while ignorantly, I continued to suffer. I prayed and finally was let to research my symptoms until I came across IBS. From there I researched that leading to allergies then came across the info on gluten. From there I began to connect it was an allergy. I healed myself without the doctors help. I used natural herbs to heal my upper and lower intestine and started eating organic gluten free food. Its sucks in taste and texture but such as it is for the sake of living a free life from agony and a slow death!

I have a leaky gut for 6yrs, no end of problems that lead to chronic fatigue I felt I didn't want to live anymore but a Christian friend of mine put me onto Kefir within four days I had the most energy I have had for many years also no more bloating, cramps fatigue, e.t.c. I do know now any food with artificial sugars send me into almost instant cramping pain. Four weeks now and life is brilliant. I make it my self and drink around a pint or Litre a day it's worth more than Gold to me. Look it up even buy from some shops organic is best, the doctors will only give meds that are poison and won't work. GOD BLESS ALL.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health

comment_text: These are all the facts I need.  After age 50 Facts:  Bad hair loss -- really bad.  Bad case of Rosacea to the point my face felt like it was on fire.  Bad Eczema; paper thin fingernails; painful arthritis; no energy; often sickly during winter; constant back pain to the point I was living off of icy/hot patches and Baer Back and Body; bloating; poor digestion both going in and coming out.   After one year off of wheat -- absolutely no wheat or wheat byproduct for one full year.  Cold Turkey.  Hardest thing I ever did.  Hair started growing back -- after three years the hair is thick and full like when I was younger.  Rosacea completely gone -- nothing -- no red no itch no burn -- nothing.  No more Eczema -- not even dandruff.  Fingernails are thick again.  The arthritis -- in one hip and one shoulder -- pain completely gone.  Nothing.  The inflammation literally non-existent.  No more back pain and I am a stocker at a big box store lifting heavy boxes all night 5 nights a week.  Full of energy now -- back to walking and moving at my normal quick pace.  Been off of wheat for 3 full years now and no winter colds for those three years.  No more bloating and digestion is normal -- both ways.  These are all detectable effects that have happened since I completely cut wheat out of my diet.  And wheat was the only change I made to the diet; so I know wheat was the culprit.  I was told, when I started -- warned actually -- that if I chose to walk away from wheat and I truly had an allergy to it; I would never be able to eat it again.  The body would not allow it.  I did not believe that until I accidentally ate something that had a wheat filler in it.  In less than 30 minutes (which is how long it takes food to leave the stomach and hit the intestines) literally what I ate was projectile vomited right out of me.  Sorry for being graphic but it is what happened.  Leading up to the projectile vomiting, I got extremely weak, could hardly walk to the point that I needed help getting to the bath.  It felt like I was going to pass out.  I was told I got white like a sheet, and the people with me were really concerned.  Once I vomited, and it was out of my body My strength and color came back and I was fine.  Gluten intolerance is absolutely real and it can kick in at any age.  I look back over all the years (I am 60 now) and I can see all the signals my body was using to warn me of the effects wheat (gluten) was having on my body, but I was ignorant to the signs.  But I realize how much discomfort I could have avoided had I only known.  Hind site is always 20/20.  It was a real challenge to walk away from wheat (especially bread) but I did it and it payed off.  Now my goal is to cut back on process sugar.  That is really hard, but it is happening.  Things I used to crave like ice cream and chocolate are loosing their appeal on me.  When I can go to the store and stare at the freezer of ice cream and walk away because nothing appeals to me -- I know I am kicking the addiction.  It's been harder kicking the sugar addiction, than the wheat habit, but it is happening.  We are what we eat -- that is the most truest statement that was ever mumbled by humans.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Digestive Health; Skin Health; Arthritis

comment_text: It was, we just didn't know it. My grandmother had celiac disease, never diagnosed. Why do I think she had it? She had gut problems -- gas and bloating, and probably diarrhea -- and someone in her bridge group recommended a doctor, who was a GP pretending to be a surgeon. She died on his table, at age about 36 with 5 children aged 5 to 15.  My mother would not leave the house from middle age on without diarrhea medicine. Just before she died, she came to stay with me -- and she had lost maybe 30 or 40 pounds, was stupid, couldn't function, and eventually went into a coma, and they diagnosed her with Wernicke's encephalopathy. There are three causes for that, one of which is celiac disease. They did not mention that; they just said she didn't eat well because she was elderly. Too bad for me! Because I was retired early as disabled after I lost about 40 pounds and got utterly stupid, trembled all the time, just like my mother. If I had had any idea what was wrong with me, I would not be so poor now, as I did not get my complete pension.  And I continued to lose weight, got down to maybe 85 pounds, trying really hard not to lose weight, eating 4000 calories a day. And I had weighed around 145. Maybe 150. At least third generation celiac disease.
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Autoimmune Disease

comment_text: I can't get my allergist to think past my symptoms. Started after allergy shots and my horrible insomnia. Dr took me off monolukast and food sensitivites skyrocketed since. I started my own research online and my symptoms. I have eliminated all high histamine and histamine releasing foods for 6 weeks has improved, inflammation is gone, and digestion is better. Presented with anxiety, palpitations, temp regulation, and digestion issues. Got specially formulated probiotics and taking Quercertin, D3, Vit C, zinc, magnesium, selenium, B6, flaxseed oil, turmeric, and L-glutimine. Gastro going to run tests on gut again. Going to eliminate my phone time. Try sleeping more.  Have lost 28 lbs which is great because I needed it but the food sensitivites and side effects are still tough.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I moved to Germany from the USA and in the first year I lost 80 pounds of fat without even trying. America's obesity epidemic = the continual poisoning of US citizens' food supply.
I started eating only unprocessed food as much as possible.   My blood pressure went from 146 to 116 in six months.

I eat a 6 egg omelet for lunch every day. And a nice juicy steak for dinner. Everything cooked in butter (from raw milk, I live in Europe, it's legal here). I feel fantastic! Migraines gone, depression gone, anxiety gone, I have mental clarity and energy again, I feel like I'm in my 20s again (I'm 55).
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation

comment_text: Nope. I didn't "select" your video thumbnail, I'm subscribed and this popped up in my feed. I have been considering an "egg fast" to jump start my diet. I lost over 200 pounds, then I moved and all of my usual routines and healthy habits were disrupted. I put on a few pounds, so I need to take them off again. {sigh} It's a never-ending battle for me. I'm definitely NOT a 'lean body hyper-responder," but I still find your content highly educational. Keep up the good work!

10 years ago i was told by my doctor that i had high cholesterol and that i needed to go on meds and had to start eating healthier. He asked if i ate eggs and he asked that i stop since i consumed 4-6 of them daily. I listened to the doc and stoped eating eggs and went on meds. For an entire year i saw small balance in my cholesterol and they kept increasing my meds. To make a long story short i stopped taking meds, starting eating 4-8 eggs a day again, and my cholesterol finally started getting better withour meds. What did i do? I stopped eating too much carbs, specially stuff like bread and pasta and alsk exercised more often.

I am a 52-year-old man, 184 cm tall, weighing 77 kg, a non-smoker, drink little to no alcohol, and am athletic. Unfortunately, a small plaque (1.3 mm) was found in my right carotid artery a little over a year ago. In March, my LDL cholesterol was 147. At the end of August, I started intermittent fasting, keto, and low carb independently of this, and I have been doing regular ergometer cardio training for a good two years. I lost a good 5 kg last month, even though I was already quite slim. Unfortunately, my LDL is higher than ever due to keto: 204! I have my next blood test in two months. I am now cutting out eggs, bacon, butter, and meat again, but I am sticking to intermittent fasting and low carb with fish, olive oil, avocados, etc. I hope I can reduce my LDL again, because my HDL has improved, as have my triglycerides. My internist is already talking about statins, but I don't think much of them. I'm also cutting out red rice for now and will increase my nattokinase dose from the current 2,800 FU to 8,000 FU. Do you have any other ideas or tips? Many thanks and best regards from Austria. 🇦🇹🙏🏼🫶🏼❤️
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

I am a 52-year-old man, 184 cm tall, weighing 77 kg, a non-smoker, drink little to no alcohol, and am athletic. Unfortunately, a small plaque (1.3 mm) was found in my right carotid artery a little over a year ago. In March, my LDL cholesterol was 147. I lost a good 5 kg last month, even though I was already quite slim. Unfortunately, my LDL is higher than ever due to keto: 204! I have my next blood test in two months. I am now cutting out eggs, bacon, butter, and meat again, but I am sticking to intermittent fasting and low carb with fish, olive oil, avocados, etc. I hope I can reduce my LDL again, because my HDL has improved, as have my triglycerides. My internist is already talking about statins, but I don't think much of them. I'm also cutting out red rice for now and will increase my nattokinase dose from the current 2,800 FU to 8,000 FU. Do you have any other ideas or tips? Many thanks and best regards from Austria. 🇦🇹🙏🏼🫶🏼❤️

comment_text: I just came across your video. I am that classic lean mass hyper- responder. Last September at my doctor's visit, she was so upset because my cholesterol was off the charts, but my HDL/LDL ratio was great and so was triglycerides. Real question is,  why try to lower cholesterol? It has been demonised by people who have an interest in pointing the blame away from sugar, carbs, processed foods. Im carnivore for 7mths. Lost 70lbs, cured every chronic illness I had from arthritis to cardiovascular illness. Ive kicked my meds, first to go was statins. At 63 i cant remember being this healthy ever. My bloodwork is pristine, although my ldl is elevated (i dont care). My total calcium score, the best indicator of cardiovascular health, returns at 0.

I have a friend who was put on l*****r due to his blood chol. results.
