I'm a keto coach and I decided to try the sugar diet/sugar fasting and I can tell you that it does work.  I lost 7 pounds in 7 days and documented my entire experience on my channel by weighing myself everyday, workout performance and weight loss. However, I'm metabolically healthy already.  I just used it as a test and wanted to see if I could tighten up by losing 10 pounds.  Am I going to do it long term...no but I now know that you can use it as a tool.  Also one thing I want to point out is that this diet isn't eating a bunch of candy bars, donuts, cakes, etc.  It's basically, fruit, smoothies, maple syrup, honey and the occasional candy that has 0 fat.

@13:35 right here, take this and talk to LillieKane and her partner. He has mentioned in order to LOSE the LAST bits of his belly fat and get ripped, he had to ADD CARBS to carnivore.  I was looking at him, he is male, healthy, mid20s, already sub 20% body fat. It seems to fit.  Meanwhile I have lost 200 lb carnivore, but still at 35% bodyfat. Yeah this is not for me. I'll try it when i hit 200 lb. BBL.

I have been on a keto/carnivore diet for over 5 years with great results. I tried this diet for 6 days with fruit and honey instead of sugar. I lost 4 pounds of fat and maintained my lean mass. I am 65 years old, 149lbs and 10.6% body fat according to my In Body 570 scale.

I just posted a short video on my blood glucose levels after eating candy. I am not a proponent of eating candy but just wanted to see what would happen. I had a regular spike like you see with eating a carb food. After about an hour my blood sugar went down to 54. I have been on the diet for an about a week and have lost 10lbs.  My blood glucose average has gone down 20 points in one week on the diet. My blood glucose is now staying between 60-90 while I am losing 1-2lbs/day. This diet has to be cycled. An animal study showed that long term, the diet caused diabetes. The diet also lowers testosterone levels. However, these issues can be resolved by doing the diet the right way.

comment_text: My energy was awful while keto. Felt like I was running on motor oil instead of gasoline. I feel like I have that ‘get up and go’ type of energy on the sugar diet. So far, sugar diet feels more sustainable for me. I have about 30 more pounds to lose. I’ve lost 9 pounds in just under 3 weeks on sugar.

Sugar fasting is a game changer for weight loss when Keto and carnivore fail. Which it does. The entire keto space has overnight become black mirror vegans  screaming about glycation instead of arteriosclerosis. I'd say the most surprising thing about this diet besides weight loss is actually more stable blood sugars and the complete lack of food noise and cravings.

comment_text: I'm 61, and have been on the carnivore lifestyle for 15 months. Within 10 days, I had completely eliminated my IBS. Within 30 days, my joints (knees, mostly) stopped aching. In addition, I lost over 25 lbs, felt less anxious, and started sleeping better. I'll never go back. Never.

comment_text: Stopped fiber decades ago. Low carb for decades. Keto for 7-8 years. CARNIVORE for 25 months. NEVER felt better, been leaner, been stronger, looked better, or gotten hit on as much by both sexes as today. My N of 1 assessment: Carnivore is ABSOLUTELY the optimum eating practice for my global wellbeing and health (and I suspect for all human beings). I dare anyone to try carnivore for 6 months, long enough to be fully fat-adapted regardless of their starting point, and SEE if they don't improve every aspect of their health. My contribution to humanity for today. Cheers!    :)

I have struggled with obesity my entire 30 years of life. Ive tried everything from exercise/gym, dieting, a combination of both.. Eating what i thought was "healthy" foods.. I was introduced to the Carnivore diet late 2023 early 2024 when i saw my brother embark on that journey and lose over 100 pounds (he's lost 120 on carnivore and is now bulking trying to gain weight in the gym LOL). On march 27th, 2024 i began my journey with the carnivore diet. The results are just unbelievable. Literally unbelievable. I started out at 288 (though a few months prior i was 294-296, so pushing 300 pounds. Ill note that during this journey ive only exercised maybe a total of 3 months, so majority of weight loss has been my diet change and fasting as well. The other day on my 10 month anniversary, i scaled at 198. Writing this i am still in awe. I have not been below 200 pounds in 15+ years - since i was a freshman in highschool. This has been a life changer. Carnivore has saved my life. Ive completely changed my relationship with food and have listened to some of the 🐐's such as Berry, Chaffee, Saladino, Baker.. and more recently Nick here. Learning everything that i have (things that go against what i have previously learned) and applying it to my everyday life has caused a complete 360° turn around in my life. I truly hope anyone that reads this and is thinking about starting Carnivore is inspired. The best time to start is TODAY.

I've been carnivore for 2 1/2 years. On this diet I have put 7 autoimmune diseases into drug free remission including Myasthenia Gravis and osteoarthritis. At 6 months my bipolar disorder symptoms dissapeared completely with no bipolar incidents since then. I've also lost 250 pounds so far. Many other things have healed as well. I would venture to say there are tens of thousands of us healing on this diet. The slowest thing to heal for me was gut issues. It took about two years to heal my leaky gut and repair damage left behind by a medication caled Mestinon. It wrecked my gut.  I was in a wheelchair for 15 years due to myasthenia gravis. At exactly one month carnivore my neurologist declared me in full remission and took me off all autoimmune medications. Wheelchair gone, accessible apartment long gone. So far, no heart problems or scurvy lol.
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Cardiovascular Disease; Autoimmune Disease; Arthritis

comment_text: Carnivore diet broke my sugar addiction, and binge eating habits. Ive been a sugar addict since i was a little girl, I ate lots of fast food during my college years and early 20s. Pandemic made me gain 12kg and a wosw than ever eating habit. I discovered keto on 2023, tried it, yoyo-d, lost weight, but cravings for dessert was still there due to keto desserts. Fell off the wagon at the end of 2023, and got frustrated, and found carnivore.. i thought the diet would suit me to help with my carb addiction. Feb 2024 i started carnivore cold turkey, and stuck strict for months, lost all the weight, got amazing energy, eczema controlled, sugar cravings gone. Diet got ruined at family vacay around july, and fell off again. Decided to get back up around the end off november, and stuck with it since. Never going back. I just turned 31. I have a lot of things to say about the carnivore diet. And i think given my relationship with food, i cant afford to go back.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Appetite & Satiety; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Just over a year on the Carnivore Diet and my Crohn's Disease is gone, my GERD is gone, my migraines are gone.  This might only be anecdotal according to scientists, but it means the whole world for me!  I recommend to everyone just to give it a go for a month, and see how they feel.

I am 51 years old and have struggled with weight and inflammation since my mid 30's. I have been successful in many different diets and was able to lose weight. Ultimately I would end up falling  back into the standard American diet due to the fact that although I lost weight, I still felt terrible in many other ways. Some mental health, but mostly inflammation and other issues. It wasn't until I started Carnivore that everything changed. I thought it was crazy at first, but at my age what did I have to lose. I was on the verge of 50, and actually felt like I was going to die young. I was severely over weight and struggled to even move around. I lost 50 lbs. in my first 90 days and felt like I was 25 again, it was a game changer! It changed my ways of thinking entirely, and I decided to get all of my bad habits in check. One being my oral health, I had not been to a dentist in 20+ years. Unfortunately I needed surgery and a lot of work which caused me to fall out of carnivore due to not being able to sustain the diet after my surgeries. I gained some of my weight and mental fog back, but I am going strong again and weight is dropping, and I'm beginning to feel the benefits again, but this time with a healthy mouth and all my teeth fixed. I'm not sure how sustainable it will be for the months or years to come, but feeling the way I do, I can't imagine ever going back to my old ways of eating.  I am hopeful that carnivore will get a fair shake at some point with some real research. It will be an uphill battle with all of the money the big food industry stands to lose.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Great explanation! I've been on this woe for about 5 years, it put my rhumatoid arthritis in remission with minimal medication (no need for chemotherapy). I gave me back my life. Thanks for explaning it that well!

I've been on the carnivore diet for a year now. Don't plan on coming off. I've tried, keto (and multiple variations), IF, vertical diet, moderate carb whole food based, paleo, iifym, etc. This is by far the best I've felt and it has cleared all health issues and also pains from college soccer and my scoliosis. One of the best things to come from it is not ingesting any fiber which gives me perfect bowel movements and no pain. I also don't go number 2 about 3-5 times a day like I used to. My mother went on this diet and got her yearly blood test. After 8 months strict her blood test results were basically perfect. She's lost 40lbs. All health issues cleared up. Her triglycerides were some of the lowest the doctor has seen. Compared to here past several yearly blood tests she looks like a new human, even though she was considered fairly healthy prior from her blood tests.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Digestive Health; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

comment_text: Been mostly on carnivore diet since May 2024, dropped 30kg in 4+ months (combining with IF/ADF) and been keeping it off ever since. Pain in my back and knee gone - regular headaches gone, "pot belly" gone. Sugar cravings, which was horrible ( tub of ice-cream(often 2 in a day), full bar of chocolate in 1 sitting) completely gone. I do eat fruits and one-off veggies time to time( which is mostly pickles ) and sometimes honey. Regular in the gym, stronger than ever and feeling 5-10 years younger again ( in the bed I feel like I'm 20 again! ). Eating mostly meat ( duck, chicken, beef, pork ) cooking only with butter, and about 30 eggs per week in various forms. Cheese, milk and diary no problem and I still do drink coffee with milk (no syrups or sugar). Most importantly: I feel like I can continue like this without much sacrifice & even saving money eating like this.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I'm ending 8 months of carnivore today and transitioning to a more typical keto. Just ordered your book, The New Mediterranean Diet. I was diagnosed with UC pancolitis in 2012 and used prednisone then. Since then the only pharmaceutical I've used is mesalamine (which I'm okay with). I flared last May (I was eating a normal diet then) and started the carnivore diet soon after. Fecal calprotectin went from 529 ug/g -> 3160 -> 1250 -> 360 -> 145 -> 62 (on Jan 22). It took a while but I'm in remission again (and my symptoms were pretty well controlled since June despite FCP remaining high). I did escalate mesalamine dosage, but did not use steroids. I've done a number of dietary interventions from the very beginning (starting with the specific carbohydrate diet). Looking at how consistently I've seen results, i find it pretty hard to explain away as coincidence.

I'm 69 years old and into the third year of paleo journey. First it's an elimination diet, then the hormones get reset.  Then 🧠 fog goes away, 🏋 and  HIIT are part of MY routine.  No Rxs or aches and pains... It changed my life. 💪 Crazy thing is my disabled veteran neighbors  have no interest in getting better. WTF!!😢
I’m very satisfied with my carnivore diet, 77lb down

5 years carnivore now. I fall off the wagon get run over by the wagon following every so often but it’s easy to get back on again. Down 40 lbs and many health problems and aches and pains gone. Age 50 🥩

comment_text: Those of us fixing ourselves don't need science to approve or prove we are right. I had chronic sinusitis for 35 years. ENT, operations, painkillers every 5-7 days. With the medical system just passing me around I went on carnivore (Thanks to Dr ChatGPT), and amazingly, 6 week after been in ER unable to sleep for day and not a full night in weeks, everything turned around. I'm now 12 weeks in. 8kg lighter and not a single sinus headache in 12 weeks... AFTER 35 YEARS! For me, something in a modern diet was the source I removed. Carnivore didn't "fix" me, it just excluded whatever the problem was. Paleo 'partly' helped a few years ago and I never really worked out why it wasn't fully successful. I won't guess here, because I'm 'fixed' finally.

I also had a heart attack at the end of that year. Now recovering from triple bypass surgery. Nice! I will say, my acid reflux went away also.

comment_text: I tried keto with eating 90% meat & eggs for a year. Initial results were amazing, lost 10kg became lean and mentally clear. But ended up with gout due to high uric acid from all the red meat. My kidney function also went down  so now I eat a clean normal diet with intermittent fasting.

Down 50lbs. Lower back and knees no longer sore...I'm running around with my great grand daughter like I'm the little kid. Haven't felt like this since my 30's when I played tennis every day. Blood work is great, no meds. Carnivore for just over 2 years....no veggies, no fruit. I'll never go back. Nothing to debunk...It works.

I am keto and the carnivore diet is a subset of a ketogenic diet. I eat less than 20 total carbs a day, not net carbs but total so as you can imagine I’m pretty close to carnivore. I lost 88 pounds., got my blood pressure under control. Got my irritable, bowel syndrome under control., and I have a lot more energy. However, I do have very high LDL and I haven’t made my mind up on how big a deal this is.. also my fasting blood sugar is low and so is my insulin, I have them measured. I still occasionally., maybe once every three weeks have one bowel movement that is explosive diarrhea. Can’t find a pattern what causes it. But before I went keto, it was basically every day so you can see how improved my health is…Oh, my sleep has improved and before I went keto and lost weight I was starting to get some tingling in my hands and legs, and that has went away.  It’s hard to say if it’s all keto or it’s my weight loss or my exercise routine because I do exercise now I didn’t originally .
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: For me, the first off warning was that everyone dives into just beef and bacon. Which I don't. Mix it up. I eat a can of sardines every day. 2 of oysters a week. and 2 eggs a day, sometimes more. One full rotisserie chicken a week. Some occasional liver. Burgers and steaks, sure, but also sausages and pork. All that is still "carnivore" but you get a much larger micro nutrient profile. Second, I season. not just salt. Hot pepper, hot sauce, mustard. One lemon of juice per day, and the zest, too. Occasionally some pickled jalapenos. Fresh herbs from the garden in small amounts, chives, dill, basil, parsley, thyme. The flavor alone makes sticking to the diet much easier. And it solves the vitamin C problem. But the real secret to vitamin C is tea. Specifically, rose hip tea, but you can put a little lemon juice into any tea. Third, I give it a rest. 3 weeks on, one week off. I go heavy on fiber and produce on off weeks, and still fairly low carb makes getting back on much easier. But beans, fruit and veggies. Bell peppers, cucumbers, spinach, apples, citrus, celery. One week off, 3 on.  I went from 295 to 205 pounds, and feel amazing, more than any 60 your old guy has a right to.
In a year, I lost 55 pounds and 100% reversed my fatty liver so for me the carnivore diet is awesome

comment_text: i never thought that losing weight would be so easy, i lost 30 pounds in 2 months with this diet. i felt low energy for a week and once my body really switched on fat burning, everything was fine.skin got softer like if i got younger. fatty beef, beacon, eggs, salmond, butter,salt and black coffee . most of the days i only eat once.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I tried keto with eating 90% meat & eggs for a year. Initial results were amazing, lost 10kg became lean and mentally clear. But ended up with gout due to high uric acid from all the red meat. My kidney function also went down  so now I eat a clean normal diet with intermittent fasting.

Down 50lbs. Lower back and knees no longer sore...I'm running around with my great grand daughter like I'm the little kid. Haven't felt like this since my 30's when I played tennis every day. Blood work is great, no meds. Carnivore for just over 2 years....no veggies, no fruit. I'll never go back. Nothing to debunk...It works.

I am keto and the carnivore diet is a subset of a ketogenic diet. I eat less than 20 total carbs a day, not net carbs but total so as you can imagine I’m pretty close to carnivore. I lost 88 pounds., got my blood pressure under control. Got my irritable, bowel syndrome under control., and I have a lot more energy. However, I do have very high LDL and I haven’t made my mind up on how big a deal this is.. also my fasting blood sugar is low and so is my insulin, I have them measured. I still occasionally., maybe once every three weeks have one bowel movement that is explosive diarrhea. Can’t find a pattern what causes it. But before I went keto, it was basically every day so you can see how improved my health is…Oh, my sleep has improved and before I went keto and lost weight I was starting to get some tingling in my hands and legs, and that has went away.  It’s hard to say if it’s all keto or it’s my weight loss or my exercise routine because I do exercise now I didn’t originally .
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: For me, the first off warning was that everyone dives into just beef and bacon. Which I don't. Mix it up. I eat a can of sardines every day. 2 of oysters a week. and 2 eggs a day, sometimes more. One full rotisserie chicken a week. Some occasional liver. Burgers and steaks, sure, but also sausages and pork. All that is still "carnivore" but you get a much larger micro nutrient profile. Second, I season. not just salt. Hot pepper, hot sauce, mustard. One lemon of juice per day, and the zest, too. Occasionally some pickled jalapenos. Fresh herbs from the garden in small amounts, chives, dill, basil, parsley, thyme. The flavor alone makes sticking to the diet much easier. And it solves the vitamin C problem. But the real secret to vitamin C is tea. Specifically, rose hip tea, but you can put a little lemon juice into any tea. Third, I give it a rest. 3 weeks on, one week off. I go heavy on fiber and produce on off weeks, and still fairly low carb makes getting back on much easier. But beans, fruit and veggies. Bell peppers, cucumbers, spinach, apples, citrus, celery. One week off, 3 on.  I went from 295 to 205 pounds, and feel amazing, more than any 60 your old guy has a right to.
In a year, I lost 55 pounds and 100% reversed my fatty liver so for me the carnivore diet is awesome

I have lost 50 lb in 3 months going strict carnivore. It completely healed my fatty liver, IBS, and inflammation in an old knee injury. Eat what you want, but I’ll continue taking my chances with a diet that has made me feel like I’m in my 20s again. 💪😎
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Fatty Liver Disease; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I've lost 60 pounds, kicked alcoholism, sugar cravings, snoring and brain fog. I'm 50 and I've never felt better. I'm jacked and look better than when I was 21.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Those of us fixing ourselves don't need science to approve or prove we are right. I had chronic sinusitis for 35 years. ENT, operations, painkillers every 5-7 days. With the medical system just passing me around I went on carnivore (Thanks to Dr ChatGPT), and amazingly, 6 week after been in ER unable to sleep for day and not a full night in weeks, everything turned around. I'm now 12 weeks in. 8kg lighter and not a single sinus headache in 12 weeks... AFTER 35 YEARS! For me, something in a modern diet was the source I removed. Carnivore didn't "fix" me, it just excluded whatever the problem was. Paleo 'partly' helped a few years ago and I never really worked out why it wasn't fully successful. I won't guess here, because I'm 'fixed' finally.

I did the carnivore diet for one year. Cholesterol levels sky rocketed. I did lose weight, I lost 30lb's. I was only 25lb's over what my Dr. wanted me at which is 160lb's.  I also had a heart attack at the end of that year. Now recovering from triple bypass surgery. Nice! I will say, my acid reflux went away also.
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: I tried keto with eating 90% meat & eggs for a year. Initial results were amazing, lost 10kg became lean and mentally clear. But ended up with gout due to high uric acid from all the red meat. My kidney function also went down  so now I eat a clean normal diet with intermittent fasting.

Down 50lbs. Lower back and knees no longer sore...I'm running around with my great grand daughter like I'm the little kid. Haven't felt like this since my 30's when I played tennis every day. Blood work is great, no meds. Carnivore for just over 2 years....no veggies, no fruit. I'll never go back. Nothing to debunk...It works.

I am keto and the carnivore diet is a subset of a ketogenic diet. I eat less than 20 total carbs a day, not net carbs but total so as you can imagine I’m pretty close to carnivore. I lost 88 pounds., got my blood pressure under control. Got my irritable, bowel syndrome under control., and I have a lot more energy. However, I do have very high LDL and I haven’t made my mind up on how big a deal this is.. also my fasting blood sugar is low and so is my insulin, I have them measured. I still occasionally., maybe once every three weeks have one bowel movement that is explosive diarrhea. Can’t find a pattern what causes it. But before I went keto, it was basically every day so you can see how improved my health is…Oh, my sleep has improved and before I went keto and lost weight I was starting to get some tingling in my hands and legs, and that has went away.  It’s hard to say if it’s all keto or it’s my weight loss or my exercise routine because I do exercise now I didn’t originally .
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: For me, the first off warning was that everyone dives into just beef and bacon. Which I don't. Mix it up. I eat a can of sardines every day. 2 of oysters a week. and 2 eggs a day, sometimes more. One full rotisserie chicken a week. Some occasional liver. Burgers and steaks, sure, but also sausages and pork. All that is still "carnivore" but you get a much larger micro nutrient profile. Second, I season. not just salt. Hot pepper, hot sauce, mustard. One lemon of juice per day, and the zest, too. Occasionally some pickled jalapenos. Fresh herbs from the garden in small amounts, chives, dill, basil, parsley, thyme. The flavor alone makes sticking to the diet much easier. And it solves the vitamin C problem. But the real secret to vitamin C is tea. Specifically, rose hip tea, but you can put a little lemon juice into any tea. Third, I give it a rest. 3 weeks on, one week off. I go heavy on fiber and produce on off weeks, and still fairly low carb makes getting back on much easier. But beans, fruit and veggies. Bell peppers, cucumbers, spinach, apples, citrus, celery. One week off, 3 on.  I went from 295 to 205 pounds, and feel amazing, more than any 60 your old guy has a right to.
In a year, I lost 55 pounds and 100% reversed my fatty liver so for me the carnivore diet is awesome

I have lost 50 lb in 3 months going strict carnivore. It completely healed my fatty liver, IBS, and inflammation in an old knee injury. Eat what you want, but I’ll continue taking my chances with a diet that has made me feel like I’m in my 20s again. 💪😎
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Fatty Liver Disease; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Those of us fixing ourselves don't need science to approve or prove we are right. I had chronic sinusitis for 35 years. ENT, operations, painkillers every 5-7 days. With the medical system just passing me around I went on carnivore (Thanks to Dr ChatGPT), and amazingly, 6 week after been in ER unable to sleep for day and not a full night in weeks, everything turned around. I'm now 12 weeks in. 8kg lighter and not a single sinus headache in 12 weeks... AFTER 35 YEARS! For me, something in a modern diet was the source I removed. Carnivore didn't "fix" me, it just excluded whatever the problem was. Paleo 'partly' helped a few years ago and I never really worked out why it wasn't fully successful. I won't guess here, because I'm 'fixed' finally.

I did the carnivore diet for one year. Cholesterol levels sky rocketed. I did lose weight, I lost 30lb's. I was only 25lb's over what my Dr. wanted me at which is 160lb's.  I also had a heart attack at the end of that year. Now recovering from triple bypass surgery. Nice! I will say, my acid reflux went away also.

Singularly, the most AMAZING discovery in theoretical anti aging evidence in a LONG time. Imagine what this could mean to those suffering from chronic auto immune diseases. Would LOVE to see rigorous testing on patients with latent Lyme disease profiles. "COULD" be a game changer!

The timing of this video is excellent! I solved years of gut issues by supplementing with inulin powder. I tried so many things including l reuteri yoghurt, different fibres, vitamin supplements etc but it was the inulin that made the positive changes. When I first started the inulin, it was very obvious that my microbiome was not accustomed to it and I suffered some embarrassing symptoms for a few weeks, but i pushed through, the symptoms subsided and my guts have never felt so good! I now supplement with a bigger range of different fibres in an attempt to broaden my microbiome range and have zero issues processing fibre. Years ago, I couldn't stand taking fibres.

I did keto for 6 months and my LDL went sky high. My total was over 10. But my HDL was also high and triglycerides low. HDL:Trig ratio was 0.5. My GP freaked out about my LDL until I pointed out my other numbers. Not only had my HDL and triglycerides improved a lot from my previous results, but other biomarkers were also much better. He calmed down, and I started eating carbs again and my LDL went down. However, my triglycerides went up and my total is still high with good ratios. HDL/LDL is now 3.2 even though my LDL is over 5. My BMI is 23, my blood pressure is usually around 100/68 and resting pulse around 60. I'm not worried. But my GP is very confused and focused on that LDL number. It's like it's the only thing that matters out of 30 different numbers.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Lipid Profile

comment_text: I am a 76 yo woman. I started a ketogenic diet in 2015. I had a history of high LDL cholesterol. I lost 60 lbs. I am still not lean but am about 5’9” and 170 lbs. since going keto/carnivore, my LDL has gone to over 300. My TG and HDL are good. I had CAC scans in 2019 and 2022 and they were 0 both times. I was on statins in my 40’s-50’s but could not tolerate them. I use my CAC scans to keep doctors off my back about taking statins.

As 49 year old male, at approx 13% bodyfat, who has been on a low-carb diet for nearly 30 years, before it was such a "thing".  After watching you videos on lean mass hyper-responders, I decided to re-introduce carbs into my diet for 3 months, having extensive blood labs taken immediately before and after.  After just under 3 months my total cholesterol dropped from 285 to 182 and my LDL dropped from 202 to 120.  Although my labs improved, I felt much more lethargic and gained about 10 unwanted pounds.  I am currently back on my low carb diet.  I already feel more energetic and am starting to lose some of the bodyfat.  That being said, I will likely do short 1 month cycles of light carb re-introduction every few months.  I am hoping to get the best of both worlds.  Thank you for this very important work.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Anthropometric Changes; Lipid Profile

comment_text: Keep up the great work! I'm not sure I'm a LMHR, but listening to these discussions over the last few years helped me with my Dr. I'm 61, lost 85 pounds over 2 years and eat a low carb diet. My LDL-C at last visit was 267. After push back from me, Dr. finally ordered a CAC. This came back a ZERO. I would be on statins if it hadn't been for Dave and his further digging and making his discoveries and discussions available on the internet. I appreciate you all staying curious and so generously sharing your N=1 results that eventually turned into this research! 🎉

I ran across your info 2-1/2 years ago when I went carnivore and I have been following ever since. As I got better and off medications, my LDL was the only lab off. I would have been crazy from the doctors insisting on a statin if I hadn't heard of the lean mass hyperesponder, which fit me perfectly. Cardiologist refused to order a CAC but a few months ago, a new PCP did order it. It was ZERO! I'm 69 and now the doctors shake their head but leave me alone about my diet. Thank you for doing this study. I'll be sharing it.

This is Sue ,Rick’s wife…I’m very close to being a LMHR…I’m 70. Started keto 12/7/23…started Carnivore 1/20/24 been since. Had CAC score 2/7/24 550..yes,I no I’m high risk. comment_text: I've had rheumatoid arthritis for 23 years. As my joints deteriorated, so did my mobility, and weight had crept on. I need to lose about 80 - 100lb to feel like myself again and improve my functionality. I looked at Ozempic, researched it, and ultimately decided I wouldn't touch it with a 10 foot barge pole. Loss of muscle mass was one of the main factors.   Before RA, I was never 'skinny', rather  a stocky and strong size 12 - 14. I felt healthy and robust. I had a physically demanding job (archaeologist working on excavations, shovelling, wheelbarrowing etc). Even with RA crippling up my legs, my upper body has stayed strong. I didn't want to lose that.  I decided to do intermittent fasting, high protein, low carb, whole foods, no refined sugars, seed oils, ultra processed foods or snacks etc, and stick to around 1200 to 1500 calories max a day. I also do strength training. I only use 3kg weights as I don't want to put to much stress on my damaged joints.  In 3 months I've lost 25lb. I've also found in not particularly hungry, even after 18 hours of fasting. Some days I do a full 24hrs. I'm also naturally eating less. I don't need as much food to feel satisfied. I often come in under my caloric goal and have to increase my food intake the next day to make sure I'm getting the nutrients I need. One of my favourite meals is full fat Greek yoghurt, fresh strawberries, 10g of dried mango, chia seeds, and 5g of wheat bran. I mix it all up, and let it sit a few mins. It turns into a cheesecake consistency due to the chia seeds and is awesome.  I no longer wake up hungry. As I'm writing this, 12 hours ago I had dinner (150g rib eye steak and 200g homemade cauliflower cheese - about 600cal - 400 from the steak). I'm not hungry at all and won't be for another 4 to 6 hours.  I'm extremely happy with my progress. This is a lifestyle I can stick to. I still have the odd treat, like a hot chocolate,  ice cream, some dark chocolate, etc. But my portion sizes have greatly reduced and I don't often feel like having these things, particularly the ice-cream. It's far too sweet for me now and somewhat repulsive. I'll make a fresh alternative by blitzing up frozen berries and Greek yoghurt.  So, no Ozempic for me. In addition, I'm proud that I'VE managed to make these changes with success rather than giving the thanks to a injection. I'm sure there are people it may benefit, such as those with severe food addictions or extreme morbid obesity, or as an alternative to bariatric surgery. But it is not for me.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes; Autoimmune Disease; Arthritis

comment_text: Yes, this doctor is correct.  Any time you lose weight, you will lose muscle.  This is true for both if you are on Ozempic and if you are not on Ozempic. The important thing is you diet and exercise while you take it.    I lost 80 lbs on Zepbound, and I feel amazing.  I dieted, exercised, and ate a lot of protein.  I am the healthiest I have been in years, and I have had no problem keeping the weight off after going off the drug.

Doctors need to give out an instruction pamplet on how to use it for weight loss, and what things patients should do to prevent muscle loss.  Unfortunately, too much time is spent trying to get the insurance company to cover it, and by the time they do it is weeks after the prescription was written.  I lost 136 lbs without medication then got approved for Zepbound and lost an additional 68 lbs.  I did it on my own from my own research.  I started out pretty much as an invalid 2.5 years ago using a wheel chair briefly, then a walker for short distances, then a cane, then trecking polls to no assistance.  Now I walk 3 to 8 miles a day.  Unfortunately, I don't have the hot guy beach bod yet, but I'm working on it.

From what several of my friends and coworkers experienced it seemed like a paid for eating disorder. None of them had the desire to eat and if they did they were all running to the bathroom for one end or another. After watching that happen to them, I decided to go old school and go to a bootcamp in the morning and watch my macros. I'm a month in and down 8 pounds. I'm good with that.
I love the medicine.  I have always lift some weights.  I have lost 85 pounds and feel better than I have felt in 39 years

I was on Ozempic just for a couple of months to kick start my weightloss. I have lost 32lbs so far in 2.5 months. I am able to continue losing weight on my own. My debilitating depression has gone. (I was 320lbs and miserable) Aches and pains have gone. More energy. I'm getting my life back thanks to Ozempic.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I laugh when Drs say this. I was 15 stone and fat was wrapped around my organs, a fatty liver and I could hardly walk without pain. Now I am 9 stone, nearly pain free and able to exercise.  My sugar addiction is completely vanished and I  feel amazing. Do not judge us with your skinny body. I was fit until my 50's when I was crippled for 7 years waiting for TKR s. I finally got my life back

I can attest- was trying to get off last 10lbs. (to 133 at 5'5" fit female) I use a BMI scale at the gym which is a high quality machine. In one month I lost 5lbs. but my body fat %went UP and I actually lost 2 lbs. of hard earned lean muscle. So mad, beyond the pale. I worked out during that month and my diet had plenty of protein but it was absolutely CATOBOLIC on my muscle. Not to mention by 3rd week I was exhausted. I did low carb and kept calories at 1200 which is what I have to do to move the needle. Sure it was good to not be hungry but several months later and back to my original BMI and muscle, I can say that I appreciate my strength and appetite. For me it was horrible, made me feel old and fragile and I really wonder if it did any more damage. Great drug for the death-fats but if you are close t to ideal weight don't bother. I've also noticed that some of the actress that have taken it indeed look smaller but just less shapely, as if they  lost a lot of muscle too. Erica Jane as an example.

comment_text: I lost over 40 lbs on ozempic after trying so many things and failing all my life. My life is so much better now. No more food cravings and anxiety over food. I'm living my best life.

comment_text: I didn't do Ozempic I did Rybelsus and it was amazing.  Was on it for about 6 months.  Lost about 30 lbs and I'm keeping it off.  You learn to eat better and eat smaller portions.  Don't eat to get full, eat to not be hungry.  Due to the delay in signal transmission between the stomach and brain you will be over full if you eat till you actually feel full.  If you feel the least bit full STOP EATING.  I don't care how much is still on the plate.  This was a BIG thing I had to learn and still occasionally struggle with.  Eyes bigger than the stomach.  Portion control.  Eat several times a day, smaller portions.  It works for most.  Just takes freat discipline and will power.  I fully understand it may not work for everyone for various reasons.  I can only speak for myself and my experience
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

I take Ozempic for my T2 diabetes. Over the past 2 years, I have lost 80 lbs on Ketovore. I hope I have avoided losing muscle mass.

Loving my results on mounjaro.  If we don’t loose the weight, we’ve already lost longevity.  Without some weight loss most of us are not able to exercise.  I’ll take my chances.  Down 15pounds and looking forward to going hiking again in a few weeks for the first time in years.

This is massively misleading. I have been on ozempic for two months. I get regular body scans to check fat loss and skeletal muscle mass. I have lost 18lbs of fat and maintained my muscle mass. This is because, in addition to clean eating I lift weights 3x per week, run 2mi or more 2x a week, and take reformer pilates classes 2-3x per week. I was already doing all of this prior to the drug, but I struggled to control my eating habits. Ozempic is an aid to weight-loss. It is not some magical solution.   I'd like to point out that I tend not to take medical advice from random people online. Talk to a real doctor if you have concerns. Know that just because someone is wearing a white coat infront of a camera, that doesn't mean they are an authority on what they are talking about.
Oh please. I’ve lost 30 lbs. Still working out. No muscle loss. Girl bye.

I'm a stage 3 breast cancer survivor. I've lost 40 lb on ozempic and it's been amazing for my whole body recovery from cancer treatment. I'm absolutely not starving, my hair actually started growing back after 3 years, my inflammation all over is down. It's been amazing and I'm 49. You just want everyone sick and on multiple meds.
I have lost 40 pounds and deadlift 220lbs, Squat 200 and bench 100. I have gained muscle mass and lost fat by prioritizing protein and strength training. This is total bullshit.

My elderly mother uses Ozempic, or Wegove or whatever. I can't tell you how much it has changed her life. Before she was nearly crippled by back pain and could barely stand for more than 5 minutes at a time. Now she does PT twice a week and has lost nearly 80lbs on a 5'2 frame. Perhaps its not always the best choice, but for those who are on a track for death by immobility, it can swing the direction of the rest of their lives.

I did an experiment a little over a year ago because I kept hearing this exact problem. People were losing a lot of weight, but also a lot of muscle mass. My theory was that it was simply because they remained sedentary during their caloric deficit and their body disproportionally dropped muscle as a result.   So, I got my body fat analyzed at the University of New Mexico (underwater hydrostatic body fat analysis), went on Tirzepatide (a GLP1 agonist simulator to Ozempic) for 12 weeks, but lifted weights to failure every day, and ate 200 g of protein per day. In 12 weeks I lost 20 pounds of fat, and actually gained 1 pound of muscle. It's not the GLP-1 agonist that is the problem, it is the lack of activity and protein during the weight-loss journey that is the problem.

Lost 35 pounds. Feeling just fine. Did personal training twice weekly but already had a good amount of muscle. This whole “you will waste away” thing simply isn’t true. For reference ending weight was 137, im a woman. Have maintained for four months on lowest dose available. Took 2 years to lose 35 pounds on various glp-1s. Zepbound 7.5mg  is where I lost most of the weight. On 2.5mg now to maintain.

I went through something 3 years ago where every time I ate anything I had severe gallbladder pain to the point where I only ate one bowl of oat bran every 7 days…I lost 40 lbs very fast. I lost a ton of weight and I believe autophagy healed my shoulder and gallbladder. I never had my face muscles sag though, I never took Aleve ever again or any painkillers after 10 years.

I’m 14 and on it only because I feel really insecure about my weight because it’s an unhealthy weight to be at, I just have to make sure I do work out too and so far I have lost 35lbs!!!🎉

Well fruit is my middle name, I have fruits everyday,every week every month,every year.Not stopping, if I have been lied to about amen,so let it be.I am 61 years young,do not have diabetes, nor high blood pressure. This morning I have a ripe,banana,an othatie apple,one starapple and a piece of payapa.Never felt better.

I lost 170  pounds cutting out sugar, including fruit and carbohydrates with intermittent fasting. Now I am able to maintain my weight and I eat  fruit daily like apples, pears, cantaloupe, watermelon and bananas. Keeps me happy and content. Things I avoid permanently after weight loss are bread, pizza, oatmeal, pancakes, cereal, and pastries.  I love sweet potatoes, nuts and seeds, dried fruit, eggs, all fresh veggies and fruits, meat and fish, coconut oil, nut butters!  Losing weight is definitely a different story than when you’re just maintaining.

I lost 30 pounds and brought my A1C down from 6.3 (almost fully diabetic) to 5.8 (almost normal range) by eating meat, fruits, and Vegetables. I actually eat MORE fruit than I used to. I eat more potatoes now too. I cut out candy, chips, desserts, most bread, sweet drinks, and a lot of standard American junk foods.
Fruit saved my life. Had stage 4 kidney disease I decided to go 90% fruit and 10% veggies for 90 days. I'm off the transplant list 💪🏾🙏🏾

I ate 4-6 apples, sometimes 4-6 bananas, sometimes 4-6 oranges every single day for 14 weeks, with a few other things.  I'm 55, I went from 208 down to 168, in 14 weeks, and lost my Cancer without any treatments etc. I worked painting houses, and like most human beings should,  I exercised a bit on top of that. 1-2 moderate workouts, barely breaking a sweat. I probably wasn't working out enough, but it still worked.  I included a little over half pound of organic grass-fed ground beef every day

When i get a sugar craving i pop a slice of butter and let it slowly melt and cravings are gone.  Amazing!

This actually works.  I ate too many carbs earlier in the day.  This evening I still felt physically full but very unsatisfied and was craving food, but didn’t feel like eating.  I ate about a tablespoon of butter on one cracker, and the craving went away completely within 10 min.  It’s 2 hours later, and I’m completely satisfied still. Thank you.
I agree, started eating butter every day and have lost 50lbs in 6 months, along with starting a carnivore diet. Feel great and not tired all the time anymore.

Dr. Boz is 100% right on this; I experienced this first hand when I had diabetes and my neck and underarms were completely dark. I also had skin tags. I then temporarily went on the Keto diet for almost a year and my diabetes was gone, along with no more skin tags and the darkness on my skin cleared up.  Amazing 🙌
I once weighed 350 now 135. No more insulin resistance and it took 11 years but I reached my goal weight.
MY A1c is 5.7. All my labs came up great today after changing my eating habits . My skin is great, and I have hairy toes.

What an excellent, clear, easily understandable video! I'm on day 21 of my first fast. I've lost 45 pounds so far, from 340 down to 295. But the last few days the weight loss has been slowing down and autophagy has been reshaping my body before my eyes. My wife tells me I look 10 years younger. My goal weight is 200, so I have a long way to go but I feel fantastic! It isn't easy but I've been destroying my health for 57 years. Dieting has never worked for me. I finally have the tools to escape morbid obesity.

I did a 22 day water fast, I lost 34 lbs and look 10 to 15 years younger, the loose skin I thought I had was completely gone, it's miraculous! My face now looks like I'm in my 20s!  Edit: people keep asking my age so here you go, I'm 39 😁🌞🌄

I’m 56 I started fasting 5 weeks ago doing Omar from Sunday at around 4:30 till Tuesday around 4:30 I eat nothing. Rest of the week just one meal a day. I’ve lost 25 pounds. My energy is awesome my mind clarity is so much better. My shoulder pain I had when raising my right arm is gone. I haven’t felt this great in years. I would say I haven’t felt this good in many years.Fasting did this for me. And I thank God for it.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I started keto and fasting 5 months ago and i already lost 100 pounds, love keto and love fasting... i do most of the time one meal a day, my skin is great, no more diabetes and no more high blood pressure, i still want to loose another 80 pounds in the next six months, I haven’t done any excercise i feel great
aspect_names: Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I'm 65 and a babyboomer. Did as doctors told me and lowfat diet.  I ditched the MDs and lost 80 pounds and I'm fat adapted and use the tools of fasting and IF Keto. I feel for the young kids today who are all much fatter than i was at 25 years old.   MDs have not changed so i pray they watch you on utube.

That explains a lot. I stepped on the scale around Thanksgiving of 2012, I weighed 454 pounds. I was able to lose 30 pounds fairly fast just cutting back on some things. After that it was like a yoyo deal for me. I dropped to the 380's, went back up to 410, dropped again, went up again. In May of this year (2018), I was around 365. I had these huge flaps of fatty skin hanging down from my belly, and had seen the surgery on my 600lb life on TV, and was wondering if there was any way to naturally lose that. I started doing a google search and came across another doctor who said if you would do the Ketogenic Diet, over time your body would get rid of that. I thought initially why not try it for the summer, so I did. Since then, I have lost 40 pounds, smashing the 360 thresh hold I could never get through before, but the difference this time is, that flappy area has not gotten larger, in fact it seems to have shrunk a little. I typically eat dinner between 6 and 7 PM, and then lunch the next day after 11. I guess I was doing the right thing by accident, but thats the great thing about Keto if you do it right, your not hungry. Thanks for confirming what I did by accident.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: 2 years ago I began alternate day fasting. It was tough as I was eating a diet with carbs. Every fast day was a huge challenge (as I am the only person who cooks in my family) and I hoped I would become adapted and just get used to it. Alas it was a struggle and after a year I gave up. Despite cutting my calories in half I only lost 25lbs. Now on keto fasting is so easy I never feel truly hungry. I breeze through every fast day with no faintness or brain fog. In fact I feel mentally sharper while fasting. I have lost more weight in first couple of months than I did in my fasting year. At 50 I feel I've found performance fuel for body and mind. Who knew it would be fat.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I am 2 1/2 years in on a Ketogenic life style, OMAD regularly and really only go back to 2 meals a day for a week just to keep my metabolism on its toes then right back to 1 meal. I've only pushed out to about 27 hours with 0 calories but it was easy. I've lost about 65lbs inflammation is gone arthritis is gone about to get off blood pressure meds or a least drastically reduced. My mood is way better, brain feels way sharper.  I am going to try out going 7 days fasting. I am now 53 and was at 250 lbs 2.5 years ago now down to 178 lbs and 5'9" and feel great. Quit drinking alcohol 2.5 years ago as well. My only regret is not doing it at lot sooner but better late than never. Thanks to people like you DR. BOZ spreading the word really make it possible to get healthy. Thank you for all you do!
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Arthritis

comment_text: I have done 20 hour a day fasting for some time now. I’ve lost 60kg and reversed diabetes type 2. All by eating sensibly between 1pm and 5pm. No snacking. And cycling a lot to help with cardio and heart

One of the best videos I've found.  I have been slowly figuring this system out through trial and error.  I'm in ketosis, intermittent fast regularly for 24 hrs, basically one meal a day and sometimes fast  for 48-72 hrs and feel like a million dollars afterwards. I've lost 100lbs, and don't have skin hanging.  You've got a subscriber here!  Very interested to learn more, now I know it's called autophagy. Thanks!

I'm so glad I found out about autophagy as I'm on a 175 lb weightloss. I've lost 70 lbs so far without any loose skin... I normally do 23:1 fast but my friend wants us to do a 7 day fast. I've done 5 days before so I think i can handle it

Perfect!!!! I got the info I needed!!! I’ve been in ketosis for 10 weeks now! And my only fear throughout all this process is having loose skin at the end. So far I’ve lost 55 lbs and I didn’t know if I was going to have a lot of loose skin at the end but now I’ve found out that I have been doing autophagy all this time! This makes me happy and less stressed out. Thank you Doc!

comment_text: Wow. Thank you. I at one point lost 150lbs.. and it didn't go well. Lose skin left me depressed and I gained 40 pounds back. I got back on track a month ago with intermittent fasting and powerlifting now I'm hopeful of my results.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: Hunger isn't the villain in weight loss. If you take the time to recognize how you actually feel when you go without food you begin to find that fasting isn't as hard as initially perceived. There are a LOT of videos on this subject and all of the current science agrees with this video, yet covers even more benefits. I tried fasting way back in 2001-2 or so, but only made it to 4 full days. It made me feel comparatively great at the time, but I didn't keep it up. In the last month I've learned a lot more about the process than before and so I tried again. This time I made it to 7 full days.  It has drastically reduced the amount of pain medication I require, but I still have a degenerative disease that makes it very difficult to use my hands. So I plan to try again after the holidays. In the meantime I've reduced my sugar intake as much as I can and lost 30 lbs or so. I feel better and the food I do eat tastes so much better than before it seems unreal.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I've lost 55 pounds in the last 6 months, mostly by stopping eating grains, beans, fruit, sugar, dairy and all animal products that weren't local and pasture raised or wild caught. I've also started eating once a day (around 4-5pm), plus a little snack of nuts or avocados for lunch. Haven't had breakfast since January. This summer i've also done 3 5-day water fasts (about once a month), and i've never ever felt this light or good in my life. Be aware that it's very possible that your LDL cholesterol will go through the roof. Probably not your particle counts or the other lipids, but The total cholesterol will definitely freak out your doctor. comment_text: 63F - I've been keto/carnivore for at least a decade - lost 150 regained 40.   I love sardines so I thought I'd try this. Did it for 4 days and lost 10 lbs and feel like a superhero. I did add hot sauce, mayo and vinaigrette sometimes, but ate most plain out of the can. Fasting blood sugars in the 70s, never went higher than 120 after eating.  I even ate diet Jello and had a big coffee every day the whole time. Truly amazing.

I lost 5lbs on 1st day, 4lbs on 2nd day. Started off at 293 and 2 days later 284, for a total of 9 LBS !!!   Almost down to my pre-covid wt.  I feel SO much better, not stopping till I am happy.  Costco is my favorite: Boneless, Skinless & Finless in Olive Oil.  Update: I stopped for 3 weeks and gained it all back (from carbs), something smells fishy for my future.

Sardines are delicious.  I dont have a microwave, single and live alone.  Then my fridge broke so I started thinking of things I could eat without major cooking, fridge storage, etc.  And I am hooked.  There are so many delicious brands of sardines.  I can't stop.  I like the ones in olive oil most but will rotate with water ones so I don't overdo on fat.  It is very satisfying and I have been doing it for a few months.  But when I cut added sugar and other, that was the game changer.  14 pounds melted away in six weeks and I was never hungry.  Now I got all these guys chasing me....20 something year olds and I'm 44! Really was just trying to be healthy! I should say I don't only eat sardines.  But they are my main course.  I also eat nuts, dates, occasionally a small banana and a bit of 100% cacao. comment_text: I've always wondered why eating just meat can become so complicated and you've certainly simplified carnivore.  I'm a 72 years young female and found my way to carnivore after Keto then ketovore for 2 1/2 years and the whole time on Keto/ketovore wondered why I was still eating veggies, fruits and salads and all the keto treats.  Hated veggies, was never a fruit eater but the treats were nonstop. I lost 70 lbs eating that way then moved on to carnivore and never looked back BUT in the 1 1/2 years I've been carnivore I've not lost the additional fat that I need to lose and now I think I know why--it's the last vestiges of Keto; the dairy, the sweeteners, the coffee, the tea (I'm southern and we grew up with ice tea as our first drink in the sippy cup). Although I've not experienced the body fat loss and recomp that I want I feel like Benjamin Button and have reverse aged in so many fantastic ways; grey hair returned to light brown (formerly blonde), eyebrows have grown back in, spider/varicose veins have all but disappeared, age spots on my hands are completely gone, etc. Thank you for all you do to get the word out and if the younger generations adopt this way of eating they'll live the life we're supposed to live as humans.

Coming up on a month! Almost 30lbs down. The way I feel is amazing. The clarity and brain fog is so noticable. And the inflammation is gone. Would be sore getting out of bed. Now I just pop right up. Love it
I'm 65 retired gained around 50 lbs started carnivore about 6weeks ago I've lost over 30 lbs and my BP is way better! I didn't want to get old and go on med's! Thank you for your help God bless you!

I've started carnivore and I've been told it's dangerous I'm ridiculous and blah blah. I'm 321 lbs high bp and I have pcos after doing a lot of research and listening to people like you and peoples testimonials. I've lost 10 pounds in 6 days so far and I'm just gonna keep going. 👍🏻

I started this 8 days ago and have lost 12 pounds. I have MS and usually start my day by taking a handful of pills to even be able to get out of bed. My wife and I started this diet around 6 months ago. It’s mind blowing on what are bodies look like and have become! We do cheat 1x week and drink 4 days a week and still insane results. If you told me this a year ago. I’d say your crazy.   The only bad part is we keep having to buy new cloths because our others don’t fit anymore. Who would of thunk!? Media lies and obviously wants you sick to buy their products.

I was keto for 4 years (meat/salad/few nuts and cheese) then hit plateau… My son and I did a carnivore challenge for 30 days. I had a couple slip ups but I lost 10 lbs in a month. What happened after was the big thing…. My whole body composition changed. My clothes were falling off—I think Carnivore reset my gut and now I’m at my college weight/size. Skin good. comment_text: So I started my carnivore way of eating a little under 4 weeks ago.  I was at the stage where everything hurt, energy was really low, i was napping 2 or 3 times a day at 65 years old. I was exhausted by the smallest physical tasks and about to accept that my trajectory was down quickly from here to a rocking chair and shawl within less than a decade.  I was woken regularly with stabbing pains in my legs and hands, a few arthritic joints that came to always hurt after a while.  I can only sleep on my side but both hips and shoulders on both side hurt so much I would literally swap sides 40 times a night, if not more.  I was sleeping, inc naps about 13 hrs a day and i couldn't make a fist as my arthritis meant my index finger only bent half way in and finally i had a six month pregnant pot belly, although the rest of me was slim and i couldn't shift it..........not good.  After two weeks eating just meat, salt and nothing else, all of these symptoms disappeared except the pain in my shoulders which was half fixed.  As i write they are now completely fine and pain free.  I can't even remember when i had this much energy, when my mood was this good and i was this pain free; maybe in my 30's.  Funny thing was that i loved meat at that stage, never ate vegies or salad if i could avoid them.  My ex wife used to say, "you're going to have to change your diet, it's unhealthy and you're starting to smell like meat.  I was honestly shocked how quickly things healed and repaired eating carnivore; I have a ne lease on life.  It was the "Petersen's family story that convinced me to give it a go.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Arthritis

comment_text: The biggest problem I faced with the carnivore diet with intermittent fasting was not being able to sleep well.  2 simple tricks fixed it for me. 1: Eat your first meal around 9am, and your second meal around 1pm.  Then, don’t eat any solid foods again until 9am, & just have water & electrolytes about 2 hours before bed.  Apparently you sleep a lot better when your stomach is not busy digesting food. 2: I know this sounds strange, but if you wear socks to bed, it will help your body temperature stay lower which allows for quicker, and deeper sleep.  These 2 changes are a game changer for the carnivore or even the keto diet. I learned about these tricks from Dr Berg and Dr Daniel Pompa on YouTube.

I started carnivore the weekend after Thanksgiving. I weighed 152 lbs @ 5’6” today I weigh 138. I had daily heartburn and 1 T of vinegar in water worked so good that I am off Omeprizol. I am 70 and I haven’t had this much energy in decades, and no more brain fog!

comment_text: Eating this way has honestly made my second pregnancy so much better than my first. I’m more than half way and have soooo much energy, and my cravings are gone! Love it!

Dr.Chaffee, thank you for all you do. My husband and I became carnivores 10 days ago. I have lost 5 lbs and hubby 10 lbs. We are doing this for health reasons more than weight loss, although we do need to drop a few pounds. Life is changing ❤

That's the best-looking beef I've seen 😮 All jokes aside, Dr. Chaffee has changed my life. I'm healthy, my gorgeous 62 yr old husband is healthy, and yep, we are on no meds, and we don't have scurvy 😂 Quick update- I've lost 35 lbs and my husband  53 lbs effortlessly. We are 10 months into Carnivore. It's our life now

Thats what my hubby ate everyday for about a year. He is a 1st responder and had reversed all arthritis, chronic pain and acid reflux plus fatty liver. Then shockingly autoimmune condition hit his kidneys. Minimal Change Disease. He bgan losing albumin levels, edema hit 1st and then after Bumex and 80 mg Prednisone he began to go into remission.  Sadly, it hasn't stuck and he is on round 2 and 6th month of steriods...possibly retuximab next. No, it was Carnivore that caused it...I suspected the mRNA jabs and even his Nephrologist says she has seen axsharp risevin Nephrotic Syndrome and MCD since the jabs even though his biopsy was supposedly inconclusive duevto lack of enough material to tell for certain.  The point is he had to get the jabs he'd thought because he was responding to covid life flights and other case. We are believers in science and vaccines but I had refused this particular one do to having had ckd 4 in the past and reversing it on Carnivore to stage 2. I didn't feel there was enough information on it yet and when the EMA asked for a halt do to kidney issues I opted out... he had already had 2 jabs by then though if Moderna. So... such is luck. There arev2 dead and 3 others in my town that are personal friends with newly aquired autoimmune diseases. I dont regret ever skipping this jab
aspect_names: Digestive Health; Fatty Liver Disease; Chronic Kidney Disease; Autoimmune Disease; Arthritis

I also do OMAD. I have NO cravings. Sleep is better  Energy is better Skin is clearer  Etc etc etc  No regrets.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Skin Health; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: I started Paleo 12 yrs ago when I started doing Crossfit. I dropped 10 lbs, built muscle. I have fed my labradors (all rescues) raw from the time I adopted them. Two passed back to back 3 yrs ago, one was 13 and the other 14...the third is still with me and approaching 16.  So this diet is for all living, not just human. I am a F, 62 yrs old, and weight 137, 5'7", came from an obese family.....this has worked for me very well!
I’ve read all Thomas Seyfried books. I’m doing this on my own. For the last year my head pain is gone. This man is so amazing.

My great grandson Scotty died from brain cancer at 2 and Im determined to help others till my last breath. I've been on Keto diet for two years, went from 175 to 130. I love to walk. I'm a professional singer, a teacher, a fine art and sign painter, nurse and dr. assistant. I am also a social services college student . I am ready  for my next sacred phase in my life. Being groomed for Broadway from age 11.. but my heart told me to volunteer my music therapy to hospitals etc. Robert is 52 , stage 3, I'm so excited after listening to this info I'm grabbing my guitar . God bless you both!

I found that drinking multiple cups of coffee when I was losing weight, actually made my PSA number rise quite markedly. I stopped drinking coffee for 3 weeks prior to getting a PSA blood test, and the figure was cut in half. I realised this was due to the inflammatory nature of caffeine. I now only have 1 cup a day, and I am due to have another PSA test in 4 months, so we will see. I have also been on a carnivore diet for the last 18 months and have never felt better - I am now 60 years old, and am looking forward to many more years of healthy life.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

Professor Thomas is brilliant and very caring about the health of others like yourself. Thanks Doctor Chaffee because of caring Doctor's like you I'm getting healthier I've lost 40 lbs now and my A1c is down to 5.4 from 9.3 and off of my type 2 diabetes  medicine I'm 70 yrs old and no longer on any medication because of the ancestrial appropriate carnivore diet. I started July 1st and went cold turkey I went through alot strange feelings in my body they are kinda of hard to explain and I'm feeling more normal now I've only fasted   intermittently on some  days of the week I'm contemplating on a 3 day water fast with minerals how do you feel about this Doctor, thanks, Avery

I'm a rebel RN who practices and teaches metabolic therapy. Lost 50 lbs my first year doing keto/carnivore and never so fit as I am now at 55 years old. I follow your weekly podcast. Would you consider doing one on DAWN to educate us a bit more? People need to know about this.

As a BC survivor, I wish I'd known about about Keto diet earlier, but I went with whatever they said and did chemo and  radiation. For me it wasn't that terrible, I managed it. But the minute I found out about Keto AND fasting, which was through Dr. Jason Fung and Dr. Ken Berry right here on YT. I jumped on it that day, fasted the rest of the day and never looked back. I lost over 125 pounds and turned my health around, I had been headed towards T2D, had HBP, ALL gone after eating the Keto diet. Convinced me!!! I appreciate the doctors and professors that question things and are so very intelligent to figure all this out, all we have to do is follow it. Thanks Prof Seyfried & Dr. Chaffee!!!

My brother is a cancer surgeon and he suggests the keto diet. I have cancer and went on the keto diet. It was rough getting started but after two weeks become rutine. I lasted 3 months lost 35 pounds and felt terrific. Once i started chemotherapy i couldn't stand the smell of meat and could not touch anything cold due to the effects on my nervous system. I had to stop that diet and tried to eat what i could as i had no appetite. Now im through with therapy and want to get back to that diet but maybe not so strict and ad more greens and beans.  The modern off the shelf food is poison, if it doesn't kill you directly it will surely kill someone you know. Our government and all the food producers are in on it as they control it. When you go to the grocery store you see many many labels but in reality there is only a dozen companies. You think you have options but its a lie. Stick to the parameter of the store and stay away from boxed, bagged and most canned foods. Pick the lessor brands as these are lower in sugars, salts and chemicals to enhance taste

I appreciate the doctors and professors that question things and are so very intelligent to figure all this out, all we have to do is follow it. Thanks Prof Seyfried & Dr. Chaffee!!!

Never felt better, though.  IBS is gone, consistent energy, sleep well, BP down.  I clicked with this b/c going grocery shopping is an issue b/c almost every aisle is 100% plant. aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: At first I did not believe this ...until I changed my diet to carnivore. My IBS and gluten allergies and chronic stomach ache and migraines went away. Muscle aches and flu like symptoms and inflammations went away....and the best part. I do not fart lol! Sooo weird.

I’ve been carnivore 80% of the time for almost a year. My chronic joint pain is gone and my eczema is virtually gone. Symptoms start returning when I have a slip up/lazy week from time to time. Everyone just needs to find what works for them. If you have no issues eat a balanced diet, its alot more enjoyable. If you have any problems with your body and or mind there is good chance you can fix it with a diet modification.

I was eating eggs and meat when I was bodybuilding and I never felt better in my life or had more energy and my wife couldn't keep me off her. Im talking 3 pounds of beef and 15 eggs at least per day.

I was near death before embracing the carnivore diet, but just six months in, my depression is gone, my energy is back to what it was in my youth, my clarity of thought and productivity has almost fully returned. The standard American diet is only good for the profits of the Pharma industry, the doctors that are improperly treating patients with their products and the processed food industry. This is reminiscent of the historical revelations that lead, (Pb),  then used in food and wine packaging was making people insane and causing early miserable death.  Thank you doctors for your pioneering efforts, your great courage and your scientific honesty.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being

comment_text: I know two brothers (age 74 and 77) who both have been diagnosed with Lewy Body Dementia.  One is on allopathic and is doing poorly on several medications, still having hallucinations, is like a child...  The other brother who was diagnosed several years earlier, has been on Bredesen's protocol for about three years and is LBD symptom free.  No processed foods, low carb, good fats like olive oil, lots of Vital Choice wild salmon and sardines, etc.  He also did a 4 month QuickSilver Scientific mold cleanse that was a turning point for him.  Appreciate this podcast - diet MATTERS.

At 62 I was found with artery blockage. I gave up sugary and processed food immediately. I didn't know how much fat was inside me. Two months into this I gave up all grain and fruit. Ninety days in, down 16 lbs look thin like I did when a teenager, restless leg that I'd had for 15 years gone. Fall to sleep easily, Depression gone with new goals for the future. Mental fog and midday tiredness gone. My dear wife of 32 years saw what was happening to me and is on board and looking great. Thank you all in the low carb community
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: We cured my  husband of 29 years  of chronic lymphatic leukemia, hbp and diabetes with diet and the Greek test to see what ivs would be best for him. It took a year but his doctor had to keep halving his meds until he was off. Then he looked 10 years younger for 2 years completely healed. Then he took the tainted flu shot. Gone in 4 months. Bravo for you helping people. You are an Angel dear sir.

This was very good news to hear that carnivore diet is helping Alzheimer’s patients! My mother suffered from Alzheimer’s and was such a terrible  disease. My father also had dementia, but was not diagnosed with Alzheimer’s. I’ve always assumed that this would be in my future as well. However, I started a carnivore diet 15 months ago and have lost 75 pounds and feel wonderful and am age 68. I now feel after seeing this video that I will have a much better chance of avoiding Alzheimer’s!! Thank you for the work you do!

I had a friend die last year of Alzheimer’s.Her husband took care of her for her last 5 years. I had suggested a keto diet but he felt she was too far gone for it to help and he did not want to take away the remaining things she loved, sandwiches, cookies pastries etc. So we watched her slip away. My husband’s cancer is in remission, his. Colitis, has not had any incidents. I thought he was going to die Halloween 2022. He bleed out his behind, was on the floor in a pool of blood. We are carnivore, we’ve both lost 70 lbs. our cognitive abilities have improved. I am 72 he is 74. He retired because he could not stay on track during his lectures. He was teaching landscape architecture at UCLA. He believes in the regenerative practices of using cattle to reclaim desertified land.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Neurodegenerative Disease; Cancer; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I'm 68 yrs old and started my carnivore lifestyle in August,  right after getting blood work completed. Had new blood work completed Nov 1st. My triglycerides went from 176 in July to 74, and my HDL increased from 33 to 48, my VLDL decreased from 33 to 12. My A1c was 5.5 after being told I was pre- diabetes back in July. I'm also now at my high school weight and feeling great. I also eat like a teenager again just to maintain my weight. Carnivore for life!!!
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: I find what you said about females and hormones interesting and worth looking into.  Anecdotally, I was a healthy and fit size 8 heading into menopause… 15 years later I was struggling with maintaining a size 14 - had brain fog, low energy, and bowel cancer.  Ketovore has made a world of difference in my mental health, energy levels, and general health.  (And I’ve lost 15 kilos since March of 2023) Thanks Dr Chaffee. (…and I Love your Patreon group)
