comment_text: Started carnivore at age 60.  62 now and lost 55lbs. No inflammation, no joint pain,more energy, better sleep, better mental clarity. I haven’t had a cold or flu now in almost three years. Big food and pharma are the absolute enemy. Nothing but money making corporations now.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation

comment_text: 2 years ago I came across your presentation on plants are trying to kill you.  After that video I followed you and started the journey.  2 years on carnivore I am off blood pressure meds, I went from a 36 inch waist to a 31. Hover at 10 to 12 percent BF at 190lbs at 57 years old. God told me to listen to you and I did.   Everyone always ask what the hell i did, when I tell them they just scoff and say it’s steroids bahahahaha!!!! Helluva of a compliment if you ask me.   Thank you doc for everything.

I respect everything the Doc has to say. I’ve dropped 85lbs I’m fit as a butcher’s dog. Stopped drinking strict carnivore and at 64 I’m in the best condition of my entire life but I love my coffee. Honestly I think I could give it up but I don’t think I want to. My drug of choice.

So good! Been carnivore over 1 year…lost 55 lbs. but now in a long stall. Good news is I am holding steady weight wise but would like to shed about 10 more pounds…anyway this was great and something i can pass along to others. Thanks!

i did carnivore for a year the most i lost was 40 lbs. and that was in only 3 months. then i started putting on weight a little at a time but my pants were still loose and my shirts. i am 63 and have a physical job so l did not realize i was gaining muscle.so after a year i was 10 pounds short of where i started.so i quit the diet for a year. gained 30 pounds.now back on it and in 8 weeks have lost 20 lbs. i will stick to it this time.
Went carnivore dropped 60lbs and my sleep apnea is gone. I had sleep apnea before i got depressed and fat.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: It really bothers me when I see "Carnivore Influencers" who are in my opinion cheating, lying or alcoholics. They shouldn't be "Carnivore Influencers" until they are ready and dedicated to being an example to others. You, Dr. Chaffee are one of the few carnivore Doctors/Influencers I trust because you are the most strict. Almost all the issues people have are from not starting and following the carnivore diet correctly.  If you start with fatty beef and water first, it makes a huge difference. Everyone has different reasons for starting the carnivore lifestyle. For me personally, I wanted to lose weigh and reduce chronic pain. Since I lost 60+ pounds (in 1 year) and my pain is gone, my focus now is getting a six pack, building muscle and improving my physical and mental well-being.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I am carnivore for 2 years now, I lost 30kg, no more HBP, my vision got better, no acne, no nothing, I stopped watching all carnivore content and I am glad this popped out after a year or so without any carnivore video or picture. The only ,,guilty pleasure" for me is 2 cups of coffee early in the day, with alot of butter, and trust me when I say - alot of butter in both of those cups!  Guys, this will heal you and release your mind and body, you will start taking new paths in business, family, life in general, just like I did. Good luck and much love!

I did Carnivore along with intermittent fasting for about 9 weeks I lost 38 pounds. I went from 180 to 142 pounds fast but I felt like crap . I’m a 57 year old male who has worked out and cycled for 35 years and let myself go for the last year up until I decided to get back in the game . I started from scratch and now I’m back in the gym on my bike and I’m 145 pounds with about 10% body fat. I only limited my diet to carnivore for a short time .  I’m back now to 16/8 fasting and eating a high protein low carb diet.  I feel like a new man .  Being consistent on any diet and with your activity levels is the key . It’s not a one size fits all situation.  I eat what I want one day a week in moderation , life is to short not too.  Btw … I’m only 5’5 so the extra weight was terrible for me 😂  Stay consistent and you’ll get results and you’ll be glad you did.  Remember what works for me might not work for you so be patient and experiment with different types of diets and fasting and eat as healthy as possible.

Had to stop carnivore because I could no longer afford the price of all the meat and butter (and the eggs). Groceries had risen to 25 Euro per day for just one person, not sustainable on social security obviously. Gained back most of the weight I'd lost (50kg lost...) over the last 2 years in under 2 months, again have the chronic intestinal inflammation that made me go carnivore in the first place. My mental health is shot to hell as well, but that's got more to do with having worked in extremely stressful jobs for the last 15 years which finally broke my mind (possibly permanently according to my team of mental health experts, while there has been improvement in the last 6 months of treatment I'm nowhere near where I was a year ago, let alone 15 years ago). Obviously if you feel better physically you're going to feel better mentally as well, and vice versa, that's a given.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I lost over 230 lbs on the carnivore diet. I reversed a lifetime of metabolic disease. My son who is in 6th grade now has witnessed this first hand. In his health class he is being taught about healthy eating which he is told includes fruits and vegetables. He told the teacher that she is wrong about that and on a worksheet for the class answered a question to the effect that eggs, red meat and fat are the only healthy foods for humans, and as you can imagine he got a bad grade. I told him that it's ok, that he got the bad grade, but going forward, just answer the questions the way he knows they want him to answer them and not with  actual correct answer, and that there will be other times he will be taught things in school that he will KNOW is absolutely not true, and the best thing to do is just play along, and forget about it when the lesson is done.

I was keto for years, but still hungry and fell into the trap of keto sweets with sweeteners, nuts, nut butters, and cheese. I had lots of times getting off track and gaining/losing weight. Since carnivore, the cravings are gone. What a relief ! Consistent weight loss. Thanksgiving is this week but I’m not afraid I’ll fall off the wagon and be out of control from now until New Years. Thank-you Dr Chafee !

My husband thinks im crazy to eat meat and eggs, I can buy ground brisket when I am here in Florida, when we are in PA it is very hard to find any ground meat below 80/20 and the quality is not good.  I have to sit across from my husband gobbling down salads and vegetables all the while he and several friends gain weight and  get more and more hunched over .  We are both 74, most people think I am much younger (at least on low stress days lol). Many times he gets the comment, is she your daughter or some think I am my granddaughters aunt. I will stick with my carnivore, it has healed my gut and definitely made me stronger and healthier.  Thank you Dr Chafee you are a God send, you encourage me and are the one I point to for validation. Blessings!
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Digestive Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I’m mid 50s and I’ve been carnivore for 35 months. Lost 25 lbs (back to my college rowing team weight).  I’ve always been pretty active, but now lost most of my belly fat and see more muscle definition. Surprising improvements include no more seasonal allergies and great energy levels. I can do long distance cycling without mid ride fueling with carbs. I wish I discovered carnivore sooner. Thanks Dr Chaffee for all the helpfull videos

I’m going back on carnivore due to my dysphagia from eosinophillic esophagitis I’ve developed from inflammation in my body and eating crappy food. I’ve had stomach issues for SO long, ileitis, colitis and they think I have Crohn’s disease. I’m 28 and can barely eat anything now without choking but I KNOW that carnivore works. I did it back in 2023 and lost 40 lbs within 2 and a half months. I felt and looked my very best. I am very excited to see where I’m at by this time next year.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: I did carnivore for 9 months, but I allowed myself dairy. I only lost around 20 pounds unfortunately, and I started having headaches every time I ate beef or pork, it really ruined the lifestyle for me, I still don't know why meat was giving me headaches. I was salting my meat, and I was taking Dr. Berg electrolyte capsules sometimes.  I never cheated the diet, I really enjoy eating meat and didn't feel any need to. When I try carnivore again, I'll avoid cheese, but if I start getting headaches again when I eat meat I don't know what I'll do.  For reference, I've had a headache issue with some foods since early childhood, the first food to do it was eggs.

I discovered I bloat and have gas with sour cream etc and severe diarrhea when have ghee. But not butter    I am losing my too much hair. And I’m getting dimply looking fat on my arms. I need help!!!

comment_text: Was on Keto for six months. Last month on that diet, I I had one meal a day consisting of full salad (with heaps of BABY SPINACH) and a piece of porterhouse steak. After that month I felt like crap, I mean really crap. I thought I would feel great. Nope. The next day I started the carnivore diet. That was six months ago, The pain from my RA has lessened by at least 1/3rd . I have lost 16kg and feel great.

I am very thankful to you! I’ve been carnivore for 6 months. I cured my IBS Symptoms, my chronic fatigue, chronic anaemia. My mind is sharp and I feel great. I’ve also lost 5 kg. Thank you for sharing your knowledge ❤
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Anthropometric Changes; Inflammatory Bowel Disease

comment_text: Had no choice but to go carnivore. I had tingling all over, sharp shooting pains like 1,000 andts biting me, I had some burning in my legs, heavy legs waking up, 3 to 4 leg cramps a day,  dizziness, white flakes skin, sore in my mouth cracked lips, mucus in the stool.  No doctor could figure it out and took me 3 years to figure it out.  I started noticing the days I didn't eat a lot, I felt okay, the days I went to the all you can eat salad bar were pure hell.  I stopped eating them and most of those symptoms went away, the remaining are way less.  Only been carnivore a month.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: Loving the work, in remission from arthritis chrons and psoriasis. Thanks to you and saladino.

I'll add another testimonial.   This is embarrassing.  I am a retired chiropractor (20 years).   We are supposed to be, you know. . .health nut kooks, big alt med.  You know the type - granola, oats, gym nuts, protein shakes, supplements and triathlons and all that.   I was athletic before chiro school - beach lifeguarding.  But after that intense exercise ended, obesity caught up with me.   I tried everything adn it was embarrassing to my patients that although I'm a STRONG, MUSCULAR guy, eventually became built like an offensive lineman, I was definitely obese.  Tried keto and Atkins with decent result but carb cravings would come back.  The craziest thing I did was a grape fast for 47 days. . .I was nearly euphoric so there is SOMETHING to elimination.    Years of IBS have plagued me.  I am troubled by dyslipedemia and I admit I am still worried about LDL (hey, even the traditional allopathy indoctrination seeps into alt. med), but after 1 month on carnivore, I am ready to hit the pool again, hell, I was ready 2 weeks ago  Libido and performance is up.  IBS is "tamed" (not sure about an optimal biome for me). . .it's not perfect in there but at least I don't have to go everywhere worried about where a bathroom is and "exploding" in there.  Transit time for a plant, even something as innocuous as a bunch of grapes used to be about 2 hours, not kidding.  It' nice to have things quiet down there.  Sleeping better and my musculoskeletal inflammation is reduced to nearly gone.  Intrusive thoughts of food are gone.  I don't know what, if anything I'll do when I go to my vegan PCP and my LDL's are going to be high but I am hesitant to swap metabolic syndrome and obesity for a lower LDL.  I hope NAFLD won't be a thing, nor uric acid levels.   My gallbladder is gone and I am not thrilled at the idea of poisoning my liver to reduce my LDL's with a statin. . .but maybe I'm being stubborn.  All in all, this was a good trade of risks I think.  I'll get APOB, full lipid, insulin levels, and a CA++ score done and see where I am at, I guess, along with the normal panels and PSA I guess.
aspect_names: Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile; Fatty Liver Disease; Gout; Inflammatory Bowel Disease; Gallbladder Disease

The Matrix wants you to stay sick, weak, and dependent on this system.  Always remind yourself why your are doing this, and that should keep you from going back to carbs.  That is what I do. comment_text: Swapped to meat and veg diet a month ago. Eat lots of cheese when i want to, cook in butter instead of oil. Only need to eat at night because i don't get hungry anymore, practically don't fart anymore. Don't get tired during days, and have lost 13kg so far without any extra exercise. I think all the carbs, sugars and processed crap is what is stuffing us over.

I live with my son, his wife, and my three grandchildren. My adorable daughter-in-law loves to bake and cook and can't get the concept of how the food she buys is poisoning herself and the kids. TOO MUCH SUGAR! She complains about having to prepare my overweight, disabled son, her husband, special Keto foods separately from her and the kids. He's lost 100 lbs. since I moved in a year ago, and feel terrific!  I asked her why she would feed herself and the kids "poison carbage" when she sees what food can do to hurt a person, like my son? I said to feed everyone the same Keto foods, and that it is actually less expensive and much, much healthier.  She is a major work in progress....
My BP has been out of control for weeks.   GOD spoke to me and told me to stop eating.  My weight has dropped 11 lbs in as many days and my BP has started to stabilize.  GOD is good.
I changed my diet due to high triglycerides the last month to keto mostly veggies and a lot of meat. Love this diet and cut out all carbs. Lost 10 pounds already.
I cut soda from my diet, lost 20lbs in a month.
I fell victim of this at 10 years old. I now only eat meat, butter, bacon and eggs and lost 100 lbs without exercise, off 8 med's , cured my COPD, my cataracts, anxiety, diebetes, have energy. 🧐

comment_text: There’s a sports doctor named Mike on here that has muscle, but is really obese and he’s always saying the public didn’t listen to the government advice. YES WE DID. I’m 61 and have been misled and lied to my whole life. I’ve tried every way possible to lose weight and have lost over 100 lbs the hard way and now the easy way. Now I know to eat meat and veggies, basically. Watch my blood sugar on a cgm because a spike of blood sugar is followed by a spike of insulin that tells the body to store that fat! Feel better at 61 than I did at 16.

comment_text: Well said.   I am primarily carnivore. Been diabetic for 10 yrs.  Switched to meat based diet. Very close to coming off my meds.  54 yrs old feel 20 years younger.   Lost 70 lbs.    meat meat meat.

I'm mostly carnivore these days. I started back on keto in March and have lost 24kg (~53 lbs) since. I stuck to the macros for about 4 months but slowly I've started eating mostly meat and eggs. I eat some zucchini, because I love the taste, and some mushrooms now and then. I haven't felt this good in years. I have people tell me all the time that I'm doing terrible harm to my body or I'm damaging my organs but I've been doing my due diligence with my GP and my bloods and other health metrics are within normal ranges. Plus my wife says my snoring is almost non-existent now. So if I am doing harm as the naysayers are want to say, I guess I'm harming my way to a healthier, fitter body. :/

I used to weigh 532lbs. As of a couple days ago I’m down to 342. Mostly thanks to carnivore which has helped reverse my high blood pressure, arthritis, Hydradenitis Supperativa, and gout, yes, gout, as well as anxiety and panic from CPTSD. My depression is completely gone and my default emotion is happiness all the time. Going carnivore drastically reduced my gout flare ups from completely debilitating to almost non-existent. Going carnivore saved my life. I’m a 36 year old woman and was diagnosed with gout, high blood pressure, arthritis, as well as many other diseases, before the age of 30. At age 32 I was hospitalized with lung failure from being a smoker for 18 years. Over 4 years without tobacco and over a year into carnivore, my life is completely different. People don’t recognize me if they haven’t seen for a couple years. Please, if you haven’t yet but are considering going carnivore especially if it’s your last resort, do it. Don’t give up. There is hope. You can heal. I have to add that long term fasting paired with intermittent fasting has also been a major component to my healing journey.

Week six of carnivore, lost 13 lbs, feel great also drinking LMNT electrolytes. I'm 59 and workout regularly but couldn't drop 10 or 15 lbs like I used to. I struggled through my 50's loosing weight now with CV🐄🐮 finally getting results. Also dermatitis under my nose has cleared up. So glad to be part of this community now and learn more.
I gave up vegetables and fruits four months ago. Never felt better. My gut calmed down quite a lot.
I have been trying meat only diet (organ meats) for the last few weeks and I feel mentally and physically so fit. I feel like reverse aging. All pains and headaches gone !!!

At 59, I've had a heart attack and stroke. Suffered with diabetes, high blood pressure, and brain fog. I changed my eating to a Carnivore lifestyle six months ago. My A1C went from 12.2 to 6.5. My doctor has taken me off diabetic meds. My blood pressure was also back to normal, as well as having zero brain fog. My energy levels are through the roof, and I lost over fifty pounds. You are 100% correct. We human beings were  never meant to eat plants. 😔 I wish I had known this when I was younger, but it's never too late to live a healthier life. I am living proof, along with the countless other people who have found  their way to a healthier more productive way of eating.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure; Type 2 Diabetes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: “My blood sugar went from 90s to 140s after eating an all meat diet and I felt like hell. Added back some carbs and my blood sugar went back to normal in a day or two.”  Michel-gb7l

I’m ok with people being vegan. That leaves more meat for me! Since going keto I eat mostly meat and my arthritis is almost gone, no swelling in my joints and I weight the same as I did in my 20s . I do get a lot of ribbing for it but I’m healthier than any of those guys. Going strong for 3 years now.🎉🎉🎉

I have just come to this realization the last couple of weeks. What started my thinking on this was I had 3 customers just volunteer their experience on a carnivore diet. 2 weeks ago I decided to try it for a month.. I am diabetic, twice in the last year, one of my insulins was out of stock. I knew I had to change something to get off the insulin. Not trusting the system. My first day eating only meat, my blood sugar went to normal and has been normal since.. I did not need insulin when I ate.. I was still taking the longterm, til last night.. because my blood sugar was getting too low, I decided to try, not taking it last night.. (I had already been cutting back on it). My blood sugar was 89 this morning. Which brings me to another realization.   What about the vegetables, the main ones available to us..when and where did they originate and why? Learning how we have been duped and lied to about so many things, what if we were being lied to about vegetables? I already ate mostly just meat and veggies, and only other carbs occasionally.  Now it's high quality meat for me and my husband, and he isn't complaining.. Score... 😊 and I've lost 7 pounds.. Thank you for this information, I  truly feel I am on the right track.

I did the Atkins diet for 20 years, mountain biked, etc.  Developed diabetes then had 100 percent blockage and a heart attack. When I did a vegan diet for 5 months my A1C went from 7 to 5, no meds. Makes sense to me that the bathing of cells in saturated animal fats can shut down insulin signaling. Cleaning out that fat seems to eliminate the insulin resistance.  Extreme carnivore and extreme vegan both suck. Extreme carnivore caused me to HAVE TO be an extreme vegan to fix the problem.  My goal is to be an Omnivore without being extreme.

I gave up animal products.  I suffered from metabolic syndrome (obesity, high cholesterol, high triglycerides, and high blood sugar).  I went whole food plant based no sugar oil or salt and I lost 65 pounds, all my conditions greatly improved, my gums stopped bleeding, and erections and energy returned.  I used to have an appetite that was never satisfied; whole foods cured it.   I no longer use a sleep apnea machine.   Finally, the food is cheap-Beans, lentils, rice, potatoes and seasonal apples.  Also, I save money by not having to buy pills or visit the Doctor.  I am thankful to all the vegans who suffered attacks to reveal the truth to me.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Sleep Quality; Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control; Lipid Profile

I do this now, I fast 20/4 and break my fast with a savory meal of around 1000 calories, and the same meal right before the fast. I eat 300g of chicken, 3 eggs, and one avocado (avocado for unsaturated fat and fiber). It keeps me full the 20 hours, stay hydrated, water is the best. I have lost 10 lbs in the last month, and my glucose levels are green. For snacks, I eat macadamia nuts, which have good fat, and nutrients.

I have eliminated majority of carbohydrates and everything he says is true. Always have energy ,never very hungry ,never felt healthier. 61 years old and stronger than I can remember. Mostly carnivore.
I believe this. It feels amazing. Carnivore here.  Cravings are gone.

Not true! It took me over 9 months on carnivore to get my blood sugars down to 100. Even at 7 months they were still running between 160 170 150. So 3 days is definitely not enough for somebody who has fatty live with disease
I vouch for this, went on 1 keto meal a day for 2 weeks, dropped 2 stages of fatty liver and lost about 10kg
Born to teach could listen forever KOKO keep on keeping on down 140 lbs n 16 months off 4 years now been with u from the start

Intermittent fasting, 18-6, with oatmeal,  lentils,  beans, fruit   for carbs, veggies,   Chicken, beef, fish, cottage cheese, greek yogurt,  hard cheeses, 45 minutes of brisk walking every day, and my type 2 diabetes on remission (7.5 down to 5.2) and lost 42 pounds. No rice, pasta, tortillas, breads and added sugars like sodas and no fruit juices. After a 6 months i introduce just some corn tortillas (2) weekly some pizza or rice too, and my weight and glucose still nice.
2 weeks was the study I heard that cured fatty liver.

❤Fatty liver / Enlarged Liver diagnosed December 2024  Carnivore literally healed my liver in 3 months - I was morbidly obese at the time, type 2 diabetes and basically I was in a horrific state health wise.  I also had an infected gallbladder which I thankfully managed to save by eating as little carb as possible.  If you're starting out, PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE TAKE EITHER TUSCA OR A DIGESTIVE ENZYME PLUS OX BILE.  WHY?  because you may already have a gallbladder waiting to flare and you provavly already have gallstones you never knew you had.  When you start carnivore the gallbladder has to wake up as you fed it low fat crap possible for years.... so to protect it take your pure ox bile with a digestive enzyme for about 3 months.  I still take 2 a day because I know I've probably still got gallbladder sludge ❤

I'll just trust my own eyes and how I great I feel. 2 years carnivore and I look like a professional athlete, and I looked like that before I decided to start working out because I had so much energy, not because I wanted to lose weight. People who try want to work out to try to compensate for a shitty diet are doing it wrong. Go carnivore or low carb long enough and you will just feel like starting to work out. Calories In Calories Out is BS when in ketosis, I eat as many calories as I ever have and lost 40 lbs without working out. If I ever feel like the carnivore diet just suddenly starts killing me, I'll change my diet.

I have been low carb, keto, Ketovore for many years. I’ve done intermittent fasting and extended fasts and have lost 130 pounds in the process. I am almost always pretty low carb. I sometimes go carnivore for a few days, but I will never be carnivore for much more than a a week or so at a time. It’s just too constricting for me, and I don’t think it’s necessary. 💜

I suppose we all have different degrees of optimal diet. I can definitely say I am best without any carbs. I really don’t understand all this in detail. I know that being pain free is terribly important to me, so I stick close to beef butter and eggs.  No pork no gorgeous bacon, but no pain.

Practicing carnivore here (lost 50kg bla bla bla) i think this is one of your most insightful episodes yet because I've always wondered why we have so much amylase in our saliva, in a unique way compared to other herbivorus great apes.  We definitely have a unique and species specific metabolism and physique.  I've been contemplating the evolutionary reasons behind this and i suspect our ketosis heavy metabolism combined with our elevated subcutaneous adiposity is partly a response to evolving without fur.  On another note i was born with a congenital heart deformity called transposition of the major vessels in 74 (im lucky to be alive) and I've been using ketone IQ during exercise to improve my heart function - its the real deal to put it mildly 💪💪

Alot of pure carnivores do develop high blood sugar long term on this diet. Also they end up looking old and depleted in critical minerals like potassium. I agree with him  Use the diet as a tool for weight loss and to get off medications  Not a long term way of life  Ive heard other experts say this.

This at least answers my question of why my A1c went up from 5.0 for 2 years to 5.6 after going low carb (100 or less) 4mos ago. Good to know I need to be careful as I've heard some say not to worry about A1c going up. As to carnivore diet, while I'm hardly an expert, based on archeological articles I've read over the years, the idea that humans' diet was originally all meat is just ludicrous. Fats from meats have made me nauseous since I was a child, as does too much sugar. Anyways, I guess I need to find my carb happy place. I have lost 17lb, which is great, but cardiovascular disease is an issue for me. Definitely not LMHR. When we get down to it, genetics isn't there yet and will play a big role in the future in deciding nutritional issues. It is just irresponsible to make sweeping recommendations for all people when the truth is your genetic makeup rules what is and isn't right for your body.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Glycemic Control; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: We need protein for essential amino acids, we need fat for essential fatty acids, we do not need carbohydrates so that seems to indicate to me we are carnivore , but I’m just a computer engineer. My girlfriend reversed her type 2 diabetes with Keto and has been off medication for over 20 years.

I am 3year 3months carnivore. .. I lost 25 kg in the first 18 months give or take...  However since then been on a stall. I am not at my optimum weight. I probabaly needed to drop another 8kg.  I have been searching and pondering why why why.. I also went through a spell of healing, no longer having night thirst not nightly loo breaks for about 4 months. But it has returned.. not with a vengeance but none the less, its back i do wake uo every night at least twice needing the loo and a drink of water.. so what caused this reversal.. I i decided about 2 weeks ago to add in a carb breakfast to see if the insulin spike may do something.. so started eating a cooked apple for breakfast...   And blow me over... I am finally experiencing satiety. First time since being in carnivore... so much so, that tonight I cooked 4 mini steaks... It would be my first eating since the cooked apple... I only mabaged to one of the mini steaks and just could not eat any more. (Before i would need to eat 8 mini steaks and still not even feel satiated... I am beyond fascinated.    So yes lets say carnivore healed multiple things in my body and still isnt finished but this insulin spike breakfast fullness has surprised me fully.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

comment_text: A work colleague once asked me if I chose one for the rest of my life, coffee or alcohol, what would it be? I answered coffee in any form without thinking. And that’s despite the fact that I really enjoy craft beers and fine reds, even if it is much less often nowadays. Am down 13 kg  using a combo of IF and keto with one cheat day a week. Slow but steady wins the race.

I like the don't be a perfectionist suggestion. Also, I work at not over thinking or obsessing about diet. In 6 months I've reversed 9 years T2 diabetes, lost over 30 lbs to get in normal weight range. Very low carb foods  Healthy fats. Working on a sustainable maintenance program. Love coffee. Do you think it stops autophagy? Some Drs recommend water &salt fasts only  for autophagy.

I don’t think any addictions are healthy. I feel so much better now that it is gone. It impeded my iron absorption so it wasn’t trivial for me. My iron went from 8 to 20 in 2 months when I gave it up. I only drink water. My favorite is carbonated water these days. I am very satisfied and don’t want anything else.

I am a chiropractor who recently started eating ketovore/carnivore who has lost 40 pounds in very short order.  I believed that fat made me fat and salt would make me have a heart attack and all of the other "bullshit" that the world would have you believe until you and Dr Berry have completely changed my way of thinking! Thank you so much!!!!!  I have one question though,  I have helped thousands of people stop having lower back pain by stopping them from drinking caffeine and drinking more water.  I truly don't completely understand why this works but I can assure you that it does work.  So I am hoping that maybe you can shed some light on why it works so well?  Is it getting them off of sugary drinks that causes them to not have back spasms anymore or was it caffeine that I had suspected that performs as a diuretic that I thought it was or is there something else that I am just not putting together?  I would truly appreciate your input.  Once again thank you so much for all of the work that you do, I would not have ever changed my way of eating without input from someone as educated as you explaining to me what I was doing to my body by eating all of those carbs.
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: Dr Cywes, I had to downsize my coffee intake. I had already swapped to instant coffee to go on having lots of  cups along the day.  In bed at night my leftside belly ached a lot each time.  One day I decided to cut all of it but for two 50ml cups in the morning and one cup at 6-7pm plus some in the milk later on. Problem solved. No more pain.  Not missing the long string of coffee intake events during the day. Cheers!

In 2020, I went keto/carnivore and within months lost 50 lbs, got off all meds and am now lean and even athletic at 62. I run circles around the 6 grandkids. I’m also drinking my 2-3 cups of delicious coffee every day and am not even remotely concerned.

i agree with you 1000%. I've lost 25 KG (55 lbs) last year doing strict Keto and exercise. Black coffee was and still is my go-to "drug" (serotonin activator) but also just gave me energy going through the combination of Keto flu, fatigue, work-related stress, emotional stress etc. that i experienced. I would have never been able to keep up my discipline and stick to the diet without my best friend coffee.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I've been on 20 net carbs for 5 Yrs. I'm in great health and will always eat this way. Lost 50 lbs and have never gained any back. Never felt better. Walk 2 to 4 miles a day. I'm 72:. 73 in 3 months.  I'm mostly carnivore.
Keto and intermittent fasting was life changing for me.  Brain fog and anxiety gone.  Endless energy and great sleep.  Never going back.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality

comment_text: I've been on the carnivore diet for 3.5 months. My type 2 diabetes is completely resolved without medication. Same with my high blood pressure. I was able to stop both medications. I have lost 32 lb. I feel so much healthier. My success I believe is due to keeping it simple. I don't buy quote unquote keto foods or try carnivore or keto recipes. I keep my diet very pure and simple. Beef, chicken, pork, fish, butter, some cheese, eggs.

I've been on Carnivore Diet for a month and I am rarely hungry now. I eat when I am, but I only eat a fraction of what I did before. I've lost 30 pounds and my pain is almost gone. (I have MS and had constant excruciating pain.). The weight loss is great, but the pain being almost nonexistent and being able to move much easier is the catalyst that is keeping me going. I barely ate yesterday bc I wasn't hungry and was busy all day. At 2am I woke up and was "starving". 😂 I got a piece of cold chicken out of the fridge and stood in the kitchen eating it like a thief.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: These are some good tips. Once I changed my relationship with food from eating for pleasure to eat for fuel, my entire body composition changed for the better. I've been a strict carnivore for 8 months and never felt better.
I’ve lost 71 pounds since last August doing keto! I love it!!!!

This is the first really detailed explanation as to why I lost 25 lbs in the first 8 weeks of keto, nothing for the next 20 months, and then actually gained 10 lbs, despite being pretty faithful to eating keto foods only.  Now that I actually have some reason to try hard again, I might finally be able to get the fat off.
Down 86 lbs since January 2020 on low carb and IF with a pretty heavy gym regiment
I have lost 28lbs so far on Keto, my Fibro and RA pain is better. I do intermittent fasting 16-20hrs a day. I eat once a day about 5 or 6pm. I’m never hungry and never feel deprived. 🥰
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

comment_text: I follow a Keto diet with intermittent fasting, I don’t care about what weight I lose, I’ve never felt better, all perimenopause symptoms gone, better sleep, loads of energy, great mood. I really wish someone had told me years ago not to eat carbs!
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I have been doing this for 5 months, yes this is "how" I do keto. 5 months ago blood pressure 230/120 on 3 types of pills. Now 120/88 on 2 types of pills, lost 21 lbs, feet not swelling up anymore, joints dont hurt. This is also while waiting for hernia surgery, and so NO exercise activity. Totally sedentary. Amen this strategy described works!
aspect_names: Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: I lost 100 pounds doing IF alone ,no keto or carnivore,it  was more of omnivore type diet ,i ate fruits and veggies,along with meat,i found out about keto and my health had gotten better felt great,i counted macros everyday,i started eating more carbs as i got comfortable with my weight i gained 10 pounds up to 230,now im doing carnivore with some buttef and cheese,and im down to 218 ,my own personal experience is to just say "no" to carbs completely, i dont do macros ant longer i just eat until im satisfied and its been working for me.

I always say Ketogenic way of eating but I  changed and call it LCHF but the most important thing is zero grain, Starch, Shugar and all of the bad oils. Mostly animal base products very few veggies and fermented veggies. My hunger has died and I am mostlly 2mad or 0mad. Have been lthis woe for 3 years and will never quit. Dropped 106 lbs. off my old 76 yr. old carcass and now have perfect BMI. I am also very active and don't have many aches or pains like my peers.
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes

comment_text: I lost 84 pounds and have kept it off for 3 years so far so I'm just going to keep doing what I'm doing.  I was pre-diabetic and now I'm not. So there's my story/ opinion.. if that even matters

Good grief ...comments on here. People make healthy eating SO complicated and following keto substitute keeps your mindset on living to eat, rather than eating to live. Just cut out ALL processed food, all sugar. Carbs less than 20g are the only carbs and they should be from veggies. Protein from living animals/fish. Never count "macros". Eat OMAD for sustenance. I have lost 45 lbs in 5 months, reversed my T2D, brought my triglycerides back to normal and for first time in 20 yrs, my labs are perfect!
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Lipid Profile; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: This is a great video that I will rewatch many times.  I discovered keto in 2021 and lost over 100lbs in 10 months, but I struggle with emotional eating so I've put about 30-40lbs of it back on as I go on and off keto. Gearing up for another run at it as I'd really like to lose another 70-100lbs to get down to my honeymoon weight. Profile pic is my wife from 98.

I went KETO 6 weeks ago and lost 7 lbs without an exercise routine. Just my regular outdoor duties. Learning new recipes has been fun but the most challenging effort is finding what I need on store shelves due to COVID-19.

As a diabetic I can definitely attest to the morning sugar spike. The ONLY reason I eat breakfast is because my doctor said I need to eat after taking my insulin in the morning. So I eat foods that give me no sugar spike, like eggs, avocado, etc... I eat almost zero carbs in the morning, and my daily carb intake is under 30g. I’m going down on insulin dose every 4 days though, so when I’m completely off insulin I will likely nix breakfast. Never been a fan of breakfast anyways....
This was insightful.  I woke yesterday with a fasting glucose of 114 while in ketosis (1.2mmol/L) and I was puzzled.  Forgot about the liver!  Thanks Doc!
Just turned 66, been on keto for 4yrs lost 75 pounds and reversed diabetes
That is me exactly, lost 76 pounds gained back 9.5 pounds now took off 6 of that. I need permanent weight loss.

I am addicted to sugar, I have given up carbs and sweet drinks but I still add Stevia/monk fruit in my coffee. I am slowly taking that away too. It’s been less than 2 months and the difference that I saw immediately was the inflammation decrease, joint pain is gone! It’s hard for me to get enough fat in my diet because i always ate one meal a day before  with snacks throughout the day and late into the night. The snacks were sugar,  protein loaded w/ sugar or sugar loaded crap like junk foods, but never fat loaded. Its more than an addictive way of eating its habit forming. That’s what has to change your habits. I still eat one meal a day with no snacks at all unless I get hungry enough Then I choose one of these, cheese, a few berries, celery, or boiled eggs. I have had to completely reinvent meals with foods like fatty meats that I never consumed. It’s taken me several weeks to learn how to prepare meals that have fat, and minerals but now I just know. If I am eating lean cuts of beef I add coconut oil, if I need more fat to reach my daily goal i add heavy creams, butter, cheese. My daily goal is based on what my body is telling me not a numbers chart. I have liver disease and Hashimoto’s so there are foods I consume to help those issues that are either hi glucose like beet juice, kiwis, or high protein like hummus. I have been able to fit these in several times a week and stay under 25 carbs a day, not net but total carbs.  It’s possible, it’s work but it’s possible. I have a long road ahead, my health is why I chose Keto, and I am making this a life change not a diet. Next step is quitting smoking. Then I will have overcome all my addictions. I am so glad I found this amazing man! Straight talk no BS, simple truths. Love him!
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Glycemic Control; Fatty Liver Disease; Thyroid Disease

comment_text: I was going to comment something mean. I realized I actually agree with what is being said. I have found a lot of success on keto. I have made lost 70 pounds. I am addicted to sugar, I have given up carbs and sweet drinks but I still add Stevia/monk fruit in my coffee. I am slowly taking that away too. It’s been less than 2 months and the difference that I saw immediately was the inflammation decrease, joint pain is gone! It’s hard for me to get enough fat in my diet because i always ate one meal a day before  with snacks throughout the day and late into the night. The snacks were sugar,  protein loaded w/ sugar or sugar loaded crap like junk foods, but never fat loaded. Its more than an addictive way of eating its habit forming. That’s what has to change your habits. I still eat one meal a day with no snacks at all unless I get hungry enough Then I choose one of these, cheese, a few berries, celery, or boiled eggs. I have had to completely reinvent meals with foods like fatty meats that I never consumed. It’s taken me several weeks to learn how to prepare meals that have fat, and minerals but now I just know. If I am eating lean cuts of beef I add coconut oil, if I need more fat to reach my daily goal i add heavy creams, butter, cheese. My daily goal is based on what my body is telling me not a numbers chart. I have liver disease and Hashimoto’s so there are foods I consume to help those issues that are either hi glucose like beet juice, kiwis, or high protein like hummus. I have been able to fit these in several times a week and stay under 25 carbs a day, not net but total carbs.  It’s possible, it’s work but it’s possible. I have a long road ahead, my health is why I chose Keto, and I am making this a life change not a diet. Next step is quitting smoking. Then I will have overcome all my addictions. I am so glad I found this amazing man! Straight talk no BS, simple truths. Love him!
aspect_names: Appetite & Satiety; Pain & Inflammation; Glycemic Control; Fatty Liver Disease; Thyroid Disease

"Thats like having a fart, not a bowl movement" LOL .... I have been Keto for 3 months and now feel amazing. I lost 12 lbs in the beginning and stalled for 2 months! but now am starting to see weight drop again which I am assuming is now fat instead of water. Dont give up everyone! It takes time.

I'm a food addict. Carbs made me crazy, fat and sick. I have been VLC keto since August 25th, and zero carbs for a week.  To say that I feel better is an understatement. My mood disorder has been put to bed and no more cravings.

Last year I rode 2,005 miles on my stationary bike, and only lost about 3 or 4 pounds.   I felt so frustrated that I didn’t lose more, but realize now it’s because I was still eating high amounts of carbs which caused my insulin to spike constantly.  Since starting keto a few months ago, I’ve lost 15 pounds and can attest that the enhanced mental clarity that comes with it is legit.  This has been LIFE CHANGING and I am so grateful!
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Anthropometric Changes; Glycemic Control

comment_text: I slowly adapted to a keto diet and never experienced any downside. I am 66 years old and lost 54 pounds in 6 months. I was diagnosed as pre type 2 diabetic and prescribed metformin twice daily. After 6 months my bloodwork came back with excellent results and my doctor said I could quit the metformin and we will continue to monitor. I am on the journey to becoming 'fat adaptive' and loving the scenery as the journey continues. It is a lifestyle change but once you adapt the advantages are evident. My energy level has increased, mental clarity honed, sleep quality much enhanced, joint inflammation has subsided by 80-90% so arthritis conditions have greatly improved, no more Aleve twice daily! The food tastes great and the grocery bill has reduced even though I am eating better quality of food. No snacks, no junk, no sodas, no sweets not junk food binges, but still enjoy the social aspect of going out and tipping a few drinks with friends. Oh, my golf game has improved substantially! LOL
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Energy & Vitality; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes; Arthritis

comment_text: 8 weeks ago I just decided I was eating no more carbs. Chucked them all out. Yeah it's been a bit rough at times but my philosophy is a bit of suffering does you good.  I feel a lot better these days, already lost 10kgs (93kg to 82.9kg) and I feel adaptation through my energy levels. I eat in a 7 hour window one main meal and a smaller second one.  It suits me fine, and I can see myself staying on this because I know I'm eating fuel and nutrition now instead of that awful crap I was subjecting my body to before.  My abs will come back in a few months! 55 year old and got my buzz back. 👍👍

What a wonderful and clear explanation! This is exactly what I’ve experienced the past 10 months doing this lifestyle- I’ve lost 30 pounds, no hunger or cravings and have never felt or looked better ! I wish everyone would just understand and be aware of how amazing this way of eating and intermittent fasting is instead of being brainwashed with low fat and counting calories - thank you Doc once again for everything you do.

When I started carnivore, I was doing it for weight loss. Because it wasn't instant, I did more digging and research and changed my thinking. I am on a journey to heal my liver and the weight-loss will take care of itself! It's working! Down 17 pounds this month.   I make a donation so you can keep providing excellent content! I approach your videos like it's a lecture, with pen and paper for notes. I have learned so much! I am enthused by your obvious passion for the truth! I work in an ER. I see first hand the utter destruction of humanity via "a pill for every ill". I see women younger than myself with a HUGE list of diagnosis starting with type 2 diabetes, morbidly obese, and on so many medications. I can't help but become unhappy with my chosen profession.  Most of what I see in the ER's now is preventable. So sad. And all people want are pills to "cure" whatever it was that brought them to the ER in the first place.
aspect_names: Anthropometric Changes; Liver Function; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: Being in a keto state the last year and half has improved my blood work in every aspect..... ~my sleep has improved in every aspect and my overall attitude and investment in myself has never felt better the more actively involved I have gotten in preparing my meals and caring about what I eat.

Age 70, Male, historically good physical shape, Never Sick, No Vaxx, physically active, 4 weeks into Carnivore. Had a few hits and misses with "Keto Snacks" and "Fat Bomb snacks, which threw me off.  I seemed very low on energy for the first couple weeks as my body adjusted and diets solidified.  Settling on 3 eggs + 4 strips bacon for breakfast and Mission Barbecue (No Sides) for dinner.  I can keep this diet going.  No cheap, but works.  I had a heart attack 3 years ago..  did the statin ++ BS for a year and a half..  I could always feel my stent..  sometimes it was obvious.  After going carnivore, I notice  I can no longer feel my stent (at all!).  My neck arthritus pain Nearly gone with Carnivore..  another few weeks and should be gone. Other things I've noticed w/Carnivore: 1,   Mosquito bites seem much less than before? 2.  Could my Hair be getting thicker?  skin is less itchy as well. 3.  My Teeth/Gums are MUCH better.. Gum pain is Gone..  could my gums be healing?  Using Chlorine Dioxide Swish every morning as well.. (WOW!) major improvements!!  No more Listerine Crap..  the teeth destroyer. Carnivore diet for me.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Pain & Inflammation; Skin Health; Cardiovascular Disease

comment_text: Transitioned very slowly (one month) to keto in 2021. started eliminating high carb foods one at a time: corn, rice, potatoes finally bread all the while I swapped out my high sugar treats and condiments for ones with less refined sugar. I only lost 10lbs in six weeks, but I never got the flu. Lost over 100lbs in ten months, reversed my diabetes and all my vitals and bloodwork are perfect. Mood swings are gone. Energy is almost unlimited. Sleep is restful and uninterrupted.  I do miss some old food friends from time to time and revisit them (like this last two weeks) and the weight returns very quickly, but I also lose it almost as quickly once I return to keto. Mentally preparing myself to go ketovore soon. We'll see.
aspect_names: Energy & Vitality; Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: All I can say is my body feels much more peaceful and restful since I gave up sugar and most carbs. I have between 10 and 20 grams a day. My weight is down 30 pounds so far, blood pressure is now normal and my years-long pains are gone. Sugar indeed is inflammatory.
aspect_names: Psychological Well-Being; Sleep Quality; Pain & Inflammation; Anthropometric Changes; Blood Pressure

comment_text: One of the best explanations on ketosis and fat adaption.  I have been fasting for 3 years, back in 2022.  My A1c was 12+ or something (Type 2 diabetic).  I started a variation of IF and PF to fix myself.  Every time I fast I ensure that I reach a state of Autophagy that would be 24 hrs onwards.  The longer you go into that state the more the body repairs itself. Now, it does NOT mean that if you do IF and PF you are "FAT Adopted".   On the early stages of my fasting I totally removed all forms of highly processed carbohydrates and only broke my fast by feeding on boiled eggs, leafy green salads (without dressing, only drizzling apple cider vinegar and pepper for taste), baked fish seasoned with salt + pepper + olive oil and avocado oil (NO seed oils).  And just about every steaks I prepare by myself.  6 months into that fasting period, I lost 60 lbs of weight and my A1c records to 5.5 from 12.  To help you get be "Fat Adapted", you have to remove the highly processed carbohydrates.  Also, I don't use any form of sweeteners that are considered Diabetic friendly.  It was a test-and-run journey to find the correct mix, because I was constantly testing my glucose levels from a staggering 355+ to "90 - 120mol/dl".   All because of fasting using these two (2) methods; IF (Intermittent Fasting) and PF (Prolonged Fasting).  Also, I do I mix of OMAW (One Meal a Week) and TMAW (Two Meals a Week).
aspect_names: Hormonal & Menstrual Health; Anthropometric Changes; Type 2 Diabetes

comment_text: The rest of me is in good shape but my belly is the problem , it’s big and I would like to reduce it . Last year  I was on a keto crash diet ( 6 weeks long) supervised by a Doctor , lost 35 lbs and gained 20 back. Any suggestions would be most welcome . I am type 2 my a1c is 6.9.

I had lost about 45lbs 5 years ago with this method...then fell off the wagon and gained it all back. Started back up 2weeks ago and went from just wanting to do keto into a water fast! Like after a week i didnt feel hungry because ketones are also appetite suppressants.  Havent eaten in 6 days and it feels so refreshing. Ive struggled with food addiction all my life, so to for once not think about food as much as i used to..frees up so much time and energy for me. ❤❤❤

Last year I did 3 weeks successfully on keto and was 20 lb down but then I started to get dangerous drops in my blood sugar, that were waking me, shivering and dizzy up at 2 am.  forcing me to take pure sugar to recover.  I suffer from Type 2 diabetes which I control by diet.  It was disappointing to have to eat carbs again and I eventually gained back the 20 lb. How can I go back to Keto and lose the weight that I still need to lose without my blood sugar dropping to 2.7 or 3.  I thought that Diabetics do well on Keto and I desperately want to lose weight.

I don't know if it is "fat adaptation" but on my 66 day marathon water fasting at around day 30 I started to be able to do computer programming which is the indicator of proper energy level for me. It is possible that it is very stubborn thing. Also all kinds of faint symptoms went away at that 30 day mark. In my 13 year ketogenic diet I have learned to measure glucose and B-hydroxybutyrate directly from blood. That does not involve any paid lab so you should be able to do it too. You simply get enough info to understand when you are burning fat. But having good energy level is the different beast.

In my 13 year ketogenic diet I have learned to measure glucose and B-hydroxybutyrate directly from blood. That does not involve any paid lab so you should be able to do it too. You simply get enough info to understand when you are burning fat. But having good energy level is the different beast.

How can I go back to Keto and lose the weight that I still need to lose without my blood sugar dropping to 2.7 or 3.  I thought that Diabetics do well on Keto and I desperately want to lose weight.

I used to suffer terrible chronic gout… And even the first year of doing the keto diet I would get flares… However I can say with confidence that the good doctor is correct. Almost three years into  being fat adapted and I can say with confidence I cured my gout. If you suffered from gout like I did chronically… Then you know that statement is life changing statement.

I am not a Dr. nor am I a scientist but I know one thing for sure. I was 430 pounds in 2010 and decided to go low carb. I lost 200 pounds and felt great and consumed almost no veggies. In October of 2016, I wanted to lose a few more pounds and decided I would bring in more veggies, nuts, and 90% dark chocolate. I had never eaten spinach but thought I would try it and see if I liked it. I did, and so I started with cooked spinach and then went on to raw spinach. The longer I went the more spinach I ate every single day. I also ate almonds, 90% dark chocolate every single day. In September of 2018, I was blessed with kidney stones that ended up with me having surgery and my first experience with a catheter. So in 41 years of living, I had not been eating a high oxalate diet from veggies and nuts and had never had a kidney stone. In two years of bringing in "the big salads," I am down and out with kidney stones. The urologist looked at my diet (I keep detailed spreadsheets of everything I eat) and he said this is 100% due to diet. He told me to cut out all the high oxalate foods and stay under 50 oxalates a day. So can I say for sure that it is the fault of oxalates? Again, not being a Dr. nor a Scientist I can only go off of what I read and what happened to me. After dealing with kidney stones I will let the big-armed sailor deal with kidney stones, not me.

Doc, you and Saladino are going to have to square off and settle this, because he has plants as the source of most of the world's woes. I can barely enjoy my salads any more. I get the sense that the only diet I should every consider is cold, raw grass-fed beef liver on a plate. And keto had been working so well for me for 15 months now. 108 pounds lost, diabetes kicked into remission. Cheers.

Our son, who has gone mostly carnivore, has a tendency to have joint issues. He went on a "peanut" only eating...the other week. Which btw I warned was not really a good idea.  He had been doing a very dirty keto/carnivore before that and I could tell was consuming to many "diet pops" etc... He got joint pain immediately.  He then listened to his dear Mother, me...lol and starting to eat normal keto/carnivore again w/o snacks and cut out the diet pop and  consuming peanuts only.  His symptoms went away.  BTW my husband used to get gout all the time, when we ate the standard american diet. We cut out red meat for a whole year,,,,back then...and he still got it. (that was torture lol I love red meat)   I'm so glad we found keto/carnivore 4 years ago. He reversed his diabetes and is off 13 different meds that the VA prescribed him. I reversed my "pre diabetic" symptoms and gerd and inflammation.  We are 61 and 55!!! Never to late!!!!!!

Put my T2D in remission with no meds by following a clean ketovore woe. Took out most of the veg because they were bothering my stomach????? I’m sorry, yes, you are a doctor, and like every doctor before you, you discount the pain, cramps, bloating, inflammation, and constipation, among other problems... that  “correlates” to me having eaten a high oxalate food. All doctors, at least the ones I have gone to for help, just push pharmaceuticals. There is no medicine that has ever helped me. I would go almost a month sometimes before I would have a bowel movement. Doctors all said eat fiber, which caused my pain and inflammation to go through the roof. I may not be a doctor, but I know my own body and I know the experiences that I have had since limiting my oxalates to near zero. I’m not excessively overweight, nor am i trying to lose a lot of weight, but I lost 7 pounds the very first week. My stomach before this new lifestyle was grossly bloated and felt like a spare tire around my upper stomach. I’m 5’ 8”, was 132 lbs. I’m going to live life my way, on my terms. I find it interesting that you would put yourself on YouTube to say we know nothing and you know everything.

comment_text: I developed kidney stones that passed.  AWFUL. My doc told me to not worry about oxalates, although they were oxalate stones. He called them calcium.  Anyhow I listened, this was 13 years ago.  Two years later my other kidney was full of stones! Did clean KETO and ended up in ER twice. Once with acidosis.  Kidney stones back. My eyelids started forming cysts.  I was on a mild Keto and moderate oxalates. They tested the cysts that were so sharp, that they cut my eyeball.  Doctor said it could have blinded me if it was closer to the pupil.  He put them on the scope after removing them and they were oxalate.  He said there are basically glass chards in your eyelid.  The interstitial cystitis was so severe they sent me to pain management. I could barely sit down or urinate.  So for me I cannot do much for oxalates…at all. Since going below 45, the interstitial cystitis gone, my body aches that felt like flu, gone, nerve pain gone.  But if I cheat it ALL comes back. But I know we are all different.  For me it’s a big no no. But I still eat lots of safe veggies and clean meats.  But once in awhile I do eat too many and it’s hell for me.
aspect_names: Cognitive Function; Pain & Inflammation; Fibromyalgia & Neuropathy

I also injured my Neck similarly in Jiujitsu, Muay Thai about 12 years ago. The best relief I found is doing Dead hangs for 30 seconds to a minute x 3 sets 5 days a week.  I’m 44 now and pain free most the time.

Best of luck to you. I had a similar injury that drove me crazy for a couple of years. Through consistent mobility and strengthening improvements in the problem areas, i was able to get to a pain-free and healthy state!

I was on carnivore for a few years myself but I would still get gout from time to time and I was suffering other pains on nearly on my joints that reminded me exactly like a gout flare up. I went to a ketogenic diet in May and never has my mind been blown away by all the healing that has taken place. All my metabolic markers for heart disease are in remission.

Love to hear more on your ratio choices and fat options. I’ve got some disc inflammation and have been really ketovore for a few months now, down 30 lbs and  trying to settle in on the ideal ratios of pure carnivore to ketovore.
Sending prayers Dr. Baker for a speedy recovery and to become pain free with your new diet regimen. ❤

greetings from Brazil, I was suffering by sciatic nerve pain for a decade caused by herniated disc in L4 L5, S1. I found one alternative treatment called PST ( Pulsed Signal Therapy) that uses electromagnet frequency to estimulate an original cartilage between discs, this thing save my life, after 3 month no more pain, today past almost one year after my seven session of PST, I can say that 97% of my pain is completly gone. I hope this information help many others across the world...

Sorry to hear this Shawn. I have a herniated disc in my lumbar region and did not want surgery either. So, I had a steroid injection this past May and no more pain. I am not sure how long it will last, but if I can make it for at least a year I will be happy!

I had a nerve injury from jiu jitsu that radiated from my neck down my left arm for 3 months! I was getting a massage and using a chiropractor every week, very experience out of pocket for me too. I used a grounding patch, essentially a cardiac lead from an ekg, placed it on the trap area next to my neck. Isolated grounded it for one night and the pain went away the next day. The results were so obvious and abrupt, three months of pain went away immediately after one night of direct isolated electrical grounding. I ground every night religiously and highly recommended it!

C3/C4 injury years ago.   Did not have surgery because people I know who had the surgery were not any better.   I had numbness and pain, couldn’t sleep.   With keto all the symptoms went away.  Completely off pain meds.   It’s amazing.   Good luck!  Hope you feel better soon.

Dr Baker, thank you for your many videos. I am 68 and lost 50 pounds this year. I have kept it off for the past 6 months. The curse of the yo yo diet has been broken. I will start another 90 day carnivore eating session Jan 2026. The way your video comes across to me is why bother since I have been overweight the past 30 years - the damage is done. My weight goal is 190 pounds so I have 70 more pounds to go. This may not prevent a heart attack but I would think it would still be a win for me. Fact: I am going to die, just don't how or when. I put my hope and faith in the LORD! Come quickly LORD Jesus!

The carnivore diet doesn't negate cardiovascular risk you built before you started. My partner ate very poorly. He knew that he had partial blockages in his arteries among  other health issues.  He was 300lbs at 6'7". Over the course of 2 years he lost 100lbs and has kept it off. His metrics were pretty good but his LDL was still high. His triglycerides were quite low. His ApoB higher than it should be and his CTA showed a 50% blockage in the widowmaker. That wasn't caused by carnivore. He knows that.   He continues to do the carnivore diet but he also expands his envelope to include foods he would not have eaten before like lots of fish like sardines. Like natto for vitamin K2  Niacin and berberine and a few other targeted supplements. He's learning to breath properly so he gets nitric oxide. He's considering a short stint on PCSK-9 inhibitors to get his cholesterol to a better number and works on ways to lower high ApoB.   He can't take for granted what he did to himself before. He doesn't want a heart attack but knows there is a chance he will die of a heart attack. But he feels like he's living his life now with what carnivore had done for him so he does not want to quit but is realistic about it. We also don't follow the people who think that carnivore is the cure all for everything. It's not. But it is one of the best regimens for health with or without complications from previous poor habits.

I had two friends who lost over 100 lbs recently in their 50s and both died of heart issues after they lost all the weight.  It was sad, but unfortunately the damage had already been done.  Don't matter which diet you choose if you're body is already severely damaged. They were also on normal diets most of their lives, eating like complete shit.

I’m 58 and had a heart attack this past June, I had lost 50 lbs the prior year on carnivore. I was told to get a coronary artery scan when I started, but never got around to it, if I had I would’ve known about the clog that had building up for the last 30 years and I could’ve had the stent put in in office for about a tenth of my hospital stay cost and without heart damage. It’s worth the $200 to get one

Our family friend went on WW and lost over 100 pounds. He didn't do cardio exercise. He had found out he was so heavy his heart had moved in his chest. About 6 months after losing the weight, he had a massive heart attack. He couldn't be found in his home for around 50 minutes, from 911 call to getting him in ambulance and he had 7 strokes. Took another 5 months to get him well enough for major heart surgery. When he came home afterwards, he died 2 hours after he got home. It wasn't Carnivore, and he went through hell after losing 100 pounds. morbidly obese.

I have a pacemaker and have dropped 80 pounds  from 330 to 250. I was even down to 230 but felt better at 250 so I adjust what I eat to stay there. Afib runs in my family and is definitely inherited.  I do take blood thinners but that's it for medicine.  I feel great, very active, driving my wife nuts because I keep moving, cleaning, organizing, efficient beings no brain fog. Loving life at 67 even with Non Hodgkin's Lymphoma.

Just from my own experience, I am 56 with a family history of heart attacks and have been 90%+ carnivore for 3 years the first time and about six months on my restart (my former doctor made me second guess my decision even though I felt my best being very low carb). My LDL and HDL concerns my current doctor as they are in the danger zone even though I eat whole foods, do HIIT three times a week, and lost 20 pounds. I know it may be just me but according to the mainstream I should be on statins and a Mediterranean diet but I am thriving on just 90%+ carnivore! Thanks, Dr Baker, for putting out the truth!

I lost 111 pounds and gave myself nutritional neuropathy, had gallbladder removed, appendix removed also

I went on a carnivore diet back in 2017 after hearing u on the joe Rogan podcast lost 44 pounds and never felt better, my dad was so impressed he also went on the carnivore diet exactly the way I did same food choices etc. But he was very obese for at least 15 plus year he was 5’ 11” and weighed roughly 290 pounds give or take. He lost around 70 pounds and started walking more  etc. about a year and 1/2 later he had an heart event not a heart attack but complications shortness of breath fatigue etc with being Sid he also had sleep apnea used a Cpap machine for about 4 years my dad ended up getting quadruple bypass surgery and blamed me for his heart attack said I talked him into the carnivore diet which I did not I told him consult your doctor ask questions like I did my cardiologist was all for it the only exception was he wanted me to do more of a mediterranean style keto diet which I did not. Btw the cardiologist told my dad that the carnivore diet did not cause his heart issues those was caused by decades of poor eating excessive weight gain no exercise basically a couch potato for about 15 to 20 years that’s what blocked his arteries. As a matter of fact the cardiologist told him if he didn’t loose all that weight and started walking more that he would have most certainly died of a massive heart attack or stroke. So the carnivore diet saved his life! It bought him time 100% I’m sure of this. Unfortunately he passed away from COVID complications in late 2020 at 80 and 1/2 years old. Since 2021 I’ve slipped and slide on and off the carnivore diet but I’m going to jump back on and hammer it hard , I had a calcium scan test done 2 years ago calcium score of ZERO all across the board so I feel confident going forward again with a total carnivore diet although I do love sauerkraut and olives any thing fermented so maybe I’ll still hang onto that part of my regular eating this time. Take care Doctor Baker and thank you for the bottom of my heart for opening my eyes back in 2017 I’m a believer in carnivore way of eating . Merry Christmas and have a happy new year.

I went on a carnivore diet back in 2017 after hearing u on the joe Rogan podcast lost 44 pounds and never felt better, my dad was so impressed he also went on the carnivore diet exactly the way I did same food choices etc. But he was very obese for at least 15 plus year he was 5’ 11” and weighed roughly 290 pounds give or take. He lost around 70 pounds and started walking more  etc. about a year and 1/2 later he had an heart event not a heart attack but complications shortness of breath fatigue etc with being Sid he also had sleep apnea used a Cpap machine for about 4 years my dad ended up getting quadruple bypass surgery and blamed me for his heart attack said I talked him into the carnivore diet which I did not I told him consult your doctor ask questions like I did my cardiologist was all for it the only exception was he wanted me to do more of a mediterranean style keto diet which I did not. Btw the cardiologist told my dad that the carnivore diet did not cause his heart issues those was caused by decades of poor eating excessive weight gain no exercise basically a couch potato for about 15 to 20 years that’s what blocked his arteries. As a matter of fact the cardiologist told him if he didn’t loose all that weight and started walking more that he would have most certainly died of a massive heart attack or stroke. So the carnivore diet saved his life! It bought him time 100% I’m sure of this. Unfortunately he passed away from COVID complications in late 2020 at 80 and 1/2 years old. Since 2021 I’ve slipped and slide on and off the carnivore diet but I’m going to jump back on and hammer it hard , I had a calcium scan test done 2 years ago calcium score of ZERO all across the board so I feel confident going forward again with a total carnivore diet although I do love sauerkraut and olives any thing fermented so maybe I’ll still hang onto that part of my regular eating this time. Take care Doctor Baker and thank you for the bottom of my heart for opening my eyes back in 2017 I’m a believer in carnivore way of eating . Merry Christmas and have a happy new year.
